= self.tasks.write().await;\n170\t            registry.insert(miroir_id.clone(), task.clone());\n171\t        }\n172\t\n173\t        // Spawn a background task to poll for status updates using real node polling\n174\t        let registry = self.clone();\n175\t        let miroir_id_clone = miroir_id.clone();\n176\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n177\t            registry.poll_task_status_with_poller(&miroir_id_clone, poller).await;\n178\t        });\n179\t\n180\t        Ok(task)\n181\t    }\n182\t\n183\t    /// Get task by ID (async version).\n184\t    pub async fn get_async(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Option<MiroirTask> {\n185\t        let tasks = self.tasks.read().await;\n186\t        tasks.get(miroir_id).cloned()\n187\t    }\n188\t\n189\t    /// Delete a task from the registry.\n190\t    pub async fn delete(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n191\t        let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n192\t        Ok(tasks.remove(miroir_id).is_some())\n193\t    }\n194\t\n195\t    /// Count total tasks in the registry.\n196\t    pub async fn count(&self) -> usize {\n197\t        let tasks = self.tasks.read().await;\n198\t        tasks.len()\n199\t    }\n200\t\n201\t    /// Prune old tasks (in-memory only, for Phase 3 this will use durable storage).\n202\t    pub async fn prune_old_tasks(&self, _cutoff_ms: u64) -> Result<usize> {\n203\t        // In-memory implementation: no pruning in Phase 2\n204\t        // Phase 3 will add durable storage and pruning\n205\t        Ok(0)\n206\t    }\n207\t\n208\t    /// Update the overall task status based on node task statuses.\n209\t    pub async fn update_overall_status(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n210\t        let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n211\t        let task = match tasks.get(miroir_id) {\n212\t            Some(t) => t.clone(),\n213\t            None => return Ok(false),\n214\t        };\n215\t\n216\t        // Determine overall status from node tasks\n217\t        let mut all_succeeded = true;\n218\t        let mut any_failed = false;\n219\t        let mut all_terminal = true;\n220\t\n221\t        for (_node_id, node_task) in &task.node_tasks {\n222\t            match node_task.status {\n223\t                NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n224\t                    all_terminal = false;\n225\t                    all_succeeded = false;\n226\t                }\n227\t                NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded => {}\n228\t                NodeTaskStatus::Failed => {\n229\t                    any_failed = true;\n230\t                }\n231\t            }\n232\t        }\n233\t\n234\t        let new_status = if any_failed {\n235\t            TaskStatus::Failed\n236\t        } else if all_terminal && all_succeeded {\n237\t            TaskStatus::Succeeded\n238\t        } else if !all_terminal {\n239\t            TaskStatus::Processing\n240\t        } else {\n241\t            TaskStatus::Enqueued\n242\t        };\n243\t\n244\t        if new_status != task.status {\n245\t            if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n246\t                t.status = new_status;\n247\t            }\n248\t            Ok(true)\n249\t        } else {\n250\t            Ok(false)\n251\t        }\n252\t    }\n253\t\n254\t    /// Poll node tasks to update the overall Miroir task status.\n255\t    /// Uses exponential backoff: 25ms → 50 → 100 → ... → 1s cap.\n256\t    ///\n257\t    /// Phase 2: Simulates node polling (tasks complete after ~500ms)\n258\t    /// Phase 3: Will poll actual nodes via HttpClient using topology\n259\t    async fn poll_task_status_simulated(&self, miroir_id: &str) {\n260\t        let mut delay_ms = 25u64;\n261\t        let max_delay_ms = 1000u64;\n262\t\n263\t        loop {\n264\t            // Get the current task state\n265\t            let task = self.get_async(miroir_id).await;\n266\t\n267\t            let task = match task {\n268\t                Some(t) => t,\n269\t                None => return, // Task was deleted\n270\t            };\n271\t\n272\t            // Check if we've reached a terminal state\n273\t            if matches!(task.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n274\t                return;\n275\t            }\n276\t\n277\t            // In a real implementation, we would query the nodes here\n278\t            // For Phase 2, we simulate status progression\n279\t            // Phase 3 will add actual node polling via HttpClient\n280\t\n281\t            // Check each node task's status\n282\t            let mut all_terminal = true;\n283\t            for (_node_id, node_task) in &task.node_tasks {\n284\t                match node_task.status {\n285\t                    NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n286\t                        all_terminal = false;\n287\t                    }\n288\t                    NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded | NodeTaskStatus::Failed => {}\n289\t                }\n290\t            }\n291\t\n292\t            // For testing purposes, simulate tasks completing\n293\t            // In production, this would poll actual nodes\n294\t            if !all_terminal && delay_ms >= 500 {\n295\t                // Simulate completion for testing\n296\t                let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n297\t                if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n298\t                    for (_node_id, node_task) in &mut t.node_tasks {\n299\t                        if matches!(node_task.status, NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing) {\n300\t                            node_task.status = NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded;\n301\t                        }\n302\t                    }\n303\t                    // Update overall status\n304\t                    let mut all_succeeded = true;\n305\t                    let mut any_failed = false;\n306\t                    for (_node_id, node_task) in &t.node_tasks {\n307\t                        match node_task.status {\n308\t                            NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded => {}\n309\t                            NodeTaskStatus::Failed => any_failed = true,\n310\t                            NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n311\t                                all_succeeded = false;\n312\t                            }\n313\t                        }\n314\t                    }\n315\t                    if any_failed {\n316\t                        t.status = TaskStatus::Failed;\n317\t                    } else if all_succeeded {\n318\t                        t.status = TaskStatus::Succeeded;\n319\t                    } else {\n320\t                        t.status = TaskStatus::Processing;\n321\t                    }\n322\t                    // Set finished timestamp for terminal states\n323\t                    if matches!(t.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n324\t                        t.finished_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n325\t                            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n326\t                            .unwrap_or_default()\n327\t                            .as_millis() as u64);\n328\t                    }\n329\t                }\n330\t                return;\n331\t            }\n332\t\n333\t            // Exponential backoff with cap\n334\t            tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(delay_ms)).await;\n335\t            delay_ms = (delay_ms * 2).min(max_delay_ms);\n336\t        }\n337\t    }\n338\t\n339\t    /// Poll node tasks to update the overall Miroir task status, using real node polling.\n340\t    /// Uses exponential backoff: 25ms → 50 → 100 → ... → 1s cap.\n341\t    async fn poll_task_status_with_poller<P: NodePoller>(&self, miroir_id: &str, poller: Arc<P>) {\n342\t        let mut delay_ms = 25u64;\n343\t        let max_delay_ms = 1000u64;\n344\t\n345\t        loop {\n346\t            // Get the current task state\n347\t            let task = self.get_async(miroir_id).await;\n348\t\n349\t            let task = match task {\n350\t                Some(t) => t,\n351\t                None => return, // Task was deleted\n352\t            };\n353\t\n354\t            // Check if we've reached a terminal state\n355\t            if matches!(task.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n356\t                return;\n357\t            }\n358\t\n359\t            // Collect node IDs and task UIDs to poll\n360\t            let node_polls: Vec<(NodeId, u64)> = task.node_tasks\n361\t                .iter()\n362\t                .filter(|(_, nt)| !matches!(nt.status, NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded | NodeTaskStatus::Failed))\n363\t                .map(|(node_id, nt)| (NodeId::new(node_id.clone()), nt.task_uid))\n364\t                .collect();\n365\t\n366\t            if node_polls.is_empty() {\n367\t                // All node tasks are terminal, update overall status\n368\t                let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n369\t                if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n370\t                    let mut all_succeeded = true;\n371\t                    let mut any_failed = false;\n372\t                    for (_node_id, node_task) in &t.node_tasks {\n373\t                        match node_task.status {\n374\t                            NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded => {}\n375\t                            NodeTaskStatus::Failed => any_failed = true,\n376\t                            NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n377\t                                all_succeeded = false;\n378\t                            }\n379\t                        }\n380\t                    }\n381\t                    if any_failed {\n382\t                        t.status = TaskStatus::Failed;\n383\t                    } else if all_succeeded {\n384\t                        t.status = TaskStatus::Succeeded;\n385\t                    } else {\n386\t                        t.status = TaskStatus::Processing;\n387\t                    }\n388\t                    // Set finished timestamp for terminal states\n389\t                    if matches!(t.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n390\t                        t.finished_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n391\t                            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n392\t                            .unwrap_or_default()\n393\t                            .as_millis() as u64);\n394\t                    }\n395\t                }\n396\t                return;\n397\t            }\n398\t\n399\t            // Poll each node for status\n400\t            let mut node_statuses = HashMap::new();\n401\t            for (node_id, task_uid) in &node_polls {\n402\t                // Get node address from topology (would need topology here)\n403\t                // For now, use a mock address - in production, this would come from the topology\n404\t                let address = format!(\"http://{}\", node_id.as_str());\n405\t\n406\t                match poller.poll_node_task(&node_id, &address, *task_uid).await {\n407\t                    Ok(status) => {\n408\t                        node_statuses.insert(node_id.clone(), status);\n409\t                    }\n410\t                    Err(e) => {\n411\t                        tracing::warn!(\"Failed to poll node {} for task {}: {}\", node_id, task_uid, e);\n412\t                        // On poll failure, keep the current status but mark for potential degradation\n413\t                    }\n414\t                }\n415\t            }\n416\t\n417\t            // Update node task statuses\n418\t            {\n419\t                let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n420\t                if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n421\t                    for (node_id, status) in node_statuses {\n422\t                        if let Some(node_task) = t.node_tasks.get_mut(node_id.as_str()) {\n423\t                            node_task.status = status;\n424\t                        }\n425\t                    }\n426\t\n427\t                    // Update started_at timestamp if moving to processing\n428\t                    if t.status == TaskStatus::Enqueued {\n429\t                        let any_processing = t.node_tasks.values().any(|nt| {\n430\t                            matches!(nt.status, NodeTaskStatus::Processing)\n431\t                        });\n432\t                        if any_processing && t.started_at.is_none() {\n433\t                            t.started_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n434\t                                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n435\t                                .unwrap_or_default()\n436\t                                .as_millis() as u64);\n437\t                            t.status = TaskStatus::Processing;\n438\t                        }\n439\t                    }\n440\t                }\n441\t            }\n442\t\n443\t            // Exponential backoff with cap\n444\t            tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(delay_ms)).await;\n445\t            delay_ms = (delay_ms * 2).min(max_delay_ms);\n446\t        }\n447\t    }\n448\t\n449\t    /// List tasks with optional filtering (Meilisearch-compatible).\n450\t    pub async fn list_async(&self, filter: &TaskFilter) -> Result<Vec<MiroirTask>> {\n451\t        let guard = self.tasks.read().await;\n452\t        let mut result: Vec<MiroirTask> = guard.values().cloned().collect();\n453\t\n454\t        // Apply status filter\n455\t        if let Some(status) = filter.status {\n456\t            result.retain(|t| t.status == status);\n457\t        }\n458\t\n459\t        // Apply index_uid filter\n460\t        if let Some(index_uid) = &filter.index_uid {\n461\t            result.retain(|t| t.index_uid.as_ref().map_or(false, |uid| uid == index_uid));\n462\t        }\n463\t\n464\t        // Apply task_type filter\n465\t        if let Some(task_type) = &filter.task_type {\n466\t            result.retain(|t| t.task_type.as_ref().map_or(false, |ty| ty == task_type));\n467\t        }\n468\t\n469\t        // Apply offset\n470\t        if let Some(offset) = filter.offset {\n471\t            if offset < result.len() {\n472\t                result = result[offset..].to_vec();\n473\t            } else {\n474\t                result.clear();\n475\t            }\n476\t        }\n477\t\n478\t        // Apply limit\n479\t        if let Some(limit) = filter.limit {\n480\t            result.truncate(limit);\n481\t        }\n482\t\n483\t        Ok(result)\n484\t    }\n485\t}\n486\t\n487\timpl Default for InMemoryTaskRegistry {\n488\t    fn default() -> Self {\n489\t        Self::new()\n490\t    }\n491\t}\n492\t\n493\t/// Stub TaskRegistry implementation for compatibility.\n494\t/// This })\n523\t    }\n524\t\n525\t    fn update_status(&self, miroir_id: &str, status: TaskStatus) -> Result<()> {\n526\t        let registry = self.clone();\n527\t        let miroir_id = }\n540\t\n541\t    fn update_node_task(\n542\t        &self,\n543\t        miroir_id: &str,\n544\t        node_id: &str,\n545\t        node_status: NodeTaskStatus,\n546\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n547\t ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n591\t// TaskRegistryImpl — runtime-selected backend (Phase 3)\n592\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n593\t\n594\t/// Task status as lowercase string for store serialization.\n595\tfn status_to_str(s: TaskStatus) -> &'static str {\n596\t    match s {\n597\t        TaskStatus::Enqueued => \"enqueued\",\n598\t        TaskStatus::Processing => \"processing\",\n599\t        TaskStatus::Succeeded => \"succeeded\",\n600\t        TaskStatus::Failed => \"failed\",\n601\t        TaskStatus::Canceled => \"canceled\",\n602\t    }\n603\t}\n604\t\n605\t/// Parse task status from store string.\n606\tfn str_to_status(s: &str) -> TaskStatus {\n607\t    match s {\n608\t        \"succeeded\" => TaskStatus::Succeeded,\n609\t        \"failed\" => TaskStatus::Failed,\n610\t        \"processing\" => TaskStatus::Processing,\n611\t        \"canceled\" => TaskStatus::Canceled,\n612\t        _ => TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n613\t    }\n614\t}\n615\t\n616\t/// Convert a store `TaskRow` to a `MiroirTask`.\n617\t///\n618\t/// Per-node status is not persisted; all nodes are set to `Enqueued`.\n619\t/// The GET /tasks/{id} endpoint polls nodes to reconstruct per-node status.\n620\tfn row_to_task(row: crate::task_store::TaskRow) -> MiroirTask {\n621\t    let node_tasks: HashMap<String, NodeTask> = row\n622\t        .node_tasks\n623\t        .into_iter()\n624\t        .map(|(node_id, task_uid)| {\n625\t            (\n626\t                node_id,\n627\t                NodeTask {\n628\t                    task_uid,\n629\t                    status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n630\t                },\n631\t            )\n632\t        })\n633\t        .collect();\n634\t\n635\t    MiroirTask {\n636\t        miroir_id: row.miroir_id,\n637\t        created_at: row.created_at as u64,\n638\t        started_at: row.started_at.map(|t| t as u64),\n639\t        finished_at: row.finished_at.map(|t| t as u64),\n640\t        status: str_to_status(&row.status),\n641\t        index_uid: row.index_uid,\n642\t        task_type: row.task_type,\n643\t        node_tasks,\n644\t        error: row.error,\n645\t        node_errors: row.node_errors,\n646\t    }\n647\t}\n648\t\n649\t/// Convert a route `TaskFilter` to a store `TaskFilter`.\n650\tfn filter_to_store(f: &TaskFilter) -> crate::task_store::TaskFilter {\n651\t    crate::task_store::TaskFilter {\n652\t        status: f.status.map(|s| status_to_str(s).to_string()),\n653\t        index_uid: f.index_uid.clone(),\n654\t        task_type: f.task_type.clone(),\n655\t        limit: f.limit,\n656\t        offset: f.offset,\n657\t    }\n658\t}\n659\t\n660\t/// Runtime-selected task registry backend.\n661\t///\n662\t/// - `InMemory`: Phase 2 behaviour, tasks lost on restart (default for sqlite without path).\n663\t/// - `Sqlite`: persisted via `SqliteTaskStore`, survives restarts.\n664\t/// - `Redis`: persisted via `RedisTaskStore`, required for multi-replica HPA.\n665\t#[derive(Clone)]\n666\tpub enum TaskRegistryImpl {\n667\t    InMemory(InMemoryTaskRegistry),\n668\t    Sqlite(Arc<crate::task_store::SqliteTaskStore>),\n669\t    #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n670\t    Redis(Arc<crate::task_store::RedisTaskStore>),\n671\t}\n672\t\n673\timpl TaskRegistryImpl {\n674\t    /// Open a SQLite-backed registry at the given path.\n675\t    pub fn sqlite(path: &str) -> Result<Self> {\n676\t        let store = crate::task_store::SqliteTaskStore::open(std::path::Path::new(path))?;\n677\t        store.migrate()?;\n678\t        Ok(Self::Sqlite(Arc::new(store)))\n679\t    }\n680\t\n681\t    /// Open a Redis-backed registry at the given URL.\n682\t    #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n683\t    pub fn redis(url: &str) -> Result<Self> {\n684\t        let store = tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n685\t            tokio::runtime::Handle::current()\n686\t                .block_on(crate::task_store::RedisTaskStore::open(url))\n687\t        })?;\n688\t        store.migrate()?;\n689\t        Ok(Self::Redis(Arc::new(store)))\n690\t    }\n691\t\n692\t    /// In-memory fallback (no persistence).\n693\t    pub fn in_memory() -> Self {\n694\t        Self::InMemory(InMemoryTaskRegistry::new())\n695\t    }\n696\t\n697\t    /// Return a reference to the underlying `SqliteTaskStore`, if this is the Sqlite variant.\n698\t    pub fn as_sqlite(&self) -> }\n704\t\n705\t    /// Return a reference to the underlying `RedisTaskStore`, if this is the Redis variant.\n706\t    #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n707\t    pub fn as_redis(&self) -> }\n713\t\n714\t    /// Register a new task with metadata (sync, compatible with `TaskRegistry` trait).\n715\t    pub fn register_with_metadata(\n716\t        &self,\n717\t        node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64>,\n718\t        index_uid: Option<String>,\n719\t        task_type: Option<String>,\n720\t    ) -> Result<MiroirTask> {\n721\t        match self {\n722\t            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => {\n723\t                crate::task::TaskRegistry::register_with_metadata(\n724\t                    r, node_tasks, index_uid, task_type,\n725\t                )\n726\t            }\n727\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n728\t                let miroir_id = format!(\"mtask-{}\", Uuid::new_v4());\n729\t                let created_at = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n730\t                    .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n731\t                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"clock error: {}\", e)))?\n732\t                    .as_millis() as i64;\n733\t\n734\t                let new_task = crate::task_store::NewTask {\n735\t                    miroir_id: miroir_id.clone(),\n736\t                    created_at,\n737\t                    status: \"enqueued\".to_string(),\n738\t                    node_tasks,\n739\t                    error: None,\n740\t                    started_at: None,\n741\t                    finished_at: None,\n742\t                    index_uid,\n743\t                    task_type,\n744\t                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n745\t                };\n746\t\n747\t                store.insert_task(&new_task)?;\n748\t\n749\t                Ok(MiroirTask {\n750\t                    miroir_id: new_task.miroir_id,\n751\t                    created_at: new_task.created_at as u64,\n752\t                    started_at: None,\n753\t                    finished_at: None,\n754\t                    status: TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n755\t                    index_uid: new_task.index_uid,\n756\t                    task_type: new_task.task_type,\n757\t                    node_tasks: new_task\n758\t                        .node_tasks\n759\t                        .into_iter()\n760\t                        .map(|(nid, uid)| (nid, NodeTask { task_uid: uid, status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued }))\n761\t                        .collect(),\n762\t                    error: None,\n763\t                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n764\t                })\n765\t            }\n766\t            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n767\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n768\t                let miroir_id = format!(\"mtask-{}\", Uuid::new_v4());\n769\t                let created_at = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n770\t                    .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n771\t                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"clock error: {}\", e)))?\n772\t                    .as_millis() as i64;\n773\t\n774\t                let new_task = crate::task_store::NewTask {\n775\t                    miroir_id: miroir_id.clone(),\n776\t                    created_at,\n777\t                    status: \"enqueued\".to_string(),\n778\t                    node_tasks,\n779\t                    error: None,\n780\t                    started_at: None,\n781\t                    finished_at: None,\n782\t                    index_uid,\n783\t                    task_type,\n784\t                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n785\t                };\n786\t\n787\t                store.insert_task(&new_task)?;\n788\t\n789\t                Ok(MiroirTask {\n790\t                    miroir_id: new_task.miroir_id,\n791\t                    created_at: new_task.created_at as u64,\n792\t                    started_at: None,\n793\t                    finished_at: None,\n794\t                    status: TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n795\t                    index_uid: new_task.index_uid,\n796\t                    task_type: new_task.task_type,\n797\t                    node_tasks: new_task\n798\t                        .node_tasks\n799\t                        .into_iter()\n800\t                        .map(|(nid, uid)| (nid, NodeTask { task_uid: uid, status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued }))\n801\t                        .collect(),\n802\t                    error: None,\n803\t                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n804\t                })\n805\t            }\n806\t        }\n807\t    }\n808\t\n809\t    /// Get a task by Miroir ID.\n810\t    pub fn get(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Result<Option<MiroirTask>> {\n811\t        match self {\n812\t            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => crate::task::TaskRegistry::get(r, miroir_id),\n813\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n814\t                Ok(store.get_task(miroir_id)?.map(row_to_task))\n815\t            }\n816\t            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n817\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n818\t                Ok(store.get_task(miroir_id)?.map(row_to_task))\n819\t            }\n820\t        }\n821\t    }\n822\t\n823\t    /// Update the overall task status.\n824\t    pub fn update_status(&self, miroir_id: &str, status: TaskStatus) -> Result<()> {\n825\t        match self {\n826\t            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => {\n827\t                crate::task::TaskRegistry::update_status(r, miroir_id, status)\n828\t            }\n829\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n830\t                store.update_task_status(miroir_id, status_to_str(status))?;\n831\t                Ok(())\n832\t            }\n833\t            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n834\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n835\t                store.update_task_status(miroir_id, status_to_str(status))?;\n836\t                Ok(())\n837\t            }\n838\t        }\n839\t    }\n840\t\n841\t    /// Update a node task's status.\n842\t    ///\n843\t    /// For persistent backends this is a no-op for per-node status since it is\n844\t    /// reconstructed from polling on each GET /tasks/{id}. The store only persists\n845\t    /// the overall task status and node task UIDs.\n846\t    pub fn update_node_task(\n847\t        &self,\n848\t        miroir_id: &str,\n849\t        node_id: &str,\n850\t        node_status: NodeTaskStatus,\n851\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n852\t        match self {\n853\t            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => {\n854\t                crate::task::TaskRegistry::update_node_task(r, miroir_id, node_id, node_status)\n855\t            }\n856\t            // Per-node status is reconstructed from polling on each GET; not persisted.\n857\t            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n858\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(_) => Ok(()),\n859\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(_) => Ok(()),\n860\t        }\n861\t    }\n862\t\n863\t    /// List tasks with optional filtering.\n864\t    pub fn list(&self, filter: TaskFilter) -> Result<Vec<MiroirTask>> {\n865\t        match self {\n866\t            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => crate::task::TaskRegistry::list(r, filter),\n867\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n868\t                let store_filter = #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n873\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n874\t                let store_filter = filter_to_store(&filter);\n875\t                let rows = store.list_tasks(&store_filter)?;\n876\t                Ok(rows.into_iter().map(row_to_task).collect())\n877\t            }\n878\t        }\n879\t    }\n880\t\n881\t    /// Count total tasks.\n882\t {\n886\t                store.task_count().unwrap_or(0) as usize\n887\t            }\n888\t            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n889\t            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n890\t                store.task_count().unwrap_or(0) as usize\n891\t            }\n892\t        }\n893\t    }\n894\t\n895\t    /// Set error on a task.\n896\t    pub fn set_error(&self, miroir_id: &str, error: }).unwrap();\n1182\t\n1183\t        // Wait for task to complete (simulated exponential backoff: 25 + 50 + 100 + 200 + 400 = 775ms)\n1184\t        rt.block_on(async {\n1185\t Arc<Topology>,\n}\n\nimpl<C: NodeClient> ClientNodePoller<C> {\n    /// Create a new node poller with the given client and topology.\n    pub fn new(client: Arc<C>, topology: Arc<Topology>) -> Self {\n        Self { client, topology }\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\nimpl<C: NodeClient> NodePoller for ClientNodePoller<C> {\n    async fn poll_node_task(\n        &self,\n        node_id: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        task_uid: u64,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<NodeTaskStatus, String> {\n        use crate::scatter::TaskStatusRequest;\n\n        let req = TaskStatusRequest    }\n    }\n\n    /// Register a new task with the given node tasks.\n    pub async fn register_async(&self, node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64>) -> Result<MiroirTask> {\n &self,\n        node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64>,\n        index_uid: Option<String>,\n        task_type: Option<String>,\n    ) -> Result<MiroirTask> {\n        let miroir_id = format!(\"mtask-{}\", task_uid,\n                status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n            });\n        }\n\n        let task = MiroirTask {\n            miroir_id: miroir_id.clone(),\n            created_at,\n Register a new task with the given node tasks and metadata, with real node polling.\n    ///\n    /// This version takes a NodePoller implementation to actually poll nodes for status updates.\n    pub Ok(0)\n    }\n\n    /// Update the overall task status based on node task statuses.\n    pub async fn update_overall_status(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n        let task = match tasks.get(miroir_id) {\n            Some(t) => t.clone(),\n            None => return Ok(false),\n        };\n\n        // Determine overall status from node tasks\n        let mut all_succeeded = true;\n        let mut any_failed = false;\n        let mut all_terminal = true;\n\n        for (_node_id, node_task) in &task.node_tasks {\n            match node_task.status {\n                NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n                    all_terminal = false;\n                    all_succeeded = false;\n                }\n                NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded => {}\n                NodeTaskStatus::Failed => {\n                    any_failed = true;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        let new_status = if any_failed {\n            TaskStatus::Failed\n        } else if all_terminal && all_succeeded {\n            TaskStatus::Succeeded\n        } else if !all_terminal {\n            TaskStatus::Processing\n        } else {\n            TaskStatus::Enqueued\n        };\n\n        if new_status != task.status {\n            if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n                t.status = new_status;\n            }\n            Ok(true)\n        } else {\n            Ok(false)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Poll node tasks to update the overall Miroir task status.\n    /// Uses exponential backoff: 25ms → 50 → 100 → ... → 1s cap.\n    ///\n    /// Phase 2: Simulates node polling (tasks complete after ~500ms)\n    /// Phase 3: Will poll actual nodes via HttpClient using topology\n    async fn poll_task_status_simulated(&self, miroir_id: &str) {\n        let mut delay_ms = 25u64;\n        let max_delay_ms = 1000u64;\n\n        loop {\n            // Get the current task state\n            let task = self.get_async(miroir_id).await;\n\n            let task = match task {\n                Some(t) => t,\n                None => return, // Task was deleted\n            };\n\n            // Check if we've reached a terminal state\n            if matches!(task.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n                return;\n            }\n\n            // In a real implementation, we would query the nodes here\n            // For Phase 2, we simulate status progression\n            // Phase 3 will add actual node polling via HttpClient\n\n            // Check each node task's status\n            let mut all_terminal = true;\n            for (_node_id, node_task) in &task.node_tasks {\n                match node_task.status {\n                    NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n                        all_terminal = false;\n                    }\n                    NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded | NodeTaskStatus::Failed => {}\n                }\n            }\n\n            // For testing purposes, simulate tasks completing\n            // In production, this would poll actual nodes\n            if !all_terminal && delay_ms >= 500 {\n                // Simulate completion for testing\n                let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n                if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n                    for (_node_id, node_task) in &mut t.node_tasks {\n                        if matches!(node_task.status, NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing) {\n                            node_task.status = NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded;\n                        }\n                    }\n                    // Update overall status\n                    let mut all_succeeded = true;\n                    let mut any_failed = false;\n                    for (_node_id, node_task) in &t.node_tasks {\n                        match node_task.status {\n                            NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded => {}\n                            NodeTaskStatus::Failed => any_failed = true,\n                            NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n                                all_succeeded = false;\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                    if any_failed {\n                        t.status = TaskStatus::Failed;\n                    } else if all_succeeded {\n                        t.status = TaskStatus::Succeeded;\n                    } else {\n                        t.status = TaskStatus::Processing;\n                    }\n                    // Set finished timestamp for terminal states\n                    if matches!(t.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n                        t.finished_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                            .unwrap_or_default()\n                            .as_millis() as u64);\n                    }\n                }\n                return;\n            }\n\n            // Exponential backoff with cap\n            tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(delay_ms)).await;\n            delay_ms = (delay_ms * 2).min(max_delay_ms);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Poll node tasks to update the overall Miroir task status, using real node polling.\n    /// Uses exponential backoff: 25ms → 50 → 100 → ... → 1s cap.\n    async fn poll_task_status_with_poller<P: NodePoller>(&self, miroir_id: &str, poller: Arc<P>) {\n        let mut delay_ms = 25u64;\n        let max_delay_ms = 1000u64;\n\n        loop {\n            // Get the current task state\n            let task = self.get_async(miroir_id).await;\n\n            let task = match task {\n                Some(t) => t,\n                None => return, // Task was deleted\n            };\n\n            // Check if we've reached a terminal state\n            if matches!(task.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n                return;\n            }\n\n            // Collect node IDs and task UIDs to poll\n            let node_polls: Vec<(NodeId, u64)> = task.node_tasks\n                .iter()\n                .filter(|(_, nt)| !matches!(nt.status, NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded | NodeTaskStatus::Failed))\n                .map(|(node_id, nt)| (NodeId::new(node_id.clone()), nt.task_uid))\n                .collect();\n\n            if node_polls.is_empty() {\n                // All node tasks are terminal, update overall status\n                let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n                if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n                    let mut all_succeeded = true;\n                    let mut any_failed = false;\n                    for (_node_id, node_task) in &t.node_tasks {\n                        match node_task.status {\n                            NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded => {}\n                            NodeTaskStatus::Failed => any_failed = true,\n                            NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued | NodeTaskStatus::Processing => {\n                                all_succeeded = false;\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                    if any_failed {\n                        t.status = TaskStatus::Failed;\n                    } else if all_succeeded {\n                        t.status = TaskStatus::Succeeded;\n                    } else {\n                        t.status = TaskStatus::Processing;\n                    }\n                    // Set finished timestamp for terminal states\n                    if matches!(t.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded | TaskStatus::Failed | TaskStatus::Canceled) {\n                        t.finished_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                            .unwrap_or_default()\n                            .as_millis() as u64);\n                    }\n                }\n                return;\n            }\n\n            // Poll each node for status\n            let mut node_statuses = HashMap::new();\n            for (node_id, task_uid) in &node_polls {\n                // Get node address from topology (would need topology here)\n                // For now, use a mock address - in production, this would come from the topology\n                let address = format!(\"http://{}\", node_id.as_str());\n\n                match poller.poll_node_task(&node_id, &address, *task_uid).await {\n                    Ok(status) => {\n                        node_statuses.insert(node_id.clone(), status);\n                    }\n                    Err(e) => {\n                        tracing::warn!(\"Failed to poll node {} for task {}: {}\", node_id, task_uid, e);\n                        // On poll failure, keep the current status but mark for potential degradation\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Update node task statuses\n            {\n                let mut tasks = self.tasks.write().await;\n                if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(miroir_id) {\n                    for (node_id, status) in node_statuses {\n                        if let Some(node_task) = t.node_tasks.get_mut(node_id.as_str()) {\n                            node_task.status = status;\n                        }\n                    }\n\n                    // Update started_at timestamp if moving to processing\n                    if t.status == TaskStatus::Enqueued {\n                        let any_processing = t.node_tasks.values().any(|nt| {\n                            matches!(nt.status, NodeTaskStatus::Processing)\n                        });\n                        if any_processing && t.started_at.is_none() {\n                            t.started_at = Some(std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                                .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                                .unwrap_or_default()\n                                .as_millis() as u64);\n                            t.status = TaskStatus::Processing;\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Exponential backoff with cap\n            tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(delay_ms)).await;\n            delay_ms = (delay_ms * 2).min(max_delay_ms);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// List tasks with optional filtering (Meilisearch-compatible).\n    pub async fn list_async(&self, filter: &TaskFilter) -> Result<Vec<MiroirTask>> {\n        let guard = self.tasks.read().await;\n        let mut result: Vec<MiroirTask> = guard.values().cloned().collect();\n\n        // Apply status filter\n        if let Some(status) = filter.status {\n            result.retain(|t| t.status == status);\n        }\n\n        // Apply index_uid filter\n        if let Some(index_uid) = &filter.index_uid {\n            result.retain(|t| t.index_uid.as_ref().map_or(false, |uid| uid == index_uid));\n        }\n\n        // Apply task_type filter\n        if let Some(task_type) = &filter.task_type {\n            result.retain(|t| t.task_type.as_ref().map_or(false, |ty| ty == task_type));\n        }\n\n        // Apply offset\n        if let Some(offset) = filter.offset {\n            if offset < result.len() {\n                result = result[offset..].to_vec();\n            } else {\n                result.clear();\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Apply limit\n        if let Some(limit) = filter.limit {\n            result.truncate(limit);\n        }\n\n        Ok(result)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for InMemoryTaskRegistry {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Stub TaskRegistry implementation for compatibility.\n/// This delegates to the async methods via tokio::task::block_in_place.\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\nimpl crate::task::TaskRegistry       }\n                Ok(())\n            })\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn list(&self, filter: TaskFilter) -> Result<Vec<MiroirTask>> {\n        let registry = self.clone();\n        tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n            let rt = tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current()\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"runtime error: {}\", e)))?;\n            rt.block_on(async move {\n                registry.list_async(&filter).await\n            })\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn count(&self) -> usize {\n        let registry = self.clone();\n        tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n            let rt = match tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current() {\n                Ok(rt) => rt,\n                Err(_) => return 0,\n            };\n            rt.block_on(async move {\n                registry.count().await\n            })\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// TaskRegistryImpl — runtime-selected backend (Phase 3)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Task status as lowercase string for store serialization.\nfn status_to_str(s: TaskStatus) -> &'static str {\n    match s {\n        TaskStatus::Enqueued => \"enqueued\",\n        TaskStatus::Processing => \"processing\",\n        TaskStatus::Succeeded => \"succeeded\",\n        TaskStatus::Failed => \"failed\",\n        TaskStatus::Canceled => \"canceled\",\n    }\n}\n\n/// Parse task status from store string.\nfn str_to_status(s: &str) -> TaskStatus {\n    match s {\n        \"succeeded\" => TaskStatus::Succeeded,\n        \"failed\" => TaskStatus::Failed,\n        \"processing\" => TaskStatus::Processing,\n        \"canceled\" => TaskStatus::Canceled,\n        _ => TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Convert a store `TaskRow` to a `MiroirTask`.\n///\n/// Per-node status is not persisted; all nodes are set to `Enqueued`.\n/// The GET /tasks/{id} endpoint polls nodes to reconstruct per-node status.\nfn row_to_task(row: crate::task_store::TaskRow) -> MiroirTask {\n    let node_tasks: HashMap<String, NodeTask> = row\n        .node_tasks\n        .into_iter()\n        .map(|(node_id, task_uid)| {\n            (\n                node_id,\n                NodeTask {\n                    task_uid,\n                    status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n                },\n            )\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    MiroirTask {\n        miroir_id: row.miroir_id,\n        created_at: row.created_at as u64,\n        started_at: row.started_at.map(|t| t as u64),\n        finished_at: row.finished_at.map(|t| t as u64),\n        status: str_to_status(&row.status),\n        index_uid: row.index_uid,\n        task_type: row.task_type,\n        node_tasks,\n        error: row.error,\n        node_errors: row.node_errors,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Convert a route `TaskFilter` to a store `TaskFilter`.\nfn filter_to_store(f: &TaskFilter) -> crate::task_store::TaskFilter {\n    crate::task_store::TaskFilter {\n        status: f.status.map(|s| status_to_str(s).to_string()),\n        index_uid: f.index_uid.clone(),\n        task_type: f.task_type.clone(),\n        limit: f.limit,\n        offset: f.offset,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Runtime-selected task registry backend.\n///\n/// - `InMemory`: Phase 2 behaviour, tasks lost on restart (default for sqlite without path).\n/// - `Sqlite`: persisted via `SqliteTaskStore`, survives restarts.\n/// - `Redis`: persisted via `RedisTaskStore`, required for multi-replica HPA.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub enum TaskRegistryImpl {\n    InMemory(InMemoryTaskRegistry),\n    Sqlite(Arc<crate::task_store::SqliteTaskStore>),\n    #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n    Redis(Arc<crate::task_store::RedisTaskStore>),\n}\n\nimpl TaskRegistryImpl {\n    /// Open a SQLite-backed registry at the given path.\n    pub fn sqlite(path: &str) -> Result<Self> {\n        let store = crate::task_store::SqliteTaskStore::open(std::path::Path::new(path))?;\n        store.migrate()?;\n        Ok(Self::Sqlite(Arc::new(store)))\n    }\n\n    /// Open a Redis-backed registry at the given URL.\n    #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n    pub fn redis(url: &str) -> Result<Self> {\n        let store = tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {\n            tokio::runtime::Handle::current()\n                .block_on(crate::task_store::RedisTaskStore::open(url))\n        })?;\n        store.migrate()?;\n        Ok(Self::Redis(Arc::new(store)))\n    }\n\n    /// In-memory fallback (no persistence).\n    pub fn in_memory() -> Self {\n        Self::InMemory(InMemoryTaskRegistry::new())\n    }\n\n    /// Return a reference to the underlying `SqliteTaskStore`, if this is the Sqlite variant.\n    pub fn as_sqlite(&self) -> Option<&Arc<crate::task_store::SqliteTaskStore>> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(s) => Some(s),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Return a reference to the underlying `RedisTaskStore`, if this is the Redis variant.\n    #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n    pub fn as_redis(&self) -> Option<&Arc<crate::task_store::RedisTaskStore>> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(r) => Some(r),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Register a new task with metadata (sync, compatible with `TaskRegistry` trait).\n    pub fn register_with_metadata(\n        &self,\n        node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64>,\n        index_uid: Option<String>,\n        task_type: Option<String>,\n    ) -> Result<MiroirTask> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => {\n                crate::task::TaskRegistry::register_with_metadata(\n                    r, node_tasks, index_uid, task_type,\n                )\n            }\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n                let miroir_id = format!(\"mtask-{}\", Uuid::new_v4());\n                let created_at = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                    .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"clock error: {}\", e)))?\n                    .as_millis() as i64;\n\n                let new_task = crate::task_store::NewTask {\n                    miroir_id: miroir_id.clone(),\n                    created_at,\n                    status: \"enqueued\".to_string(),\n                    node_tasks,\n                    error: None,\n                    started_at: None,\n                    finished_at: None,\n                    index_uid,\n                    task_type,\n                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n                };\n\n                store.insert_task(&new_task)?;\n\n                Ok(MiroirTask {\n                    miroir_id: new_task.miroir_id,\n                    created_at: new_task.created_at as u64,\n                    started_at: None,\n                    finished_at: None,\n                    status: TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n                    index_uid: new_task.index_uid,\n                    task_type: new_task.task_type,\n                    node_tasks: new_task\n                        .node_tasks\n                        .into_iter()\n                        .map(|(nid, uid)| (nid, NodeTask { task_uid: uid, status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued }))\n                        .collect(),\n                    error: None,\n                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n                })\n            }\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n                let miroir_id = format!(\"mtask-{}\", Uuid::new_v4());\n                let created_at = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n                    .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n                    .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Task(format!(\"clock error: {}\", e)))?\n                    .as_millis() as i64;\n\n                let new_task = crate::task_store::NewTask {\n                    miroir_id: miroir_id.clone(),\n                    created_at,\n                    status: \"enqueued\".to_string(),\n                    node_tasks,\n                    error: None,\n                    started_at: None,\n                    finished_at: None,\n                    index_uid,\n                    task_type,\n                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n                };\n\n                store.insert_task(&new_task)?;\n\n                Ok(MiroirTask {\n                    miroir_id: new_task.miroir_id,\n                    created_at: new_task.created_at as u64,\n                    started_at: None,\n                    finished_at: None,\n                    status: TaskStatus::Enqueued,\n                    index_uid: new_task.index_uid,\n                    task_type: new_task.task_type,\n                    node_tasks: new_task\n                        .node_tasks\n                        .into_iter()\n                        .map(|(nid, uid)| (nid, NodeTask { task_uid: uid, status: NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued }))\n                        .collect(),\n                    error: None,\n                    node_errors: HashMap::new(),\n                })\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get a task by Miroir ID.\n    pub fn get(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Result<Option<MiroirTask>> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => crate::task::TaskRegistry::get(r, miroir_id),\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n                Ok(store.get_task(miroir_id)?.map(row_to_task))\n            }\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n                Ok(store.get_task(miroir_id)?.map(row_to_task))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Update the overall task status.\n    pub fn update_status(&self, miroir_id: &str, status: TaskStatus) -> Result<()> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => {\n                crate::task::TaskRegistry::update_status(r, miroir_id, status)\n            }\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n                store.update_task_status(miroir_id, status_to_str(status))?;\n                Ok(())\n            }\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n                store.update_task_status(miroir_id, status_to_str(status))?;\n                Ok(())\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Update a node task's status.\n    ///\n    /// For persistent backends this is a no-op for per-node status since it is\n    /// reconstructed from polling on each GET /tasks/{id}. The store only persists\n    /// the overall task status and node task UIDs.\n    pub fn update_node_task(\n        &self,\n        miroir_id: &str,\n        node_id: &str,\n        node_status: NodeTaskStatus,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => {\n                crate::task::TaskRegistry::update_node_task(r, miroir_id, node_id, node_status)\n            }\n            // Per-node status is reconstructed from polling on each GET; not persisted.\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(_) => Ok(()),\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(_) => Ok(()),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// List tasks with optional filtering.\n    pub fn list(&self, filter: TaskFilter) -> Result<Vec<MiroirTask>> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => crate::task::TaskRegistry::list(r, filter),\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n                let store_filter = filter_to_store(&filter);\n                let rows = store.list_tasks(&store_filter)?;\n                Ok(rows.into_iter().map(row_to_task).collect())\n            }\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n                let store_filter = filter_to_store(&filter);\n                let rows = store.list_tasks(&store_filter)?;\n                Ok(rows.into_iter().map(row_to_task).collect())\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Count total tasks.\n    pub fn count(&self) -> usize {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(r) => crate::task::TaskRegistry::count(r),\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n                store.task_count().unwrap_or(0) as usize\n            }\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n                store.task_count().unwrap_or(0) as usize\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set error on a task.\n    pub fn set_error(&self, miroir_id: &str, error: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        match self {\n            TaskRegistryImpl::InMemory(_) => Ok(()),\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Sqlite(store) => {\n                store.set_task_error(miroir_id, error)?;\n                Ok(())\n            }\n            #[cfg(feature = \"redis-store\")]\n            TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(store) => {\n                store.set_task_error(miroir_id, error)?;\n                Ok(())\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\nimpl crate::task::TaskRegistry for TaskRegistryImpl {\n    fn register_with_metadata(\n        &self,\n        node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64>,\n        index_uid: Option<String>,\n        task_type: Option<String>,\n    ) -> Result<MiroirTask> {\n        TaskRegistryImpl::register_with_metadata(self, node_tasks, index_uid, task_type)\n    }\n\n    fn get(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> TaskRegistryImpl::list(self, filter)\n    }\n\n    fn count(&self) -> usize {\n        TaskRegistryImpl::count(self)\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    #[test]\n    fn test_in_memory_register_creates_task() {\n        let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();\n        let registry = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n        let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 1);\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), 2);\n\n        let task = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.register_async(node_tasks).await\n        }).unwrap();\n        assert!(task.miroir_id.starts_with(\"mtask-\"));\n        assert_eq!(task.status, TaskStatus::Enqueued);\n        assert_eq!(task.node_tasks.len(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_in_memory_get_returns_task() {\n        let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();\n        let registry = registry.register_async(node_tasks).await\n        }).unwrap();\n        let retrieved = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.get_async(&task.miroir_id).await\n        });\n        assert!(retrieved.is_some());\n        assert_eq!(retrieved.unwrap().miroir_id, task.miroir_id);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_in_memory_list_filters_by_status() {\n        let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();\n        let registry = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n\n        let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 1);\n\n        let (task1, _task2) = rt.block_on(async {\n            let t1 = registry.register_async(node_tasks.clone()).await.unwrap();\n            let t2 = registry.register_async(node_tasks).await.unwrap();\n            (t1, t2)\n        });\n\n        // Update task1 to succeeded - must be done within runtime context\n        let task1_id = task1.miroir_id.clone();\n        rt.block_on(async {\n            let mut tasks = registry.tasks.write().await;\n            if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(&task1_id) {\n                t.status = TaskStatus::Succeeded;\n            }\n        });\n\n        let filter = TaskFilter {\n            status: Some(TaskStatus::Succeeded),\n            node_id: None,\n            index_uid: None,\n            task_type: None,\n            limit: None,\n            offset: None,\n        };\n\n        let tasks = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.list_async(&filter).await\n        }).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(tasks.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(tasks[0].miroir_id, task1.miroir_id);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_in_memory_update_node_task() {\n        let rt =    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_update_overall_status() {\n        let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();\n        let registry = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n        let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 1);\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), 2);\n\n        let task = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.register_async(node_tasks).await\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        // Mark one node as succeeded, one as processing - must be done within runtime context\n        let task_id = task.miroir_id.clone();\n        rt.block_on(async {\n            let mut tasks = registry.tasks.write().await;\n            if let Some(t) = tasks.get_mut(&task_id) {\n                if let Some(nt) = t.node_tasks.get_mut(\"node-0\") {\n                    nt.status = NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded;\n                }\n                if let Some(nt) = t.node_tasks.get_mut(\"node-1\") {\n                    nt.status = NodeTaskStatus::Processing;\n                }\n            }\n        });\n\n        // Overall status should still be enqueued/processing\n        let updated = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.update_overall_status(&task.miroir_id).await\n        }).unwrap();\n        assert!(updated);\n\n        let retrieved = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.get_async(&task.miroir_id).await\n        }).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(retrieved.status, TaskStatus::Processing);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_in_memory_list_filters_by_index_uid() = InMemoryTaskRegistry::new();\n        let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-0\".to_string(), 1);\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), 2);\n complete (simulated exponential backoff: 25 + 50 + 100 + 200 + 400 = 775ms)\n        rt.block_on(async {\n            tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(800)).await;\n        });\n\n        let retrieved = rt.block_on(async {\n            registry.get_async(&task.miroir_id).await\n        }).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(retrieved.status, TaskStatus::Succeeded);\n Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n24\tpub struct Alias {\n25\t    /// Alias name (the key clients use).\n26\t    pub name: String,\n27\t    /// `single` or `multi`.\n28\t    pub kind: AliasKind,\n29\t match self.kind {\n79\t            AliasKind::Single => {\n80\t                let uid = self.current_uid.as_ref()\n81\t                    .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::InvalidState(\"single alias missing current_uid\".into()))?;\n82\t                Ok(vec![uid.clone()])\n83\t            }\n84\t            AliasKind::Multi => {\n85\t                let uids = self.target_uids.as_ref()\n86\t                    .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::InvalidState(\"multi alias missing target_uids\".into()))?;\n87\t                Ok(uids.clone())\n88\t            }\n89\t        }\n90\t    }\n91\t\n92\t    /// Flip this alias to a new target (single-target only).\n93\t    pub fn flip(&mut self, new_target: String) -> Result<()> {\n94\t        if self.kind != AliasKind::Single {\n95\t            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\"cannot flip multi-target alias\".into()));\n96\t        }\n97\t        self.current_uid = Some(new_target);\n98\t        self.generation += 1;\n99\t        self.updated_at = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n100\t            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n101\t            .unwrap_or_default()\n102\t            .as_secs();\n103\t        Ok(())\n104\t {\n109\t            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\"cannot update_targets on single-target alias\".into()));\n110\t        }\n111\t        self.target_uids = Some(new_targets);\n112\t .as_secs();\n117\t        Ok(())\n118\t    }\n119\t}\n120\t\n121\t/// In-memory alias registry with task-store persistence.\n122\t#[derive(Clone)]\n123\tpub struct AliasRegistry {\n124\t    aliases: }\n145\t\n146\t    /// Check if an input is an alias (vs a concrete UID).\n147\t    pub async fn is_alias(&self, input: &str) -> bool {\n148\t self.aliases.read().await.get(name).cloned()\n154\t    }\n155\t\n156\t    /// List all aliases.\n157\t    pub async fn list(&self) -> Vec<Alias> {\n158\t }\n182\t\n183\t    /// Update a multi-target alias (ILM use only).\n184\t    pub async fn update_multi(&self, name: &str, new_targets: Vec<String>) -> Result<()> {\n185\t        let mut aliases = }\n197\t}\n198\t\n199\t#[cfg(test)]\n200\tmod tests {\n201\t    use super::*;\n202\t\n203\t    #[test]\n204\t    fn test_new_single_alias() {\n205\t        let alias = }\n225\t\n226\t    #[test]\n227\t    fn test_alias_targets_multi() {\n228\t        let alias = Alias::new_multi(\"test\".into(), vec![\"a\".into(), \"b\".into()]);\n229\t        assert_eq!(alias.targets().unwrap(), vec![\"a\", \"b\"]);\n230\t    }\n231\t\n232\t    #[test]\n233\t    fn test_alias_flip() {\n234\t        let mut alias = Alias::new_single(\"products\".into(), Atomic index aliases for blue-green reindexing (plan §13.7).\n//!\n//! This module implements the alias layer that allows atomic index flips\n//! without downtime. Aliases resolve to one or more concrete Meilisearch\n//! index UIDs, supporting both single-target (writable) and multi-target\n//! (read-only, used by ILM) aliases.\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n\n/// Alias kind: single-target (writable) or multi-target (read-only, ILM-managed).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"lowercase\")]\npub enum AliasKind {\n    Single,\n    Multi,\n}\n\n/// A single alias record from the task store.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Alias {\n    /// Alias name (the key clients use).\n    pub name: String,\n    /// `single` or `multi`.\n    pub kind: AliasKind,\n    /// Current target UID (only set when kind=single).\n    pub current_uid: Option<String>,\n    /// Target UIDs as JSON array (only set when kind=multi).\n    pub target_uids: Option<Vec<String>>,\n    /// Generation incremented on each flip.\n    pub generation: u64,\n    /// Created at timestamp.\n    pub created_at: u64,\n    /// Last updated timestamp.\n    pub updated_at: u64,\n}\n\nimpl Alias {\n    /// Create a new single-target alias.\n    pub fn new_single(name: String, target_uid: String) -> Self {\n        let now = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_secs();\n        Self {\n            name,\n            kind: AliasKind::Single,\n            current_uid: Some(target_uid),\n            target_uids: None,\n            generation: 0,\n            created_at: now,\n            updated_at: now,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new multi-target alias.\n    pub fn new_multi(name: String, target_uids: Vec<String>) -> Self {\n        let now = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_secs();\n        Self {\n            name,\n            kind: AliasKind::Multi,\n            current_uid: None,\n            target_uids: Some(target_uids),\n            generation: 0,\n            created_at: now,\n            updated_at: now,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the effective target UIDs for this alias.\n    pub fn targets(&self) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n        match self.kind {\n            AliasKind::Single => {\n                let uid = self.current_uid.as_ref()\n                    .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::InvalidState(\"single alias missing current_uid\".into()))?;\n                Ok(vec![uid.clone()])\n            }\n            AliasKind::Multi => {\n                let uids = self.target_uids.as_ref()\n                    .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::InvalidState(\"multi alias missing target_uids\".into()))?;\n                Ok(uids.clone())\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Flip this alias to a new target (single-target only).\n    pub fn flip(&mut self, new_target: String) -> Result<()> {\n        if self.kind != AliasKind::Single {\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\"cannot flip multi-target alias\".into()));\n        }\n        self.current_uid = Some(new_target);\n        self.generation += 1;\n        self.updated_at = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_secs();\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Update multi-target alias UIDs (ILM-only).\n    pub fn update_targets(&mut self, new_targets: Vec<String>) -> Result<()> {\n        if self.kind != AliasKind::Multi {\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\"cannot update_targets on single-target alias\".into()));\n        }\n        self.target_uids = Some(new_targets);\n        self.generation += 1;\n        self.updated_at = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_secs();\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n/// In-memory alias registry with task-store persistence.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct AliasRegistry {\n    aliases: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, Alias>>>,\n}\n\nimpl AliasRegistry {\n    /// Create a new empty alias registry.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            aliases: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Resolve an index UID or alias name to concrete target UIDs.\n    ///\n    /// If `input` is not a known alias, returns it as-is (treat as concrete UID).\n    pub async fn resolve(&self, input: &str) -> Vec<String> {\n        let aliases = self.aliases.read().await;\n        match aliases.get(input) {\n            Some(alias) => alias.targets().unwrap_or_else(|_| vec![input.to_string()]),\n            None => vec![input.to_string()],\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if an input is an alias (vs a concrete UID).\n    pub async fn is_alias(&self, input: &str) -> bool {\n        self.aliases.read().await.contains_key(input)\n    }\n\n    /// Get a single alias by name.\n    pub async fn get(&self, name: &str) -> Option<Alias> {\n        self.aliases.read().await.get(name).cloned()\n    }\n\n    /// List all aliases.\n    pub async fn list(&self) -> Vec<Alias> {\n        self.aliases.read().await.values().cloned().collect()\n    }\n\n    /// Create or update an alias.\n    pub async fn upsert(&self, alias: Alias) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut aliases = self.aliases.write().await;\n        aliases.insert(alias.name.clone(), alias);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Delete an alias.\n    pub async fn delete(&self, name: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        let mut aliases = self.aliases.write().await;\n        Ok(aliases.remove(name).is_some())\n    }\n\n    /// Flip a single-target alias atomically.\n    pub async fn flip(&self, name: &str, new_target: String) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut aliases = self.aliases.write().await;\n        let alias = aliases.get_mut(name)\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::NotFound(format!(\"alias '{}' not found\", name)))?;\n        alias.flip(new_target)?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Update a multi-target alias (ILM use only).\n    pub async fn update_multi(&self, name: &str, new_targets: Vec<String>) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut aliases = self.aliases.write().await;\n        let alias = aliases.get_mut(name)\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::NotFound(format!(\"alias '{}' not found\", name)))?;\n        alias.update_targets(new_targets)?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for AliasRegistry {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_new_single_alias() {\n        let alias = Alias::new_single(\"products\".into(), \"products_v3\".into());\n        assert_eq!(alias.name, \"products\");\n        assert_eq!(alias.kind, AliasKind::Single);\n        assert_eq!(alias.current_uid, Some(\"products_v3\".into()));\n        assert_eq!(alias.generation, 0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_new_multi_alias() {\n        let alias = Alias::new_multi(\"logs\".into(), vec![\"logs-20260418\".into(), assert_eq!(alias.targets().unwrap(), vec![\"target_v1\"]);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_alias_targets_multi() {\n        let alias = Alias::new_multi(\"test\".into(), vec![\"a\".into(), \"b\".into()]);\n        assert_eq!(alias.targets().unwrap(), vec![\"a\", \"b\"]);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_alias_flip() {\n        let mut alias = Alias::new_single(\"products\".into(),
