Two-phase settings broadcast with verification (plan §13.5).\n2\t//!\n3\t//! This module implements the propose/verify/commit flow for settings changes,\n4\t//! replacing the sequential apply-with-rollback approach.\n5\t\n6\tuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\n7\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n8\tuse serde_json::Value;\n9\tuse std::collections::HashMap;\n10\tuse std::sync::Arc;\n11\tuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n12\t\n13\t/// Current phase of a settings broadcast.\n14\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n15\t#[repr(u8)]\n16\tpub enum BroadcastPhase {\n17\t    Idle = 0,\n18\t    Propose = 1,\n19\t    Verify = 2,\n20\t    Commit = 3,\n21\t}\n22\t\n23\t/// Status of an ongoing settings broadcast.\n24\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n25\tpub struct BroadcastStatus {\n26\t    /// Index UID.\n27\t    pub index: String,\n28\t    /// Current phase.\n29\t    pub phase: BroadcastPhase,\n30\t    /// Proposed settings fingerprint.\n31\t    pub proposed_fingerprint: Option<String>,\n32\t    /// Per-node task UIDs from Phase 1.\n33\t    pub node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64>,\n34\t    /// Per-node verification results from Phase 2.\n35\t    pub node_hashes: HashMap<String, String>,\n36\t    /// Whether verification succeeded.\n37\t    pub verify_ok: bool,\n38\t    /// Settings version after commit.\n39\t    pub settings_version: Option<u64>,\n40\t    /// Error message if any.\n41\t    pub error: Option<String>,\n42\t}\n43\t\n44\t/// Settings broadcast coordinator.\n45\tpub struct SettingsBroadcast {\n46\t    /// In-flight broadcasts (index -> status).\n47\t    in_flight: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, BroadcastStatus>>>,\n48\t    /// Global settings version (incremented on successful commit).\n49\t    settings_version: Arc<RwLock<u64>>,\n50\t    /// Per-(index, node) settings version (for X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version).\n51\t    node_settings_version: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<(String, String), u64>>>,\n52\t}\n53\t\n54\timpl SettingsBroadcast {\n55\t    /// Create a new settings broadcast coordinator.\n56\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n57\t        Self {\n58\t            in_flight: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n59\t            settings_version: Arc::new(RwLock::new(0)),\n60\t            node_settings_version: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n61\t        }\n62\t    }\n63\t\n64\t    /// Get the current global settings version.\n65\t    pub async fn current_version(&self) -> u64 {\n66\t        *self.settings_version.read().await\n67\t    }\n68\t\n69\t    /// Get the per-(index, node) settings version.\n70\t    pub async fn node_version(&self, index: &str, node_id: &str) -> u64 {\n71\t        let versions = self.node_settings_version.read().await;\n72\t        *versions.get(&(index.to_string(), node_id.to_string())).unwrap_or(&0)\n73\t    }\n74\t\n75\t    /// Start a new settings broadcast (Phase 1: Propose).\n76\t    ///\n77\t    /// The caller should:\n78\t    /// 1. PATCH /indexes/{uid}/settings on each node in parallel\n79\t    /// 2. Collect task_uid from each response\n80\t    /// 3. Call `enter_verify` with the task UIDs\n81\t    pub async fn start_propose(&self, index: String, settings: &Value) -> Result<String> {\n82\t        let mut in_flight = self.in_flight.write().await;\n83\t\n84\t        if in_flight.contains_key(&index) {\n85\t            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(format!(\n86\t                \"settings broadcast already in flight for index '{}'\",\n87\t                index\n88\t            )));\n89\t        }\n90\t\n91\t        let fingerprint = fingerprint_settings(settings);\n92\t        let status = BroadcastStatus {\n93\t            index: index.clone(),\n94\t            phase: BroadcastPhase::Propose,\n95\t            proposed_fingerprint: Some(fingerprint),\n96\t            node_task_uids: HashMap::new(),\n97\t            node_hashes: HashMap::new(),\n98\t            verify_ok: false,\n99\t            settings_version: None,\n100\t            error: None,\n101\t        };\n102\t\n103\t        in_flight.insert(index.clone(), status);\n104\t        Ok(index)\n105\t    }\n106\t\n107\t    /// Enter Phase 2: Verify.\n108\t    ///\n109\t    /// The caller should:\n110\t    /// 1. Wait for all node tasks to succeed\n111\t    /// 2. GET /indexes/{uid}/settings from each node\n112\t    /// 3. Compute SHA256 of canonical JSON for each\n113\t    /// 4. Call `verify_hashes` with the results\n114\t    pub async fn enter_verify(\n115\t        &self,\n116\t        index: &str,\n117\t        node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64>,\n118\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n119\t        let mut in_flight = self.in_flight.write().await;\n120\t        let status = in_flight.get_mut(index)\n121\t            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::NotFound(format!(\"index '{}'\", index)))?;\n122\t\n123\t        if status.phase != BroadcastPhase::Propose {\n124\t            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\"expected Propose phase\".into()));\n125\t        }\n126\t\n127\t        status.phase = BroadcastPhase::Verify;\n128\t        status.node_task_uids = node_task_uids;\n129\t        Ok(())\n130\t    }\n131\t\n132\t    /// Verify per-node settings hashes.\n133\t    ///\n134\t    /// Returns `Ok(())` if all hashes match the proposed fingerprint.\n135\t    /// Returns `Err` if any mismatch (caller should retry or abort).\n136\t    pub async fn verify_hashes(\n137\t        &self,\n138\t        index: &str,\n139\t        node_hashes: HashMap<String, String>,\n140\t        expected_fingerprint: &str,\n141\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n142\t        let mut in_flight = self.in_flight.write().await;\n143\t        let status = in_flight.get_mut(index)\n144\t            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::NotFound(format!(\"index '{}'\", index)))?;\n145\t\n146\t        if status.phase != BroadcastPhase::Verify {\n147\t            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\"expected Verify phase\".into()));\n148\t        }\n149\t\n150\t        status.node_hashes = node_hashes.clone();\n151\t\n152\t        // Check all hashes match the expected fingerprint.\n153\t        for (node, hash) in &node_hashes {\n154\t            if hash != expected_fingerprint {\n155\t                status.error = Some(format!(\n156\t                    \"node '{}' hash mismatch: expected {}, got {}\",\n157\t                    node, expected_fingerprint, hash\n158\t                ));\n159\t                status.verify_ok = false;\n160\t                return Err(MiroirError::SettingsDivergence);\n161\t            }\n162\t        }\n163\t\n164\t        status.verify_ok = true;\n165\t        Ok(())\n166\t    }\n167\t\n168\t    /// Enter Phase 3: Commit.\n169\t    ///\n170\t    /// Increments the global settings version and stamps all affected nodes.\n171\t    pub async fn commit(&self, index: &str) -> Result<u64> {\n172\t        let mut in_flight = self.in_flight.write().await;\n173\t        let status = in_flight.get_mut(index)\n174\t            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::NotFound(format!(\"index '{}'\", index)))?;\n175\t\n176\t        if status.phase != BroadcastPhase::Verify {\n177\t            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\"expected Verify phase\".into()));\n178\t        }\n179\t\n180\t        if !status.verify_ok {\n181\t            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\"verification failed\".into()));\n182\t        }\n183\t\n184\t        // Increment global settings version.\n185\t        let mut version = self.settings_version.write().await;\n186\t        *version += 1;\n187\t        let new_version = *version;\n188\t        drop(version);\n189\t\n190\t        // Update per-node versions for all nodes that verified successfully.\n191\t        let mut node_versions = self.node_settings_version.write().await;\n192\t        for node_id in status.node_hashes.keys() {\n193\t            node_versions.insert((index.to_string(), node_id.clone()), new_version);\n194\t        }\n195\t\n196\t        status.phase = BroadcastPhase::Commit;\n197\t        status.settings_version = Some(new_version);\n198\t\n199\t        // Remove from in-flight map after a short delay (caller should do this).\n200\t        Ok(new_version)\n201\t    }\n202\t\n203\t    /// Complete the broadcast and remove from in-flight tracking.\n204\t    pub async fn complete(&self, index: &str) -> Result<()> {\n205\t        let mut in_flight = self.in_flight.write().await;\n206\t        in_flight.remove(index)\n207\t            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::NotFound(format!(\"index '{}'\", index)))?;\n208\t        Ok(())\n209\t    }\n210\t\n211\t    /// Abort a broadcast (on error).\n212\t    pub async fn abort(&self, index: &str, error: String) -> Result<()> {\n213\t        let mut in_flight = self.in_flight.write().await;\n214\t        if let Some(status) = in_flight.get_mut(index) {\n215\t            status.error = Some(error);\n216\t        }\n217\t        in_flight.remove(index)\n218\t            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::NotFound(format!(\"index '{}'\", index)))?;\n219\t        Ok(())\n220\t    }\n221\t\n222\t    /// Get the status of an in-flight broadcast.\n223\t    pub async fn get_status(&self, index: &str) -> Option<BroadcastStatus> {\n224\t        self.in_flight.read().await.get(index).cloned()\n225\t    }\n226\t\n227\t    /// Check if a broadcast is in-flight for an index.\n228\t    pub async fn is_in_flight(&self, index: &str) -> bool {\n229\t        self.in_flight.read().await.contains_key(index)\n230\t    }\n231\t}\n232\t\n233\timpl Default for SettingsBroadcast {\n234\t    fn default() -> Self {\n235\t        Self::new()\n236\t    }\n237\t}\n238\t\n239\t/// Compute a fingerprint (SHA256) of settings as canonical JSON.\n240\tfn fingerprint_settings(settings: &Value) -> String {\n241\t    // Canonicalize: sort object keys, no extra whitespace.\n242\t    let canonical = if settings.is_object() {\n243\t        if let Some(obj) = settings.as_object() {\n244\t            // Collect and sort keys.\n245\t            let mut sorted_entries: Vec<_> = obj.iter().collect();\n246\t            sorted_entries.sort_by_key(|&(k, _)| k);\n247\t            // Reconstruct as a Map with sorted keys.\n248\t            let mut sorted_map = serde_json::Map::new();\n249\t            for (key, value) in sorted_entries {\n250\t                sorted_map.insert(key.clone(), value.clone());\n251\t            }\n252\t            serde_json::to_string(&sorted_map).unwrap_or_default()\n253\t        } else {\n254\t            serde_json::to_string(settings).unwrap_or_default()\n255\t        }\n256\t    } else {\n257\t        serde_json::to_string(settings).unwrap_or_default()\n258\t    };\n259\t\n260\t    // SHA256 hash.\n261\t    use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n262\t    let mut hasher = Sha256::new();\n263\t Two-phase settings broadcast with verification (plan §13.5).\n//!\n//! This module implements the propose/verify/commit flow for settings changes,\n//! replacing the sequential apply-with-rollback approach.\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n\n/// Current phase of a settings broadcast.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[repr(u8)]\npub enum BroadcastPhase {\n    Idle = 0,\n    Propose = 1,\n    Verify = 2,\n    Commit = 3,\n}\n\n/// Status of an ongoing settings broadcast.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct BroadcastStatus {\n    /// Index UID.\n    pub index: String,\n    /// Current phase.\n    pub phase: BroadcastPhase,\n    /// Proposed settings fingerprint.\n    pub proposed_fingerprint: Option<String>,\n    /// Per-node task UIDs from Phase 1.\n    pub node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64>,\n    /// Per-node verification results from Phase 2.\n    pub node_hashes: HashMap<String, String>,\n    /// Whether verification succeeded.\n    pub verify_ok: bool,\n    /// Settings version after commit.\n    pub settings_version: Option<u64>,\n    /// Error message if any.\n    pub error: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Settings broadcast coordinator.\npub struct SettingsBroadcast {\n    /// In-flight broadcasts (index -> status).\n    in_flight: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, BroadcastStatus>>>,\n    /// Global settings version (incremented on successful commit).\n    settings_version: Arc<RwLock<u64>>,\n    /// Per-(index, node) settings version (for X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version).\n    node_settings_version: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<(String, String), u64>>>,\n}\n\nimpl SettingsBroadcast {\n    /// Create a new settings broadcast coordinator.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            in_flight: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            settings_version: Arc::new(RwLock::new(0)),\n            node_settings_version: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current global settings version.\n    pub async fn current_version(&self) -> u64 {\n        *self.settings_version.read().await\n    }\n\n    /// Get the per-(index, node) settings version.\n    pub async fn node_version(&self, index: &str, node_id: &str) -> u64 {\n        let versions = self.node_settings_version.read().await;\n        *versions.get(&(index.to_string(), node_id.to_string())).unwrap_or(&0)\n    }\n\n    /// Start a new settings broadcast (Phase 1: Propose).\n    ///\n    /// The caller should:\n    /// 1. PATCH /indexes/{uid}/settings on each node in parallel\n    /// 2. Collect task_uid from each response\n    /// 3. Call `enter_verify` with the task UIDs\n    pub async fn start_propose(&self, index: String, settings: &Value) -> Result<String> {\n        let mut in_flight = self.in_flight.write().await;\n\n        if in_flight.contains_key(&index) {\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(format!(\n                \"settings broadcast already in flight for index '{}'\",\n                index\n            )));\n        }\n\n        let fingerprint = fingerprint_settings(settings);\n        let status = BroadcastStatus {\n            index: index.clone(),\n            phase: BroadcastPhase::Propose,\n            proposed_fingerprint: Some(fingerprint),\n            node_task_uids: HashMap::new(),\n            node_hashes: HashMap::new(),\n            verify_ok: false,\n            settings_version: None,\n            error: None,\n        };\n\n        in_flight.insert(index.clone(), status);\n        Ok(index)\n    }\n\n    /// Enter Phase 2: Verify.\n    ///\n    /// The caller should:\n    /// 1. Wait for all node tasks to succeed\n    /// 2. GET /indexes/{uid}/settings from each node\n    /// 3. Compute SHA256 of canonical JSON for each\n    /// 4. Call `verify_hashes` with the results\n    pub async fn enter_verify(\n        &self,\n        index: &str,\n        node_task_uids: HashMap<String, u64>,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut in_flight = self.in_flight.write().await;\n        let status = in_flight.get_mut(index)\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::NotFound(format!(\"index '{}'\", index)))?;\n\n        if status.phase != BroadcastPhase::Propose {\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\"expected Propose phase\".into()));\n        }\n\n        status.phase = BroadcastPhase::Verify;\n        status.node_task_uids = node_task_uids;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Verify per-node settings hashes.\n    ///\n    /// Returns `Ok(())` if all hashes match the proposed fingerprint.\n    /// Returns `Err` if any mismatch (caller should retry or abort).\n    pub async fn verify_hashes(\n        &self,\n        index: &str,\n        node_hashes: HashMap<String, String>,\n        expected_fingerprint: &str,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut in_flight = self.in_flight.write().await;\n        let status = in_flight.get_mut(index)\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::NotFound(format!(\"index '{}'\", index)))?;\n\n        if status.phase != BroadcastPhase::Verify {\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\"expected Verify phase\".into()));\n        }\n\n        status.node_hashes = node_hashes.clone();\n\n        // Check all hashes match the expected fingerprint.\n        for (node, hash) in &node_hashes {\n            if hash != expected_fingerprint {\n                status.error = Some(format!(\n                    \"node '{}' hash mismatch: expected {}, got {}\",\n                    node, expected_fingerprint, hash\n                ));\n                status.verify_ok = false;\n                return Err(MiroirError::SettingsDivergence);\n            }\n        }\n\n        status.verify_ok = true;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Enter Phase 3: Commit.\n    ///\n    /// Increments the global settings version and stamps all affected nodes.\n    pub async fn commit(&self, index: &str) -> Result<u64> {\n        let mut in_flight = self.in_flight.write().await;\n        let status = in_flight.get_mut(index)\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::NotFound(format!(\"index '{}'\", index)))?;\n\n        if status.phase != BroadcastPhase::Verify {\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\"expected Verify phase\".into()));\n        }\n\n        if !status.verify_ok {\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\"verification failed\".into()));\n        }\n\n        // Increment global settings version.\n        let mut version = self.settings_version.write().await;\n        *version += 1;\n        let new_version = *version;\n        drop(version);\n\n        // Update per-node versions for all nodes that verified successfully.\n        let mut node_versions = self.node_settings_version.write().await;\n        for node_id in status.node_hashes.keys() {\n            node_versions.insert((index.to_string(), node_id.clone()), new_version);\n        }\n\n        status.phase = BroadcastPhase::Commit;\n        status.settings_version = Some(new_version);\n\n        // Remove from in-flight map after a short delay (caller should do this).\n        Ok(new_version)\n    }\n\n    /// Complete the broadcast and remove from in-flight tracking.\n    pub async fn complete(&self, index: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut in_flight = self.in_flight.write().await;\n        in_flight.remove(index)\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::NotFound(format!(\"index '{}'\", index)))?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Abort a broadcast (on error).\n    pub async fn abort(&self, index: &str, error: String) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut in_flight = self.in_flight.write().await;\n        if let Some(status) = in_flight.get_mut(index) {\n            status.error = Some(error);\n        }\n        in_flight.remove(index)\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::NotFound(format!(\"index '{}'\", index)))?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Get the status of an in-flight broadcast.\n    pub async fn get_status(&self, index: &str) -> Option<BroadcastStatus> {\n        self.in_flight.read().await.get(index).cloned()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a broadcast is in-flight for an index.\n    pub async fn is_in_flight(&self, index: &str) -> bool {\n        self.in_flight.read().await.contains_key(index)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for SettingsBroadcast {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Compute a fingerprint (SHA256) of settings as canonical JSON.\nfn fingerprint_settings(settings: &Value) -> String {\n    // Canonicalize: sort object keys, no extra whitespace.\n    let canonical = if settings.is_object() {\n        if let Some(obj) = settings.as_object() {\n            // Collect and sort keys.\n            let mut sorted_entries: Vec<_> = obj.iter().collect();\n            sorted_entries.sort_by_key(|&(k, _)| k);\n            // Reconstruct as a Map with sorted keys.\n            let mut sorted_map = serde_json::Map::new();\n            for (key, value) in sorted_entries {\n                sorted_map.insert(key.clone(), value.clone());\n            }\n            serde_json::to_string(&sorted_map).unwrap_or_default()\n        } else {\n            serde_json::to_string(settings).unwrap_or_default()\n        }\n    } else {\n        serde_json::to_string(settings).unwrap_or_default()\n    };\n\n    // SHA256 hash.\n    use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n    let mut hasher = Sha256::new();\n    hasher.update(canonical.as_bytes());\n    format!(\"{:x}\", hasher.finalize())\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use serde_json::json;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_fingerprint_settings() {\n        let settings1 = json!({\"rankingRules\": [\"words\", \"typo\"], \"stopWords\": [\"the\", \"a\"]});\n        let settings2 = json!({\"stopWords\": [\"the\", \"a\"], \"rankingRules\": [\"words\", \"typo\"]});\n\n        // Order-independent canonicalization should produce same fingerprint.\n        let fp1 = fingerprint_settings(&settings1);\n        let fp2 = fingerprint_settings(&settings2);\n        assert_eq!(fp1, fp2);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_broadcast_full_flow() {\n        let broadcast = SettingsBroadcast::new();\n\n        // Start propose.\n        let index = \"products\".to_string();\n        let settings = json!({\"rankingRules\": [\"words\"]});\n        let fp = fingerprint_settings(&settings);\n        broadcast.start_propose(index.clone(), &settings).await.unwrap();\n\n        // Enter verify with node task UIDs.\n        let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), 100);\n        node_tasks.insert(\"node-2\".to_string(), 101);\n        broadcast.enter_verify(&index, node_tasks).await.unwrap();\n\n        // Verify hashes.\n        let mut node_hashes = HashMap::new();\n        node_hashes.insert(\"node-1\".to_string(), fp.clone());\n        node_hashes.insert(\"node-2\".to_string(), fp.clone());\n        broadcast.verify_hashes(&index, node_hashes, &fp).await.unwrap();\n\n        // Commit.\n        let new_version = assert!(!broadcast.is_in_flight(&index).await);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_broadcast_hash_mismatch() {\n        let broadcast = SettingsBroadcast::new();\n\n        let index = )\n35\t    }\n36\t}\n37\t\n38\timpl TimeWindow {\n39\t    /// Parse a `\"HH:MM-HH:MM\"` string (UTC).\n40\t    pub fn parse(s: &str) -> Result<Self, String> {\n41\t        let (start, end) = s\n42\t            .split_once('-')\n43\t            .ok_or_else(|| format!(\"expected HH:MM-HH:MM, got {}\", s))?;\n44\t        Ok(TimeWindow {\n45\t            start_mins: Self::parse_hm(start)?,\n46\t            end_mins: Self::parse_hm(end)?,\n47\t        })\n48\t    }\n49\t\n50\t    fn parse_hm(hm: &str) -> Result<u16, String> {\n51\t        let (h, m) = hm\n52\t            .split_once(':')\n53\t            .ok_or_else(|| format!(\"expected HH:MM, got {}\", hm))?;\n54\t        let h: u16 = h.parse().map_err(|_| format!(\"invalid hour: {}\", h))?;\n55\t        let m: u16 = m.parse().map_err(|_| }\n61\t\n62\t    /// Does `utc_minutes` (minutes since midnight UTC) fall inside this window?\n63\t    pub fn contains(&self, utc_minutes: u16) -> bool {\n64\t        if self.start_mins <= >= self.start_mins || utc_minutes < self.end_mins\n69\t        }\n70\t    }\n71\t}\n72\t\n73\t/// Resharding configuration (plan §13.1 + schedule window guard).\n74\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, }\n118\t    }\n119\t}\n120\t\n121\t/// Result of the schedule window guard check.\n122\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n123\tpub enum WindowGuardResult {\n124\t    /// Current time is inside minute-of-day.\n136\t///\n137\t/// Returns `Allowed` if `utc_minute` falls inside any configured window,\n138\t/// `NoRestriction` if no windows are configured, or `Denied` otherwise.\n139\tpub fn check_window(utc_minute: u16, config: &ReshardingConfig) -> WindowGuardResult {\n140\t    if config.allowed_windows.is_empty() {\n141\t        return WindowGuardResult::NoRestriction;\n142\t    }\n143\t\n144\t    for raw in &config.allowed_windows {\n145\t        let window = match TimeWindow::parse(raw) {\n146\t            Ok(w) => w,\n147\t            Err(_) => continue,\n148\t        };\n149\t        if window.contains(utc_minute) {\n150\t            return WindowGuardResult::Allowed {\n151\t                window: raw.clone(),\n152\t            };\n153\t        }\n154\t    }\n155\t\n156\t    WindowGuardResult::Denied {\n157\t        utc_now: format!(\"{:02}:{:02} UTC\", utc_minute / 60, utc_minute % 60),\n158\t        allowed: config.allowed_windows.clone(),\n159\t    }\n160\t}\n161\t\n162\t/// Check the schedule window against the system clock.\n163\tpub fn check_window_now(config: &ReshardingConfig) -> WindowGuardResult {\n164\t    let utc_minute = current_utc_minute();\n165\t    check_window(utc_minute, config)\n166\t}\n167\t\n168\tfn current_utc_minute() -> u16 {\n169\t    let duration = SystemTime::now()\n170\t        .duration_since(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)\n171\t        .unwrap_or_default();\n172\t    ((duration.as_secs() / 60) % (24 * 60)) as u16\n173\t}\n174\t\n175\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n176\t// Resharding load simulation\n177\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n178\t\n179\t/// Parameters for a single simulation run.\n180\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n181\tpub struct SimParams {\n182\t    /// Document size in bytes.\n183\t    pub doc_size_bytes: u64,\n184\t    /// Total corpus size in bytes.\n185\t    pub corpus_size_bytes: u64,\n186\t    /// Incoming write rate in documents per second.\n187\t    pub write_rate_dps: u64,\n188\t    /// Number of replica groups.\n189\t    pub replica_groups: u32,\n190\t    /// Replication factor (intra-group copies per shard).\n191\t    pub replication_factor: usize,\n192\t    /// Old shard count (before reshard).\n193\t    pub old_shards: u32,\n194\t    /// New shard count (after reshard).\n195\t    pub new_shards: u32,\n196\t    /// Number of nodes per replica group.\n197\t    pub nodes_per_group: usize,\n198\t    /// Backfill throttle in documents per second (0 = unlimited).\n199\t    pub backfill_throttle_dps: u64,\n200\t}\n201\t\n202\t/// Results from a single simulation run.\n203\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n204\tpub struct SimResult {\n205\t    pub label: String,\n206\t    pub doc_size_bytes: u64,\n207\t    pub corpus_size_bytes: u64,\n208\t    pub total_docs: u64,\n209\t    pub replica_groups: u32,\n210\t    pub replication_factor: usize,\n211\t    pub old_shards: u32,\n212\t    pub new_shards: u32,\n213\t    pub nodes_per_group: usize,\n214\t    pub write_rate_dps: u64,\n215\t\n216\t    /// Normal steady-state storage across entire cluster (bytes).\n217\t    pub normal_storage_bytes: u64,\n218\t    /// Peak storage during resharding (live + shadow full, bytes).\n219\t    pub peak_storage_bytes: u64,\n220\t    /// Storage amplification factor (peak / normal).\n221\t    pub storage_amplification: f64,\n222\t\n223\t    /// Normal steady-state write rate (actual node writes/sec).\n224\t    pub normal_write_rate: u64,\n225\t    /// Dual-write rate (phase 2 only, no backfill).\n226\t    pub dual_write_rate: u64,\n227\t    /// Peak write rate during backfill + dual-write.\n228\t    pub peak_write_rate: u64,\n229\t    /// Write amplification factor during dual-write only.\n230\t    pub dual_write_amplification: f64,\n231\t    /// Write amplification factor during peak (backfill + dual-write).\n232\t    pub peak_write_amplification: f64,\n233\t\n234\t    /// Backfill duration in seconds (at configured throttle).\n235\t    pub backfill_duration_secs: f64,\n236\t    /// Total bytes written during full reshard operation.\n237\t    pub total_bytes_written: u64,\n238\t\n239\t    /// Per-node peak storage (bytes).\n240\t    pub per_node_peak_storage_bytes: u64,\n241\t    /// Per-node normal storage (bytes).\n242\t    pub per_node_normal_storage_bytes: u64,\n243\t\n244\t    /// Hash distribution stats for old shards.\n245\t    pub old_shard_cv: f64,\n246\t    /// Hash distribution stats for new shards.\n247\t    pub new_shard_cv: f64,\n248\t}\n249\t\n250\t/// Run a resharding load simulation with the given parameters.\n251\t///\n252\t/// This models the six-phase resharding process from plan §13.1 using\n253\t/// the actual routing code to compute shard assignments and estimate\n254\t/// storage/write load. Document keys are synthesized for the corpus size\n255\t/// and routed through the real hash function to measure distribution.\n256\tpub fn simulate(params: &SimParams) -> SimResult {\n257\t    let total_docs = params.corpus_size_bytes / params.doc_size_bytes;\n258\t    let rf = params.replication_factor;\n259\t    let rg = params.replica_groups;\n260\t    let nodes_per_group = params.nodes_per_group;\n261\t\n262\t    // Build a synthetic topology for the simulation.\n263\t    let groups: Vec<Group> = (0..rg)\n264\t        .map(|g| {\n265\t            let mut group = Group::new(g);\n266\t            for n in 0..nodes_per_group {\n267\t                group.add_node(NodeId::new(format!(\"node-g{}-n{}\", g, n)));\n268\t            }\n269\t            group\n270\t        })\n271\t        .collect();\n272\t\n273\t    // Simulate document distribution across old and new shard counts.\n274\t    // Use the actual router hash to get realistic distribution.\n275\t    let mut old_shard_counts: Vec<u64> = vec![0; params.old_shards as usize];\n276\t    let mut new_shard_counts: Vec<u64> = vec![0; params.new_shards as usize];\n277\t\n278\t    // Track per-node storage for old and new shard assignments.\n279\t    // Each group stores the full corpus; each node in a group stores its\n280\t    // rendezvous-assigned fraction.\n281\t    let total_nodes = (rg as usize) * nodes_per_group;\n282\t    let mut node_storage_old: Vec<u64> = vec![0; total_nodes];\n283\t    let mut node_storage_new: Vec<u64> = vec![0; total_nodes];\n284\t\n285\t    for i in 0..total_docs {\n286\t        let key = format!(\"doc-{}\", i);\n287\t        let old_shard = shard_for_key(&key, params.old_shards);\n288\t        let new_shard = shard_for_key(&key, params.new_shards);\n289\t\n290\t        old_shard_counts[old_shard as usize] += 1;\n291\t        new_shard_counts[new_shard as usize] += 1;\n292\t\n293\t        // For each replica group, assign shard to RF nodes.\n294\t        for (g_idx, group) in groups.iter().enumerate() {\n295\t            let old_targets = assign_shard_in_group(old_shard, group.nodes(), rf);\n296\t            let new_targets = assign_shard_in_group(new_shard, group.nodes(), rf);\n297\t\n298\t            for node_id in &old_targets {\n299\t                let node_idx = g_idx * nodes_per_group\n300\t                    + group.nodes().iter().position(|n| n == node_id).unwrap_or(0);\n301\t                node_storage_old[node_idx] += params.doc_size_bytes;\n302\t            }\n303\t            for node_id in &new_targets {\n304\t                let node_idx = g_idx * nodes_per_group\n305\t                    + group.nodes().iter().position(|n| n == node_id).unwrap_or(0);\n306\t                node_storage_new[node_idx] += params.doc_size_bytes;\n307\t            }\n308\t        }\n309\t    }\n310\t\n311\t    // Compute distribution coefficients of variation.\n312\t    let old_cv = cv(&old_shard_counts);\n313\t    let new_cv = cv(&new_shard_counts);\n314\t\n315\t    // Normal storage: corpus replicated across RG groups.\n316\t    let normal_storage_bytes = params.corpus_size_bytes * rg as u64;\n317\t    // Peak storage: live + shadow (both fully populated).\n318\t    let peak_storage_bytes = normal_storage_bytes * 2;\n319\t\n320\t    // Per-node storage (max across all nodes).\n321\t    let per_node_normal = node_storage_old.iter().copied().max().unwrap_or(0);\n322\t    let per_node_peak = per_node_normal + node_storage_new.iter().copied().max().unwrap_or(0);\n323\t\n324\t    // Write rates.\n325\t    // Normal: each incoming doc → RF × RG actual node writes.\n326\t    let normal_write_rate = params.write_rate_dps * rf as u64 * rg as u64;\n327\t    // Dual-write: each incoming doc → 2 × (RF × RG) writes (old + new assignment).\n328\t    let dual_write_rate = normal_write_rate * 2;\n329\t    // Backfill: reads all docs, writes each to new assignment → RF × RG writes/doc.\n330\t    // Plus ongoing dual-writes for new incoming docs.\n331\t    let backfill_write_rate = params.backfill_throttle_dps * rf as u64 * rg as u64;\n332\t    let peak_write_rate = dual_write_rate + backfill_write_rate;\n333\t\n334\t    let dual_write_amplification = 2.0;\n335\t    let peak_write_amplification = peak_write_rate as f64 / normal_write_rate as f64;\n336\t\n337\t    // Backfill duration: total docs / throttle rate.\n338\t    let backfill_duration_secs = if params.backfill_throttle_dps > 0 {\n339\t        total_docs as f64 / params.backfill_throttle_dps as f64\n340\t    } else {\n341\t        f64::INFINITY\n342\t    };\n343\t\n344\t    // Total bytes written during reshard:\n345\t    // 1. Dual-write ongoing for the full reshard duration.\n346\t    // 2. Backfill writes of entire corpus.\n347\t    // Approximate: backfill_duration × dual_write_rate + corpus × RF × RG.\n348\t    let total_reshard_write_bytes = if params.backfill_throttle_dps > 0 {\n349\t        let dual_write_bytes =\n350\t            backfill_duration_secs * dual_write_rate as f64 * params.doc_size_bytes as f64;\n351\t        let backfill_bytes = total_docs * rf as u64 * rg as u64 * params.doc_size_bytes;\n352\t        (dual_write_bytes as u64) + backfill_bytes\n353\t    } else {\n354\t        0\n355\t    };\n356\t\n357\t    let storage_amplification = peak_storage_bytes as f64 / normal_storage_bytes as f64;\n358\t\n359\t    SimResult {\n360\t        label: format!(\n361\t            \"{}KB/{}GB/RG{}/RF{}\",\n362\t            params.doc_size_bytes / 1024,\n363\t            params.corpus_size_bytes / (1024 * 1024 * 1024),\n364\t            rg,\n365\t            rf\n366\t        ),\n367\t        doc_size_bytes: params.doc_size_bytes,\n368\t        corpus_size_bytes: params.corpus_size_bytes,\n369\t        total_docs,\n370\t        replica_groups: rg,\n371\t        replication_factor: rf,\n372\t        old_shards: params.old_shards,\n373\t        new_shards: params.new_shards,\n374\t        nodes_per_group,\n375\t        write_rate_dps: params.write_rate_dps,\n376\t\n377\t        normal_storage_bytes,\n378\t        peak_storage_bytes,\n379\t        storage_amplification,\n380\t\n381\t        normal_write_rate,\n382\t        dual_write_rate,\n383\t        peak_write_rate,\n384\t        dual_write_amplification,\n385\t        peak_write_amplification,\n386\t\n387\t        backfill_duration_secs,\n388\t        total_bytes_written: total_reshard_write_bytes,\n389\t\n390\t        per_node_peak_storage_bytes: per_node_peak,\n391\t        per_node_normal_storage_bytes: per_node_normal,\n392\t\n393\t        old_shard_cv: old_cv,\n394\t        new_shard_cv: new_cv,\n395\t    }\n396\t}\n397\t\n398\t/// Coefficient of variation for a distribution.\n399\tfn cv(values: &[u64]) -> f64 {\n400\t    if values.is_empty() {\n401\t        return 0.0;\n402\t    }\n403\t    let n = values.len() as f64;\n404\t    let mean = values.iter().sum::<u64>() as f64 / n;\n405\t    if mean == 0.0 {\n406\t        return 0.0;\n407\t    }\n408\t    let variance = values\n409\t        .iter()\n410\t        .map(|v| (*v as f64 - mean).powi(2))\n411\t        .sum::<f64>()\n412\t        / n;\n413\t    variance.sqrt() / mean\n414\t}\n415\t\n416\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n417\t// Tests\n418\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n419\t\n420\t#[cfg(test)]\n421\tmod tests {\n422\t    use super::*;\n423\t\n424\t    // ---- TimeWindow parsing and containment ----\n425\t\n426\t    #[test]\n427\t    fn time_window_parse_simple() {\n428\t        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n429\t        assert_eq!(w.start_mins, 120);\n430\t        assert_eq!(w.end_mins, 360);\n431\t    }\n432\t\n433\t    #[test]\n434\t    fn time_window_parse_wrap_midnight() {\n435\t        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"22:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n436\t        assert_eq!(w.start_mins, 1320);\n437\t        assert_eq!(w.end_mins, 360);\n438\t    }\n439\t\n440\t    #[test]\n441\t    fn 01:40\n445\t        assert!(!w.contains(400)); // 06:40\n446\t    }\n447\t\n448\t    #[test]\n449\t    fn time_window_contains_wrap() {\n450\t        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"22:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n451\t        assert!(w.contains(1350)); // 22:30\n452\t        assert!(w.contains(300)); // 05:00\n453\t        assert!(!w.contains(700)); // 11:40\n454\t    }\n455\t\n456\t    #[test]\n457\t    fn time_window_boundary_start() {\n458\t        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n459\t        assert!(w.contains(120)); // exactly 02:00\n460\t    }\n461\t\n462\t    #[test]\n463\t    fn time_window_boundary_end_exclusive() {\n464\t        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n465\t        assert!(!w.contains(360)); // exactly 06:00 is excluded\n466\t    }\n467\t\n468\t    #[test]\n469\t    fn time_window_invalid_format() {\n470\t        assert!(TimeWindow::parse(\"not-a-window\").is_err());\n471\t        assert!(TimeWindow::parse(\"25:00-06:00\").is_err());\n472\t        assert!(TimeWindow::parse(\"02:60-06:00\").is_err());\n473\t    }\n474\t\n475\t    // ---- Window guard ----\n476\t\n477\t    #[test]\n478\t    fn window_guard_no_restriction() {\n479\t        let config = ReshardingConfig::default();\n480\t        assert_eq!(check_window(0, &config), WindowGuardResult::NoRestriction);\n481\t    }\n482\t\n483\t    #[test]\n484\t    fn window_guard_allowed() {\n485\t        let config = ReshardingConfig {\n486\t            allowed_windows: vec![\"02:00-06:00\".into()],\n487\t            ..Default::default()\n488\t        };\n489\t        let result = check_window(180, &config); // 03:00\n490\t        assert!(matches!(result, WindowGuardResult::Allowed { .. }));\n491\t    }\n492\t\n493\t    #[test]\n494\t    fn window_guard_denied() {\n495\t        let config = ReshardingConfig {\n496\t            allowed_windows: vec![\"02:00-06:00\".into()],\n497\t            ..Default::default()\n498\t        };\n499\t        let result = check_window(720, &config); // 12:00\n500\t        assert!(matches!(result, WindowGuardResult::Denied { .. }));\n501\t    }\n502\t\n503\t    #[test]\n504\t    fn window_guard_multiple_windows() {\n505\t        let config = ReshardingConfig {\n506\t            allowed_windows: vec![\"02:00-04:00\".into(), \"22:00-23:30\".into()],\n507\t            ..Default::default()\n508\t        };\n509\t        // In first window.\n510\t        assert!(matches!(\n511\t            check_window(150, &config),\n512\t            WindowGuardResult::Allowed { .. }\n513\t        ));\n514\t        // In second window.\n515\t        assert!(matches!(\n516\t            check_window(1350, &config),\n517\t            WindowGuardResult::Allowed { .. }\n518\t        ));\n519\t        // Outside both.\n520\t        assert!(matches!(\n521\t            check_window(720, &config),\n522\t            WindowGuardResult::Denied { .. }\n523\t        ));\n524\t    }\n525\t\n526\t    // ---- Simulation ----\n527\t\n528\t    #[test]\n529\t    fn simulation_storage_always_2x() {\n530\t        // Regardless of parameters, peak storage should be exactly 2× normal.\n531\t        let params = SimParams {\n532\t            doc_size_bytes: 1024,\n533\t            corpus_size_bytes: 10 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024, // 10 GB\n534\t            write_rate_dps: 100,\n535\t            replica_groups: 2,\n536\t            replication_factor: 1,\n537\t            old_shards: 64,\n538\t            new_shards: 128,\n539\t            nodes_per_group: 3,\n540\t            backfill_throttle_dps: 10_000,\n541\t        };\n542\t        let result = simulate(&params);\n543\t        assert!(\n544\t            (result.storage_amplification - 2.0).abs() < 0.01,\n545\t            \"expected ~2.0, got {}\",\n546\t            result.storage_amplification\n547\t        );\n548\t    }\n549\t\n550\t    #[test]\n551\t    fn simulation_dual_write_is_2x() {\n552\t        let params = SimParams {\n553\t            doc_size_bytes: 1024,\n554\t            corpus_size_bytes: 1024 * 1024,\n555\t            write_rate_dps: 100,\n556\t            replica_groups: 2,\n557\t            replication_factor: 1,\n558\t            old_shards: 16,\n559\t            new_shards: 32,\n560\t            nodes_per_group: 3,\n561\t            backfill_throttle_dps: 1000,\n562\t        };\n563\t        let result = simulate(&params);\n564\t        assert!(\n565\t            (result.dual_write_amplification - 2.0).abs() < 0.01,\n566\t            \"expected 2.0, got {}\",\n567\t            result.dual_write_amplification\n568\t        );\n569\t    }\n570\t\n571\t    #[test]\n572\t    fn simulation_low_cv_with_many_docs() {\n573\t        // With enough docs, hash distribution CV should be very low (< 5%).\n574\t        let params = SimParams {\n575\t            doc_size_bytes: 1024,\n576\t            corpus_size_bytes: 1_000_000 * 1024, // 1M docs × 1KB\n577\t            write_rate_dps: 100,\n578\t            replica_groups: 1,\n579\t            replication_factor: 1,\n580\t            old_shards: 16,\n581\t            new_shards: 64,\n582\t            nodes_per_group: 4,\n583\t            backfill_throttle_dps: 1000,\n584\t        };\n585\t        let result = simulate(&params);\n586\t        assert!(\n587\t            result.old_shard_cv < 0.05,\n588\t            \"old shard CV too high: {}\",\n589\t            result.old_shard_cv\n590\t        );\n591\t        assert!(\n592\t            result.new_shard_cv < 0.05,\n593\t            \"new shard CV too high: {}\",\n594\t            result.new_shard_cv\n595\t        );\n596\t    }\n597\t\n598\t    #[test]\n599\t    fn time_window_display_roundtrip() {\n600\t        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:30-06:45\").unwrap();\n601\t        assert_eq!(format!(\"{}\", w), \"02:30-06:45\");\n602\t    }\n603\t\n604\t    #[test]\n605\t    fn time_window_parse_missing_dash() {\n606\t        let err = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00\").unwrap_err();\n607\t        assert!(err.contains(\"expected HH:MM-HH:MM\"));\n608\t    }\n609\t\n610\t    #[test]\n611\t    fn time_window_parse_invalid_hm() {\n612\t        let err = TimeWindow::parse(\"ab:00-06:00\").unwrap_err();\n613\t        assert!(err.contains(\"invalid hour\"));\n614\t    }\n615\t\n616\t    #[test]\n617\t    fn time_window_parse_invalid_minute() {\n618\t        let err = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:ab-06:00\").unwrap_err();\n619\t        assert!(err.contains(\"invalid minute\"));\n620\t    }\n621\t\n622\t    #[test]\n623\t    fn check_window_now_no_restriction() {\n624\t        let config = ReshardingConfig::default();\n625\t        assert!(matches!(check_window_now(&config), WindowGuardResult::NoRestriction));\n626\t    }\n627\t\n628\t    #[test]\n629\t    fn simulation_throttle_zero_gives_infinity_duration() {\n630\t        let params = SimParams {\n631\t            doc_size_bytes: 1024,\n632\t            corpus_size_bytes: 1024 * 1024,\n633\t            write_rate_dps: 100,\n634\t            replica_groups: 1,\n635\t            replication_factor: 1,\n636\t            old_shards: 4,\n637\t            new_shards: 8,\n638\t            nodes_per_group: 2,\n639\t            backfill_throttle_dps: 0,\n640\t        };\n641\t        let result = simulate(&params);\n642\t        assert!(result.backfill_duration_secs.is_infinite());\n643\t        assert_eq!(result.total_bytes_written, 0);\n644\t    }\n645\t\n646\t    #[test]\n647\t    fn cv_empty_and_zero() {\n648\t        assert_eq!(cv(&[]), 0.0);\n649\t        assert_eq!(cv(&[0, 0, 0]), 0.0);\n650\t    }\n651\t}\n652\t\n653\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n654\t// Six-phase resharding execution (plan §13.1)\n655\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n656\t\n657\t/// Resharding phase identifiers (matching plan §13.1 steps).\n658\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n659\t#[repr(u8)]\n660\tpub enum ReshardPhase {\n661\t    /// No active resharding.\n662\t    Idle = 0,\n663\t    /// Phase 1: Shadow index created.\n664\t    ShadowCreated = 1,\n665\t    /// Phase 2: Dual-hash dual-write active.\n666\t    DualWriteActive = 2,\n667\t    /// Phase 3: Backfill in progress.\n668\t    BackfillInProgress = 3,\n669\t    /// Phase 4: Verification in progress.\n670\t    Verifying = 4,\n671\t    /// Phase 5: Alias swap completed.\n672\t    Swapped = 5,\n673\t    /// Phase 6: Cleanup in progress.\n674\t    CleaningUp = 6,\n675\t    /// Resharding completed successfully.\n676\t    Complete = 7,\n677\t    /// Resharding failed.\n678\t    Failed = 8,\n679\t}\n680\t\n681\timpl ReshardPhase {\n682\t    /// Human-readable phase name.\n683\t    pub fn name(&self) -> &'static str {\n684\t        match self {\n685\t            Self::Idle => \"Idle\",\n686\t            Self::ShadowCreated => \"Shadow Created\",\n687\t            Self::DualWriteActive => \"Dual-Write Active\",\n688\t            Self::BackfillInProgress => \"Backfill In Progress\",\n689\t            Self::Verifying => \"Verifying\",\n690\t            Self::Swapped => \"Swapped\",\n691\t            Self::CleaningUp => \"Cleaning Up\",\n692\t            Self::Complete => \"Complete\",\n693\t            Self::Failed => \"Failed\",\n694\t        }\n695\t    }\n696\t\n697\t    /// Parse from u8 (for storage).\n698\t    pub fn from_u8(v: u8) -> Option<Self> {\n699\t        match v {\n700\t            0 => Some(Self::Idle),\n701\t            1 => Some(Self::ShadowCreated),\n702\t            2 => Some(Self::DualWriteActive),\n703\t            3 => Some(Self::BackfillInProgress),\n704\t            4 => Some(Self::Verifying),\n705\t            5 => Some(Self::Swapped),\n706\t            6 => Some(Self::CleaningUp),\n707\t            7 => Some(Self::Complete),\n708\t            8 => Some(Self::Failed),\n709\t            _ => None,\n710\t        }\n711\t    }\n712\t}\n713\t\n714\t/// Active resharding operation state.\n715\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n716\tpub struct ReshardOperation {\n717\t    /// Unique operation ID.\n718\t    pub id: String,\n719\t    /// Index UID being resharded.\n720\t    pub index_uid: String,\n721\t    /// Old shard count.\n722\t    pub old_shards: u32,\n723\t    /// New shard count.\n724\t    pub target_shards: u32,\n725\t    /// Current phase.\n726\t    pub phase: ReshardPhase,\n727\t    /// Phase started at (UNIX ms).\n728\t    pub phase_started_at: u64,\n729\t    /// Operation created at (UNIX ms).\n730\t    pub created_at: u64,\n731\t    /// Documents backfilled so far.\n732\t    pub documents_backfilled: u64,\n733\t    /// Total documents to backfill (estimated at start).\n734\t    pub total_documents: u64,\n735\t    /// Last error message (if any).\n736\t    pub last_error: Option<String>,\n737\t    /// Shadow index UID.\n738\t    pub shadow_index: String,\n739\t    /// Verification results (populated after phase 4).\n740\t    pub verification_results: Option<VerificationResults>,\n741\t    /// Cleanup retention deadline (UNIX ms).\n742\t    pub cleanup_deadline: Option<u64>,\n743\t}\n744\t\n745\t/// Results from phase 4 verification.\n746\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n747\tpub struct VerificationResults {\n748\t    /// Live index PK set size.\n749\t    pub live_pk_count: u64,\n750\t    /// Shadow index PK set size.\n751\t    pub shadow_pk_count: u64,\n752\t    /// PKs only in live index.\n753\t    pub live_only_pks: Vec<String>,\n754\t    /// PKs only in shadow index.\n755\t    pub shadow_only_pks: Vec<String>,\n756\t    /// PKs with content hash mismatch.\n757\t    pub mismatched_pks: Vec<String>,\n758\t    /// Whether verification passed.\n759\t    pub passed: bool,\n760\t}\n761\t\n762\timpl ReshardOperation {\n763\t    /// Create a new resharding operation.\n764\t    pub fn new(index_uid: String, old_shards: u32, target_shards: u32) -> Self {\n765\t        let id = format!(\"reshard-{}-{}\", index_uid, uuid::Uuid::new_v4());\n766\t        let shadow_index = format!(\"{}__reshard_{}\", index_uid, target_shards);\n767\t        let now = millis_now();\n768\t        Self {\n769\t            id,\n770\t            index_uid,\n771\t            old_shards,\n772\t            target_shards,\n773\t            phase: ReshardPhase::ShadowCreated,\n774\t            phase_started_at: now,\n775\t            created_at: now,\n776\t            documents_backfilled: 0,\n777\t            total_documents: 0,\n778\t            last_error: None,\n779\t            shadow_index,\n780\t            verification_results: None,\n781\t            cleanup_deadline: None,\n782\t        }\n783\t    }\n784\t\n785\t    /// Transition to the next phase.\n786\t    pub fn advance_phase(&mut self, new_phase: ReshardPhase) {\n787\t        self.phase = new_phase;\n788\t        self.phase_started_at = millis_now();\n789\t    }\n790\t\n791\t    /// Record an error and mark as failed.\n792\t    pub fn fail(&mut self, error: String) {\n793\t        self.last_error = fn backfill_progress(&self) -> f64 {\n805\t        if self.total_documents == 0 {\n806\t            return 0.0;\n807\t        }\n808\t        (self.documents_backfilled as f64) / (self.total_documents as f64)\n809\t    }\n810\t\n811\t    /// Check if the operation is in a terminal state.\n812\t    pub fn is_terminal(&self) -> bool {\n813\t        matches!(\n814\t            self.phase,\n815\t            ReshardPhase::Complete | ReshardPhase::Failed\n816\t        )\n817\t    }\n818\t\n819\t    /// Get the shadow index name.\n820\t    pub fn shadow_index_name(&self) -> &str {\n821\t        &self.shadow_index\n822\t    }\n823\t}\n824\t\n825\t/// Get current UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.\n826\tfn millis_now() -> u64 {\n827\t    SystemTime::now()\n828\t   }\n878\t\n879\t    /// Update an operation.\n880\t    pub fn update(&mut self, op: ReshardOperation) -> Result<(), String> {\n881\t        if !self.operations.contains_key(&op.id) {\n882\t self.index_ops.remove(&op.index_uid);\n895\t        Ok(())\n896\t    }\n897\t\n898\t    /// List all operations.\n899\t    pub fn list(&self) -> Vec<&ReshardOperation> {\n900\t Online resharding: window guard, simulation model, and six-phase execution.\n//!\n//! Implements the plan §13.1 shadow-index resharding mechanics and §15 OP#3\n//! empirical validation of the 2× transient load caveat.\n\nuse crate::router::{assign_shard_in_group, shard_for_key};\nuse crate::topology::{Group, NodeId};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Schedule window guard\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// A UTC time window like `\"02:00-06:00\"`.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub struct TimeWindow {\n    /// Start hour+minute in minutes since midnight UTC.\n    pub start_mins: u16,\n    /// End hour+minute in minutes since midnight UTC.\n    pub end_mins: u16,\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Display for TimeWindow {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        write!(\n            f,\n            \"{:02}:{:02}-{:02}:{:02}\",\n            self.start_mins / 60,\n            self.start_mins % 60,\n            self.end_mins / 60,\n            self.end_mins % 60\n        )\n    }\n}\n\nimpl TimeWindow {\n    /// Parse a `\"HH:MM-HH:MM\"` string (UTC).\n    pub fn parse(s: &str) -> Result<Self, String> {\n        let (start, end) = s\n            .split_once('-')\n            .ok_or_else(|| format!(\"expected HH:MM-HH:MM, got {}\", s))?;\n        Ok(TimeWindow {\n            start_mins: Self::parse_hm(start)?,\n            end_mins: Self::parse_hm(end)?,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn parse_hm(hm: &str) -> Result<u16, String> {\n        let (h, m) = hm\n            .split_once(':')\n            .ok_or_else(|| format!(\"expected HH:MM, got {}\", hm))?;\n        let h: u16 = h.parse().map_err(|_| format!(\"invalid hour: {}\", h))?;\n        let m: u16 = m.parse().map_err(|_| format!(\"invalid minute: {}\", m))?;\n        if h >= 24 || m >= 60 {\n            return Err(format!(\"time out of range: {}\", hm));\n        }\n        Ok(h * 60 + m)\n    }\n\n    /// Does `utc_minutes` (minutes since midnight UTC) fall inside this window?\n    pub fn contains(&self, utc_minutes: u16) -> bool {\n        if self.start_mins <= self.end_mins {\n            utc_minutes >= self.start_mins && utc_minutes < self.end_mins\n        } else {\n            // Wraps midnight, e.g. 22:00-06:00\n            utc_minutes >= self.start_mins || utc_minutes < self.end_mins\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Resharding configuration (plan §13.1 + schedule window guard).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ReshardingConfig {\n    #[serde(default = \"default_true\")]\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    #[serde(default = \"default_backfill_concurrency\")]\n    pub backfill_concurrency: usize,\n    #[serde(default = \"default_backfill_batch_size\")]\n    pub backfill_batch_size: usize,\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub throttle_docs_per_sec: u64,\n    #[serde(default = \"default_true\")]\n    pub verify_before_swap: bool,\n    #[serde(default = \"default_retain_hours\")]\n    pub retain_old_index_hours: u64,\n    /// Allowed schedule windows in `\"HH:MM-HH:MM UTC\"` format.\n    /// Empty means any time is allowed (no restriction).\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub allowed_windows: Vec<String>,\n}\n\nfn default_backfill_concurrency() -> usize {\n    4\n}\nfn default_backfill_batch_size() -> usize {\n    1000\n}\nfn default_true() -> bool {\n    true\n}\nfn default_retain_hours() -> u64 {\n    48\n}\n\nimpl Default for ReshardingConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            enabled: true,\n            backfill_concurrency: default_backfill_concurrency(),\n            backfill_batch_size: default_backfill_batch_size(),\n            throttle_docs_per_sec: 0,\n            verify_before_swap: true,\n            retain_old_index_hours: default_retain_hours(),\n            allowed_windows: Vec::new(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Result of the schedule window guard check.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum WindowGuardResult {\n    /// Current time is inside an allowed window.\n    Allowed { window: String },\n    /// No windows configured — always allowed.\n    NoRestriction,\n    /// Current time is outside all allowed windows.\n    Denied {\n        utc_now: String,\n        allowed: Vec<String>,\n    },\n}\n\n/// Check whether resharding is allowed at the given UTC minute-of-day.\n///\n/// Returns `Allowed` if `utc_minute` falls inside any configured window,\n/// `NoRestriction` if no windows are configured, or `Denied` otherwise.\npub fn check_window(utc_minute: u16, config: &ReshardingConfig) -> WindowGuardResult {\n    if config.allowed_windows.is_empty() {\n        return WindowGuardResult::NoRestriction;\n    }\n\n    for raw in &config.allowed_windows {\n        let window = match TimeWindow::parse(raw) {\n            Ok(w) => w,\n            Err(_) => continue,\n        };\n        if window.contains(utc_minute) {\n            return WindowGuardResult::Allowed {\n                window: raw.clone(),\n            };\n        }\n    }\n\n    WindowGuardResult::Denied {\n        utc_now: format!(\"{:02}:{:02} UTC\", utc_minute / 60, utc_minute % 60),\n        allowed: config.allowed_windows.clone(),\n    }\n}\n\n/// Check the schedule window against the system clock.\npub fn check_window_now(config: &ReshardingConfig) -> WindowGuardResult {\n    let utc_minute = current_utc_minute();\n    check_window(utc_minute, config)\n}\n\nfn current_utc_minute() -> u16 {\n    let duration = SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default();\n    ((duration.as_secs() / 60) % (24 * 60)) as u16\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Resharding load simulation\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Parameters for a single simulation run.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct SimParams {\n    /// Document size in bytes.\n    pub doc_size_bytes: u64,\n    /// Total corpus size in bytes.\n    pub corpus_size_bytes: u64,\n    /// Incoming write rate in documents per second.\n    pub write_rate_dps: u64,\n    /// Number of replica groups.\n    pub replica_groups: u32,\n    /// Replication factor (intra-group copies per shard).\n    pub replication_factor: usize,\n    /// Old shard count (before reshard).\n    pub old_shards: u32,\n    /// New shard count (after reshard).\n    pub new_shards: u32,\n    /// Number of nodes per replica group.\n    pub nodes_per_group: usize,\n    /// Backfill throttle in documents per second (0 = unlimited).\n    pub backfill_throttle_dps: u64,\n}\n\n/// Results from a single simulation run.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct SimResult {\n    pub label: String,\n    pub doc_size_bytes: u64,\n    pub corpus_size_bytes: u64,\n    pub total_docs: u64,\n    pub replica_groups: u32,\n    pub replication_factor: usize,\n    pub old_shards: u32,\n    pub new_shards: u32,\n    pub nodes_per_group: usize,\n    pub write_rate_dps: u64,\n\n    /// Normal steady-state storage across entire cluster (bytes).\n    pub normal_storage_bytes: u64,\n    /// Peak storage during resharding (live + shadow full, bytes).\n    pub peak_storage_bytes: u64,\n    /// Storage amplification factor (peak / normal).\n    pub storage_amplification: f64,\n\n    /// Normal steady-state write rate (actual node writes/sec).\n    pub normal_write_rate: u64,\n    /// Dual-write rate (phase 2 only, no backfill).\n    pub dual_write_rate: u64,\n    /// Peak write rate during backfill + dual-write.\n    pub peak_write_rate: u64,\n    /// Write amplification factor during dual-write only.\n    pub dual_write_amplification: f64,\n    /// Write amplification factor during peak (backfill + dual-write).\n    pub peak_write_amplification: f64,\n\n    /// Backfill duration in seconds (at configured throttle).\n    pub backfill_duration_secs: f64,\n    /// Total bytes written during full reshard operation.\n    pub total_bytes_written: u64,\n\n    /// Per-node peak storage (bytes).\n    pub per_node_peak_storage_bytes: u64,\n    /// Per-node normal storage (bytes).\n    pub per_node_normal_storage_bytes: u64,\n\n    /// Hash distribution stats for old shards.\n    pub old_shard_cv: f64,\n    /// Hash distribution stats for new shards.\n    pub new_shard_cv: f64,\n}\n\n/// Run a resharding load simulation with the given parameters.\n///\n/// This models the six-phase resharding process from plan §13.1 using\n/// the actual routing code to compute shard assignments and estimate\n/// storage/write load. Document keys are synthesized for the corpus size\n/// and routed through the real hash function to measure distribution.\npub fn simulate(params: &SimParams) -> SimResult {\n    let total_docs = params.corpus_size_bytes / params.doc_size_bytes;\n    let rf = params.replication_factor;\n    let rg = params.replica_groups;\n    let nodes_per_group = params.nodes_per_group;\n\n    // Build a synthetic topology for the simulation.\n    let groups: Vec<Group> = (0..rg)\n        .map(|g| {\n            let mut group = Group::new(g);\n            for n in 0..nodes_per_group {\n                group.add_node(NodeId::new(format!(\"node-g{}-n{}\", g, n)));\n            }\n            group\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    // Simulate document distribution across old and new shard counts.\n    // Use the actual router hash to get realistic distribution.\n    let mut old_shard_counts: Vec<u64> = vec![0; params.old_shards as usize];\n    let mut new_shard_counts: Vec<u64> = vec![0; params.new_shards as usize];\n\n    // Track per-node storage for old and new shard assignments.\n    // Each group stores the full corpus; each node in a group stores its\n    // rendezvous-assigned fraction.\n    let total_nodes = (rg as usize) * nodes_per_group;\n    let mut node_storage_old: Vec<u64> = vec![0; total_nodes];\n    let mut node_storage_new: Vec<u64> = vec![0; total_nodes];\n\n    for i in 0..total_docs {\n        let key = format!(\"doc-{}\", i);\n        let old_shard = shard_for_key(&key, params.old_shards);\n        let new_shard = shard_for_key(&key, params.new_shards);\n\n        old_shard_counts[old_shard as usize] += 1;\n        new_shard_counts[new_shard as usize] += 1;\n\n        // For each replica group, assign shard to RF nodes.\n        for (g_idx, group) in groups.iter().enumerate() {\n            let old_targets = assign_shard_in_group(old_shard, group.nodes(), rf);\n            let new_targets = assign_shard_in_group(new_shard, group.nodes(), rf);\n\n            for node_id in &old_targets {\n                let node_idx = g_idx * nodes_per_group\n                    + group.nodes().iter().position(|n| n == node_id).unwrap_or(0);\n                node_storage_old[node_idx] += params.doc_size_bytes;\n            }\n            for node_id in &new_targets {\n                let node_idx = g_idx * nodes_per_group\n                    + group.nodes().iter().position(|n| n == node_id).unwrap_or(0);\n                node_storage_new[node_idx] += params.doc_size_bytes;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Compute distribution coefficients of variation.\n    let old_cv = cv(&old_shard_counts);\n    let new_cv = cv(&new_shard_counts);\n\n    // Normal storage: corpus replicated across RG groups.\n    let normal_storage_bytes = params.corpus_size_bytes * rg as u64;\n    // Peak storage: live + shadow (both fully populated).\n    let peak_storage_bytes = normal_storage_bytes * 2;\n\n    // Per-node storage (max across all nodes).\n    let per_node_normal = node_storage_old.iter().copied().max().unwrap_or(0);\n    let per_node_peak = per_node_normal + node_storage_new.iter().copied().max().unwrap_or(0);\n\n    // Write rates.\n    // Normal: each incoming doc → RF × RG actual node writes.\n    let normal_write_rate = params.write_rate_dps * rf as u64 * rg as u64;\n    // Dual-write: each incoming doc → 2 × (RF × RG) writes (old + new assignment).\n    let dual_write_rate = normal_write_rate * 2;\n    // Backfill: reads all docs, writes each to new assignment → RF × RG writes/doc.\n    // Plus ongoing dual-writes for new incoming docs.\n    let backfill_write_rate = params.backfill_throttle_dps * rf as u64 * rg as u64;\n    let peak_write_rate = dual_write_rate + backfill_write_rate;\n\n    let dual_write_amplification = 2.0;\n    let peak_write_amplification = peak_write_rate as f64 / normal_write_rate as f64;\n\n    // Backfill duration: total docs / throttle rate.\n    let backfill_duration_secs = if params.backfill_throttle_dps > 0 {\n        total_docs as f64 / params.backfill_throttle_dps as f64\n    } else {\n        f64::INFINITY\n    };\n\n    // Total bytes written during reshard:\n    // 1. Dual-write ongoing for the full reshard duration.\n    // 2. Backfill writes of entire corpus.\n    // Approximate: backfill_duration × dual_write_rate + corpus × RF × RG.\n    let total_reshard_write_bytes = if params.backfill_throttle_dps > 0 {\n        let dual_write_bytes =\n            backfill_duration_secs * dual_write_rate as f64 * params.doc_size_bytes as f64;\n        let backfill_bytes = total_docs * rf as u64 * rg as u64 * params.doc_size_bytes;\n        (dual_write_bytes as u64) + backfill_bytes\n    } else {\n        0\n    };\n\n    let storage_amplification = peak_storage_bytes as f64 / normal_storage_bytes as f64;\n\n    SimResult {\n        label: format!(\n            \"{}KB/{}GB/RG{}/RF{}\",\n            params.doc_size_bytes / 1024,\n            params.corpus_size_bytes / (1024 * 1024 * 1024),\n            rg,\n            rf\n        ),\n        doc_size_bytes: params.doc_size_bytes,\n        corpus_size_bytes: params.corpus_size_bytes,\n        total_docs,\n        replica_groups: rg,\n        replication_factor: rf,\n        old_shards: params.old_shards,\n        new_shards: params.new_shards,\n        nodes_per_group,\n        write_rate_dps: params.write_rate_dps,\n\n        normal_storage_bytes,\n        peak_storage_bytes,\n        storage_amplification,\n\n        normal_write_rate,\n        dual_write_rate,\n        peak_write_rate,\n        dual_write_amplification,\n        peak_write_amplification,\n\n        backfill_duration_secs,\n        total_bytes_written: total_reshard_write_bytes,\n\n        per_node_peak_storage_bytes: per_node_peak,\n        per_node_normal_storage_bytes: per_node_normal,\n\n        old_shard_cv: old_cv,\n        new_shard_cv: new_cv,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Coefficient of variation for a distribution.\nfn cv(values: &[u64]) -> f64 {\n    if values.is_empty() {\n        return 0.0;\n    }\n    let n = values.len() as f64;\n    let mean = values.iter().sum::<u64>() as f64 / n;\n    if mean == 0.0 {\n        return 0.0;\n    }\n    let variance = values\n        .iter()\n        .map(|v| (*v as f64 - mean).powi(2))\n        .sum::<f64>()\n        / n;\n    variance.sqrt() / mean\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Tests\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    // ---- TimeWindow parsing and containment ----\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_parse_simple() {\n        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(w.start_mins, 120);\n        assert_eq!(w.end_mins, 360);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_parse_wrap_midnight() {\n        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"22:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(w.start_mins, 1320);\n        assert_eq!(w.end_mins, 360);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_contains_normal() {\n        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n        assert!(w.contains(180)); // 03:00\n        assert!(!w.contains(100)); // 01:40\n        assert!(!w.contains(400)); // 06:40\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_contains_wrap() {\n        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"22:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n        assert!(w.contains(1350)); // 22:30\n        assert!(w.contains(300)); // 05:00\n        assert!(!w.contains(700)); // 11:40\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_boundary_start() {\n        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n        assert!(w.contains(120)); // exactly 02:00\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_boundary_end_exclusive() {\n        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n        assert!(!w.contains(360)); // exactly 06:00 is excluded\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_invalid_format() {\n        assert!(TimeWindow::parse(\"not-a-window\").is_err());\n        assert!(TimeWindow::parse(\"25:00-06:00\").is_err());\n        assert!(TimeWindow::parse(\"02:60-06:00\").is_err());\n    }\n\n    // ---- Window guard ----\n\n    #[test]\n    fn WindowGuardResult::Denied { .. }));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn window_guard_multiple_windows() {\n        let config = ReshardingConfig {\n            allowed_windows: vec![\"02:00-04:00\".into(),  ));\n        // Outside both.\n        assert!(matches!(\n            check_window(720, &config),\n            WindowGuardResult::Denied { .. }\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // ---- Simulation ----\n\n    #[test]\n    fn simulation_storage_always_2x() {\n        // Regardless of parameters, peak storage should be exactly 2× normal.\n        let params = SimParams {\n            doc_size_bytes: };\n        let result = simulate(&params);\n        assert!(\n            (result.dual_write_amplification - 2.0).abs() < 0.01,\n            \"expected 2.0, got {}\",\n            result.dual_write_amplification\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn simulation_low_cv_with_many_docs() {\n        // With enough docs, hash distribution CV should be very low (< 5%).\n        let params = SimParams {\n            doc_size_bytes: 1024,\n            corpus_size_bytes: 1_000_000 * 1024, // 1M docs × 1KB\n            write_rate_dps: 100,\n            replica_groups: 1,\n {\n        let params = SimParams {\n            doc_size_bytes: 1024,\n            corpus_size_bytes: 1024 * 1024,\n            write_rate_dps: 100,\n            replica_groups: 1,\n
