assert_eq!(cv(&[]), 0.0);\n        assert_eq!(cv(&[0, 0, 0]), 0.0);\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Six-phase resharding execution (plan §13.1)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Resharding phase identifiers (matching plan §13.1 steps).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[repr(u8)]\npub enum ReshardPhase {\n    /// No active resharding.\n    Idle = 0,\n    /// Phase 1: Shadow index created.\n    ShadowCreated = 1,\n    /// Phase 2: Dual-hash dual-write active.\n    DualWriteActive = 2,\n    /// Phase 3: Backfill in progress.\n    BackfillInProgress = 3,\n    /// Phase 4: Verification in progress.\n    Verifying = 4,\n    /// Phase 5: Alias swap completed.\n    Swapped = 5,\n    /// Phase 6: Cleanup in progress.\n    CleaningUp = 6,\n    /// Resharding completed successfully.\n    Complete = 7,\n    /// Resharding failed.\n    Failed = 8,\n}\n\nimpl ReshardPhase {\n    /// Human-readable phase name.\n    pub fn name(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::Idle => \"Idle\",\n            Self::ShadowCreated => \"Shadow Created\",\n            Self::DualWriteActive => \"Dual-Write Active\",\n            Self::BackfillInProgress => \"Backfill In Progress\",\n            Self::Verifying => \"Verifying\",\n            Self::Swapped => \"Swapped\",\n            Self::CleaningUp => \"Cleaning Up\",\n            Self::Complete => \"Complete\",\n            Self::Failed => \"Failed\",\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Parse from u8 (for storage).\n    pub fn from_u8(v: u8) -> Option<Self> {\n        match v {\n            0 => Some(Self::Idle),\n            1 => Some(Self::ShadowCreated),\n            2 => Some(Self::DualWriteActive),\n            3 => Some(Self::BackfillInProgress),\n            4 => Some(Self::Verifying),\n            5 => Some(Self::Swapped),\n            6 => Some(Self::CleaningUp),\n            7 => Some(Self::Complete),\n            8 => Some(Self::Failed),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Active resharding operation state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ReshardOperation {\n    /// Unique operation ID.\n    pub id: String,\n    /// Index UID being resharded.\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    /// Old shard count.\n    pub old_shards: u32,\n    /// New shard count.\n    pub target_shards: u32,\n    /// Current phase.\n    pub phase: ReshardPhase,\n    /// Phase started at (UNIX ms).\n    pub phase_started_at: u64,\n    /// Operation created at (UNIX ms).\n    pub created_at: u64,\n    /// Documents backfilled so far.\n    pub documents_backfilled: u64,\n    /// Total documents to backfill (estimated at start).\n    pub total_documents: u64,\n    /// Last error message (if any).\n    pub last_error: Option<String>,\n    /// Shadow index UID.\n    pub shadow_index: String,\n    /// Verification results (populated after phase 4).\n    pub verification_results: Option<VerificationResults>,\n    /// Cleanup retention deadline (UNIX ms).\n    pub cleanup_deadline: Option<u64>,\n}\n\n/// Results from phase 4 verification.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct VerificationResults {\n    /// Live index PK set size.\n    pub live_pk_count: u64,\n    /// Shadow index PK set size.\n    pub shadow_pk_count: u64,\n    /// PKs only in live index.\n    pub live_only_pks: Vec<String>,\n    /// PKs only in shadow index.\n    pub shadow_only_pks: Vec<String>,\n    /// PKs with content hash mismatch.\n    pub mismatched_pks: Vec<String>,\n    /// Whether verification passed.\n    pub passed: bool,\n}\n\nimpl ReshardOperation {\n    /// Create a new resharding operation.\n    pub fn new(index_uid: String, old_shards: u32, target_shards: u32) -> Self {\n        let id = format!(\"reshard-{}-{}\", index_uid, uuid::Uuid::new_v4());\n        let shadow_index = format!(\"{}__reshard_{}\", index_uid, target_shards);\n        let now = millis_now();\n        Self {\n            id,\n            index_uid,\n            old_shards,\n            target_shards,\n            phase: ReshardPhase::ShadowCreated,\n            phase_started_at: now,\n            created_at: now,\n            documents_backfilled: 0,\n            total_documents: 0,\n            last_error: None,\n            shadow_index,\n            verification_results: None,\n            cleanup_deadline: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Transition to the next phase.\n    pub fn advance_phase(&mut self, new_phase: ReshardPhase) {\n        self.phase = new_phase;\n        self.phase_started_at = millis_now();\n    }\n\n    /// Record an error and mark as failed.\n    pub fn fail(&mut self, error: String) {\n        self.last_error = Some(error);\n        self.phase = ReshardPhase::Failed;\n    }\n\n    /// Update backfill progress.\n    pub fn update_backfill_progress(&mut self, backfilled: u64, total: u64) {\n        self.documents_backfilled = backfilled;\n        self.total_documents = total;\n    }\n\n    /// Calculate backfill progress ratio (0.0 to 1.0).\n    pub fn backfill_progress(&self) -> f64 {\n        if self.total_documents == 0 {\n            return 0.0;\n        }\n        (self.documents_backfilled as f64) / (self.total_documents as f64)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the operation is in a terminal state.\n    pub fn is_terminal(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(\n            self.phase,\n            ReshardPhase::Complete | ReshardPhase::Failed\n        )\n    }\n\n    /// Get the shadow index name.\n    pub fn shadow_index_name(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.shadow_index\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get current UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.\nfn millis_now() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_millis() as u64\n}\n\n/// In-memory registry of active resharding operations.\n///\n/// In production, this is persisted to the task store (SQLite/Redis).\n/// This in-memory version is for single-pod development.\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\npub struct ReshardRegistry {\n    operations: HashMap<String, ReshardOperation>,\n    /// Index UID -> active operation ID (at most one per index).\n    index_ops: HashMap<String, String>,\n}\n\nimpl ReshardRegistry {\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self::default()\n    }\n\n    /// Register a new resharding operation.\n    pub fn register(&mut self, op: ReshardOperation) -> Result<(), String> {\n        if let Some(existing_id) = self.index_ops.get(&op.index_uid) {\n            return Err(format!(\n                \"Resharding already in progress for index '{}': {}\",\n                op.index_uid, existing_id\n            ));\n        }\n        self.index_ops.insert(op.index_uid.clone(), op.id.clone());\n        self.operations.insert(op.id.clone(), op);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Get an operation by ID.\n    pub fn get(&self, id: &str) -> Option<&ReshardOperation> {\n        self.operations.get(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get mutable reference for updates.\n    pub fn get_mut(&mut self, id: &str) -> Option<&mut ReshardOperation> {\n        self.operations.get_mut(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the active operation for an index (if any).\n    pub fn get_for_index(&self, index_uid: &str) -> Option<&ReshardOperation> {\n        self.index_ops\n            .get(index_uid)\n            .and_then(|id| self.operations.get(id))\n    }\n\n    /// Update an operation.\n    pub fn update(&mut self, op: ReshardOperation) -> Result<(), String> {\n        if !self.operations.contains_key(&op.id) {\n            return Err(format!(\"Operation '{}' not found\", op.id));\n        }\n        self.operations.insert(op.id.clone(), op);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Complete an operation and remove from active index tracking.\n    pub fn complete(&mut self, id: &str) -> Result<(), String> {\n        let op = self.operations.get(id).ok_or_else(|| format!(\"Operation '{}' not found\", id))?;\n        if !op.is_terminal() {\n            return Err(format!(\"Operation '{}' is not in a terminal state\", id));\n        }\n        self.index_ops.remove(&op.index_uid);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// List all operations.\n    pub fn list(&self) -> Vec<&ReshardOperation> {\n        self.operations.values().collect()\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up completed operations older than the retention period.\n    pub fn prune_completed(&mut self, max_age_ms: u64) {\n        let now = millis_now();\n        let mut to_remove = Vec::new();\n        for (id, op) in &self.operations {\n            if op.is_terminal() && (now.saturating_sub(op.created_at) > max_age_ms) {\n                to_remove.push(id.clone());\n                self.index_ops.remove(&op.index_uid);\n            }\n        }\n        for id in to_remove {\n            self.operations.remove(&id);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests_reshard_execution {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn phase_display_names() {\n        assert_eq!(ReshardPhase::Idle.name(), \"Idle\");\n        assert_eq!(ReshardPhase::BackfillInProgress.name(), \"Backfill In Progress\");\n        assert_eq!(ReshardPhase::Failed.name(), \"Failed\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn phase_roundtrip_u8() {\n        for phase in &[\n            ReshardPhase::Idle,\n            ReshardPhase::ShadowCreated,\n            ReshardPhase::DualWriteActive,\n            ReshardPhase::BackfillInProgress,\n            ReshardPhase::Verifying,\n            ReshardPhase::Swapped,\n            ReshardPhase::CleaningUp,\n            ReshardPhase::Complete,\n            ReshardPhase::Failed,\n        ] {\n            let v = *phase as u8;\n            assert_eq!(ReshardPhase::from_u8(v), Some(*phase));\n        }\n        assert_eq!(ReshardPhase::from_u8(255), None);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn operation_creation() {\n        let op = ReshardOperation::new(\"products\".into(), 64, 128);\n        assert_eq!(op.index_uid, \"products\");\n        assert_eq!(op.old_shards, 64);\n        assert_eq!(op.target_shards, 128);\n        assert_eq!(op.shadow_index, \"products__reshard_128\");\n        assert_eq!(op.phase, ReshardPhase::ShadowCreated);\n        assert!(!op.is_terminal());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn operation_backfill_progress() {\n        let mut op = ReshardOperation::new(\"test\".into(), 16, 32);\n        op.total_documents = 1000;\n        op.documents_backfilled = 500;\n        assert!((op.backfill_progress() - 0.5).abs() < f64::EPSILON);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn operation_terminal_states() {\n        let mut op = ReshardOperation::new(\"test\".into(), 16, 32);\n        assert!(!op.is_terminal());\n        op.phase = ReshardPhase::Complete;\n        assert!(op.is_terminal());\n        op.phase = ReshardPhase::Failed;\n        assert!(op.is_terminal());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn registry_single_op_per_index() {\n        let mut reg = ReshardRegistry::new();\n        let op1 = ReshardOperation::new(\"products\".into(), 64, 128);\n        reg.register(op1).unwrap();\n        let op2 = ReshardOperation::new(\"products\".into(), 128, 256);\n        assert!(reg.register(op2).is_err());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn registry_get_for_index() {\n        let mut reg = ReshardRegistry::new();\n        let op = ReshardOperation::new(\"products\".into(), 64, 128);\n        let id = op.id.clone();\n        reg.register(op).unwrap();\n        let retrieved = reg.get_for_index(\"products\").unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(retrieved.id, id);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn registry_complete_releases_index() {\n        let mut reg = ReshardRegistry::new();\n        let op = ReshardOperation::new(\"products\".into(), 64, 128);\n        let id = op.id.clone();\n        reg.register(op).unwrap();\n        assert!(reg.get_for_index(\"products\").is_some());\n        let op = reg.get_mut(&id).unwrap();\n        op.phase = ReshardPhase::Complete;\n        reg.complete(&id).unwrap();\n        assert!(reg.get_for_index(\"products\").is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn registry_prune_old_completed() {\n        let mut reg = ReshardRegistry::new();\n        let mut op = ReshardOperation::new(\"test\".into(), 16, 32);\n        op.phase = ReshardPhase::Complete;\n        op.created_at = millis_now().saturating_sub(100_000); // 100s ago\n        let id = op.id.clone();\n        reg.register(op).unwrap();\n        reg.prune_completed(50_000); // prune ops older than 50s\n        assert!(reg.get(&id).is_none());\n    }\n}\n","numLines":1022,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1022}}}

\"rankingScore\")]\n103\t    ranking_score: Option<bool>,\n104\t    #[serde(flatten)]\n105\t    rest: Value,\n106\t}\n107\t\n108\timpl std::fmt::Debug for SearchRequestBody {\n109\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n110\t        f.debug_struct(\"SearchRequestBody\")\n111\t            .field(\"q\", &\"[redacted]\")\n112\t            .field(\"offset\", .field(\"ranking_score\", &self.ranking_score)\n117\t            .finish_non_exhaustive()\n118\t    }\n119\t}\n120\t\n121\t/// Search handler with DFS global-IDF preflight (OP#4).\n122\t///\n123\t/// This handler implements the `dfs_query_then_fetch` pattern:\n124\t/// 1. **Preflight phase**: Send term-frequency query to all shards, aggregate\n125\t///    global document frequencies at the coordinator.\n126\t/// 2. **Search phase**: Send the search query with global IDF attached so that\n127\t///    scoring uses corpus-wide statistics instead of per-shard local IDF.\n128\t///\n129\t/// Returns `X-Miroir-Degraded: shards=X,Y,Z` header when any shards are unavailable.\n130\t/// Strips `_miroir_shard` from all hits; strips `_rankingScore` unless client\n131\t/// explicitly requested it.\n132\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\n133\tasync fn search_handler(\n134\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n135\t    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n136\t    headers: HeaderMap,\n137\t    Json(body): Json<SearchRequestBody>,\n138\t) -> Result<Response, StatusCode> {\n139\t    let start = Instant::now();\n140\t    let client_requested_score = body.ranking_score.unwrap_or(false);\n141\t\n142\t    // Extract source IP from X-Forwarded-For or X-Real-IP (trust proxy)\n143\t    let source_ip = headers\n144\t        .get(\"x-forwarded-for\")\n145\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n146\t        .and_then(|s| s.split(',').next())\n147\t        .or_else(|| headers.get(\"x-real-ip\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()))\n148\t        .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n149\t        .trim()\n150\t        .to_string();\n151\t\n152\t    // Check rate limit for search UI (plan §4)\n153\t    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n154\t        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n155\t        Err(e) => {\n156\t            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n157\t            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests per minute\n158\t        }\n159\t    };\n160\t\n161\t    let backend = state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n162\t    if backend == \"redis\" {\n163\t        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n164\t            match redis.check_rate_limit_search_ui(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n165\t                Ok((allowed, _wait_seconds)) => {\n166\t                    if !allowed {\n167\t                        warn!(\n168\t                            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n169\t                            \"search UI rate limited (redis)\"\n170\t                        );\n171\t                        return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n172\t                    }\n173\t                    // Allowed, proceed\n174\t                }\n175\t                Err(e) => {\n176\t                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n177\t                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n178\t                }\n179\t            }\n180\t        }\n181\t    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n182\t        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n183\t            &source_ip,\n184\t            limit,\n185\t            window_seconds * 1000,\n186\t        );\n187\t        if !allowed {\n188\t            warn!(\n189\t                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n190\t                \"search UI rate limited (local backend)\"\n191\t            );\n192\t            return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n193\t        }\n194\t    }\n195\t\n196\t    // Get the scoped key for this index (plan §13.21).\n197\t    // If a scoped key exists, use primary_key (or previous_key during rotation overlap).\n198\t    // If no scoped key exists yet, fall back to node_master_key for initial setup.\n199\t    let search_key = if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n200\t        if let Ok(Some(sk)) = redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(&index) {\n201\t            // Refresh scoped-key beacon so the rotation leader knows this pod is serving\n202\t            // requests for this index at the current generation (plan §13.21).\n203\t            let _ = redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n204\t                &state.pod_id,\n205\t                &index,\n206\t                sk.generation,\n207\t            );\n208\t\n209\t            // Use primary_key; previous_key is the overlap fallback (both are valid in Meilisearch)\n210\t            sk.primary_key\n211\t        } else {\n212\t            // No scoped key yet — fall back to node_master_key for initial setup\n213\t            state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n214\t        }\n215\t    } else {\n216\t        // No Redis store — fall back to node_master_key (single-pod dev mode)\n217\t        state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n218\t    };\n219\t\n220\t    // Use live topology from shared state (updated by health checker)\n221\t    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n222\t    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n223\t        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n224\t        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n225\t        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n226\t        _ => return Err(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR),\n227\t    };\n228\t\n229\t    // Plan scatter using live topology (span for plan construction)\n230\t    let plan = {\n231\t        let _plan_span = tracing::info_span!(\n232\t            \"scatter_plan\",\n233\t            replica_groups = state.config.replica_groups,\n234\t            shards = state.config.shards,\n235\t            rf = state.config.replication_factor,\n236\t        ).entered();\n237\t        plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards)\n238\t    };\n239\t    let node_count = plan.shard_to_node.len() as u64;\n240\t\n241\t    // Record scatter index.clone(),\n247\t        query: body.q,\n248\t        offset: body.offset.unwrap_or(0),\n249\t        limit: body.limit.unwrap_or(20),\n250\t        filter: body.filter,\n251\t        facets: body.facets,\n252\t        ranking_score: client_requested_score,\n253\t        body: body.rest,\n254\t        global_idf: None,\n255\t    };\n256\t\n257\t    // Create node client with the scoped key (or node_master_key as fallback)\n258\t    let http_client = Arc::new(crate::client::HttpClient::new(\n259\t        search_key,\n260\t        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n261\t    ));\n262\t    let client = ProxyNodeClient::new(http_client, state.metrics.clone());\n263\t\n264\t    // Use score-based merge strategy (OP#4: requires global IDF)\n265\t    let strategy = failed\");\n279\t        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n280\t    })?;\n281\t\n282\t    // Drop topology lock before building response\n283\t    drop(topo);\n284\t\n285\t    // Strip internal fields from hits\n286\t    for hit in &mut result.hits {\n287\t        strip_internal_fields(hit, client_requested_score);\n288\t    }\n289\t\n290\t    // Build response body\n291\t    let mut body = serde_json::json!({\n292\t        \"hits\": result.hits,\n293\t        \"estimatedTotalHits\": result.estimated_total_hits,\n294\t        \"processingTimeMs\": result.processing_time_ms,\n295\t serde_json::to_value(facets).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n300\t    }\n301\t\n302\t    // Build response with optional X-Miroir-Degraded header\n303\t    let mut response = Response::builder()\n304\t        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n305\t        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n306\t\n307\t    if result.degraded {\n308\t        state.metrics.inc_scatter_partial_responses();\n309\t    }\n310\t\n311\t    if result.degraded && !result.failed_shards.is_empty() {\n312\t        let mut sorted_shards = result.failed_shards.clone();\n313\t        sorted_shards.sort();\n314\t        let shard_ids = sorted_shards.iter()\n315\t            .map(|id| id.to_string())\n316\t            .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n317\t            .join(\",\");\n318\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", format!(\"shards={}\", shard_ids));\n319\t    } else if result.degraded {\n320\t        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", \"partial\");\n321\t    }\n322\t\n323\t    let response = response\n324\t        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&body).unwrap()))\n325\t        .unwrap();\n326\t\n327\t    // Structured log entry (plan §10 shape)\n328\t    // request_id and pod_id are included from the middleware span via\n329\t    // .with_current_span(true) on the JSON subscriber layer.\n330\t    tracing::info!(\n331\t result.estimated_total_hits,\n336\t        degraded = result.degraded,\n337\t        \"search completed\"\n338\t    );\n339\t\n340\t    Ok(response)\n341\t}\n342\t\n343\t/// Strip `_miroir_shard` from all hits (always).\n344\t/// Strip `_rankingScore` unless the client explicitly requested it.\n345\tpub fn strip_internal_fields(hit: &mut Value, client_requested_score: bool) {\n346\t    if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n347\t        obj.remove(\"_miroir_shard\");\n348\t        if !client_requested_score {\n349\t            obj.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n350\t        }\n351\t    }\n352\t}\n353\t\n354\t#[cfg(test)]\n355\tmod tests {\n356\t    use super::*;\n357\t\n358\t    #[test]\n359\t    fn test_search_request_debug_redaction() {\n360\t        let body = SearchRequestBody axum::Json;\nuse miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy;\nuse miroir_core::merger::ScoreMergeStrategy;\nuse miroir_core::scatter::{\n    dfs_query_then_fetch_search, plan_search_scatter, SearchRequest, NodeClient,\n};\nuse serde::Deserialize;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::Instant;\nuse tracing::{debug, info_span, instrument, warn};\n\nuse crate::routes::admin_endpoints::{AppState, parse_rate_limit};\n\n/// Hash a value for logging (obfuscates sensitive data like IPs).\nfn hash_for_log(value: &str) -> String {\n    use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n    let mut hasher = std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher::new();\n    value.hash(&mut hasher);\n    format!(\"{:016x}\", hasher.finish())\n}\n\n/// Node client implementation using the HTTP client.\n///\n/// Wraps each node call with Prometheus metrics recording for\n/// `miroir_node_request_duration_seconds` and `miroir_node_errors_total`.\npub struct ProxyNodeClient {\n    client: Arc<crate::client::HttpClient>,\n    metrics: crate::middleware::Metrics,\n}\n\nimpl ProxyNodeClient {\n    pub fn new(client: Arc<crate::client::HttpClient>, metrics: crate::middleware::Metrics) -> Self {\n        Self { client, metrics }\n    }\n}\n\n#[allow(async_fn_in_trait)]\nimpl NodeClient for ProxyNodeClient {\n    async fn search_node(\n        &self,\n        node: &miroir_core::topology::NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &SearchRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, miroir_core::scatter::NodeError> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let result = self.client.search_node(node, address, request).await;\n        let elapsed = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n        self.metrics.record_node_request_duration(node.as_str(), \"search\", elapsed);\n        if let Err(ref e) = result {\n            self.metrics.inc_node_errors(node.as_str(), error_label(e));\n        }\n        result\n    }\n\n    async fn preflight_node(\n        &self,\n        node: &miroir_core::topology::NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &miroir_core::scatter::PreflightRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<miroir_core::scatter::PreflightResponse, miroir_core::scatter::NodeError> {\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        let result = self.client.preflight_node(node, address, request).await;\n        let elapsed = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n        self.metrics.record_node_request_duration(node.as_str(), \"preflight\", elapsed);\n        if let Err(ref e) = result {\n            self.metrics.inc_node_errors(node.as_str(), error_label(e));\n        }\n        result\n    }\n}\n\nfn error_label(e: &miroir_core::scatter::NodeError) -> &'static str {\n    match e {\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::NetworkError(_) => \"network\",\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::HttpError { .. } => \"http\",\n        miroir_core::scatter::NodeError::Timeout => \"timeout\",\n    }\n}\n\npub fn router<S>() -> axum::Router<S>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n{\n    axum::Router::new()\n        .route(\"/:index\", axum::routing::post(search_handler))\n}\n\n/// Search request body.\n#[derive(Deserialize)]\nstruct SearchRequestBody {\n    q: Option<String>,\n    offset: Option<usize>,\n    limit: Option<usize>,\n    filter: Option<Value>,\n    facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n    #[serde(rename = \"rankingScore\")]\n    ranking_score: Option<bool>,\n    #[serde(flatten)]\n    rest: Value,\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Debug for SearchRequestBody {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        f.debug_struct(\"SearchRequestBody\")\n            .field(\"q\", &\"[redacted]\")\n            .field(\"offset\", &self.offset)\n            .field(\"limit\", &self.limit)\n            .field(\"filter\", &\"[redacted]\")\n            .field(\"facets\", &self.facets)\n            .field(\"ranking_score\", &self.ranking_score)\n            .finish_non_exhaustive()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Search handler with DFS global-IDF preflight (OP#4).\n///\n/// This handler implements the `dfs_query_then_fetch` pattern:\n/// 1. **Preflight phase**: Send term-frequency query to all shards, aggregate\n///    global document frequencies at the coordinator.\n/// 2. **Search phase**: Send the search query with global IDF attached so that\n///    scoring uses corpus-wide statistics instead of per-shard local IDF.\n///\n/// Returns `X-Miroir-Degraded: shards=X,Y,Z` header when any shards are unavailable.\n/// Strips `_miroir_shard` from all hits; strips `_rankingScore` unless client\n/// explicitly requested it.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %index))]\nasync fn search_handler(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    headers: HeaderMap,\n    Json(body): Json<SearchRequestBody>,\n) -> Result<Response, StatusCode> {\n    let start = Instant::now();\n    let client_requested_score = body.ranking_score.unwrap_or(false);\n\n    // Extract source IP from X-Forwarded-For or X-Real-IP (trust proxy)\n    let source_ip = headers\n        .get(\"x-forwarded-for\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| s.split(',').next())\n        .or_else(|| headers.get(\"x-real-ip\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()))\n        .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n        .trim()\n        .to_string();\n\n    // Check rate limit for search UI (plan §4)\n    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n        Err(e) => {\n            warn!(error = %e, \"invalid search_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config, using default\");\n            (60, 60) // Default: 60 requests per minute\n        }\n    };\n\n    let backend = state.config.search_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n    if backend == \"redis\" {\n        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n            match redis.check_rate_limit_search_ui(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n                Ok((allowed, _wait_seconds)) => {\n                    if !allowed {\n                        warn!(\n                            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                            \"search UI rate limited (redis)\"\n                        );\n                        return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n                    }\n                    // Allowed, proceed\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to check search UI rate limit\");\n                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n        let (allowed, _wait_seconds) = state.local_search_ui_rate_limiter.check(\n            &source_ip,\n            limit,\n            window_seconds * 1000,\n        );\n        if !allowed {\n            warn!(\n                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                \"search UI rate limited (local backend)\"\n            );\n            return Err(StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Get the scoped key for this index (plan §13.21).\n    // If a scoped key exists, use primary_key (or previous_key during rotation overlap).\n    // If no scoped key exists yet, fall back to node_master_key for initial setup.\n    let search_key = if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n        if let Ok(Some(sk)) = redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(&index) {\n            // Refresh scoped-key beacon so the rotation leader knows this pod is serving\n            // requests for this index at the current generation (plan §13.21).\n            let _ = redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n                &state.pod_id,\n                &index,\n                sk.generation,\n            );\n\n            // Use primary_key; previous_key is the overlap fallback (both are valid in Meilisearch)\n            sk.primary_key\n        } else {\n            // No scoped key yet — fall back to node_master_key for initial setup\n            state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        // No Redis store — fall back to node_master_key (single-pod dev mode)\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n    };\n\n    // Use live topology from shared state (updated by health checker)\n    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n        _ => return Err(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR),\n    };\n\n    // Plan scatter using live topology (span for plan construction)\n    let plan = {\n        let _plan_span = tracing::info_span!(\n            \"scatter_plan\",\n            replica_groups = state.config.replica_groups,\n            shards = state.config.shards,\n            rf = state.config.replication_factor,\n        ).entered();\n        plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards)\n    };\n    let node_count = plan.shard_to_node.len() as u64;\n\n    // Record scatter fan-out size before executing\n    state.metrics.record_scatter_fan_out(node_count);\n\n    // Build search request\n    let search_req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: index.clone(),\n        query: body.q,\n        offset: body.offset.unwrap_or(0),\n        limit: body.limit.unwrap_or(20),\n        filter: body.filter,\n        facets: body.facets,\n        ranking_score: client_requested_score,\n        body: body.rest,\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    // Create node client with the scoped key (or node_master_key as fallback)\n    let http_client = Arc::new(crate::client::HttpClient::new(\n        search_key,\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    ));\n    let client = ProxyNodeClient::new(http_client, state.metrics.clone());\n\n    // Use score-based merge strategy (OP#4: requires global IDF)\n    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n\n    // Execute DFS query-then-fetch\n    let mut result = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan,\n        &client,\n        search_req,\n        &topo,\n        policy,\n        &strategy,\n    )\n    .await\n    .map_err(|e| {\n        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"search failed\");\n        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n    })?;\n\n    // Drop topology lock before building response\n    drop(topo);\n\n    // Strip internal fields from hits\n    for hit in &mut result.hits {\n        strip_internal_fields(hit, client_requested_score);\n    }\n\n    // Build response body\n    let mut body = serde_json::json!({\n        \"hits\": result.hits,\n        \"estimatedTotalHits\": result.estimated_total_hits,\n        \"processingTimeMs\": result.processing_time_ms,\n    });\n\n    // Only include facetDistribution if facets were requested\n    if let Some(facets) = &result.facet_distribution {\n        body[\"facetDistribution\"] = serde_json::to_value(facets).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    }\n\n    // Build response with optional X-Miroir-Degraded header\n    let mut response = Response::builder()\n        .status(StatusCode::OK)\n        .header(\"content-type\", \"application/json\");\n\n    if result.degraded {\n        state.metrics.inc_scatter_partial_responses();\n    }\n\n    if result.degraded && !result.failed_shards.is_empty() {\n        let mut sorted_shards = result.failed_shards.clone();\n        sorted_shards.sort();\n        let shard_ids = sorted_shards.iter()\n            .map(|id| id.to_string())\n            .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n            .join(\",\");\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", format!(\"shards={}\", shard_ids));\n    } else if result.degraded {\n        response = response.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", \"partial\");\n    }\n\n    let response = response\n        .body(axum::body::Body::from(serde_json::to_string(&body).unwrap()))\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Structured log entry (plan §10 shape)\n    // request_id and pod_id are included from the middleware span via\n    // .with_current_span(true) on the JSON subscriber layer.\n    tracing::info!(\n        target: \"miroir.search\",\n        index = %index,\n        duration_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,\n        node_count = node_count,\n        estimated_hits = result.estimated_total_hits,\n        degraded = result.degraded,\n        \"search completed\"\n    );\n\n    Ok(response)\n}\n\n/// Strip `_miroir_shard` from all hits (always).\n/// Strip `_rankingScore` unless the client explicitly requested it.\npub fn strip_internal_fields(hit: &mut Value, client_requested_score: bool) {\n    if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n        obj.remove(\"_miroir_shard\");\n        if !client_requested_score {\n            obj.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_request_debug_redaction() {\n        let body = SearchRequestBody \"user@example.com\"})),\n            facets: Some(vec![\"category\".to_string()]),\n            ranking_score: Some(false),\n            rest: serde_json::json!({}),\n        };\n        let    /// Aggregate per-shard preflight responses into global IDF.\n66\t    pub fn from_preflight_responses(responses: &[PreflightResponse]) -> Self {\n67\t        let mut total_docs = 0u64;\n68\t        let mut total_length = 0.0f64;\n69\t        let mut term_df: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n70\t\n71\t        for resp in responses {\n72\t            total_docs += resp.total_docs;\n73\t            total_length += resp.avg_doc_length * resp.total_docs as f64;\n74\t            for (term, stats) in &resp.term_stats {\n75\t                *term_df.entry(term.clone()).or_insert(0) += stats.df;\n76\t            }\n77\t        }\n78\t\n79\t        let avg_doc_length = if total_docs > 0 {\n80\t            total_length / total_docs as f64\n81\t        } else {\n82\t            0.0\n83\t        };\n84\t\n85\t        let n = total_docs as f64;\n86\t        let terms = term_df\n87\t            .into_iter()\n88\t            .map(|(term, df)| {\n89\t                let idf = if df == 0 {\n90\t                    0.0\n91\t                } else {\n92\t                    ((n - df as f64 + 0.5) / (df as f64 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0\n93\t                };\n94\t                (term, GlobalTermStats { df, idf })\n95\t            })\n96\t            .collect();\n97\t\n98\t        Self { total_docs, avg_doc_length, terms }\n99\t    }\n100\t}\n101\t\n102\t// {\n304\t    Timeout,\n305\t    HttpError { status: u16, body: String },\n306\t    NetworkError(String),\n307\t}\n308\t\n309\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n310\tpub struct SearchRequest {\n311\t    pub index_uid: String,\n312\t    pub query: Option<String>,\n313\t    pub offset: usize,\n314\t    pub limit: usize,\n315\t    pub filter: Option<Value>,\n316\t    pub facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n317\t    pub ranking_score: bool,\n318\t    pub body: Value,\n319\t    /// Global IDF data from the preflight phase (OP#4).\n320\t    pub global_idf: Option<GlobalIdf>,\n321\t}\n322\t\n323\timpl SearchRequest {\n324\t    /// Build the request body for sending to a node.\n325\t    ///\n326\t    /// Injects `showRankingScore: true` unconditionally so the merger can global-sort.\n327\t    /// Each node receives `offset + limit` results to ensure the coordinator has enough\n328\t    /// data to apply pagination.\n329\t    pub fn to_node_body(&self) -> Value {\n330\t        let mut body = self.body.clone();\n331\t\n332\t        // Inject showRankingScore: true unconditionally for global sorting\n333\t        body[\"showRankingScore\"] = serde_json::json!(true);\n334\t\n335\t        // Set limit to offset + limit so we get enough results for pagination\n336\t        // (coordinator applies final offset/limit after merging)\n337\t        body[\"limit\"] = serde_json::json!(self.offset + self.limit);\n338\t\n339\t        // Set offset to 0 on individual nodes (coordinator handles offset)\n340\t        body[\"offset\"] = serde_json::json!(0);\n341\t\n342\t        // Ensure query is set\n343\t        if let Some(q) = &self.query {\n344\t            body[\"q\"] = serde_json::json!(q);\n345\t        }\n346\t\n347\t        // Ensure filter is set if provided\n348\t        if let Some(filter) = &self.filter {\n349\t            body[\"filter\"] = filter.clone();\n350\t        }\n351\t\n352\t        // Ensure facets are set if provided\n353\t        if let Some(facets) = &self.facets {\n354\t            body[\"facets\"] = serde_json::json!(facets);\n355\t        }\n356\t\n357\t        body\n358\t    }\n359\t}\n360\t\n361\t#[derive(Debug)]\n362\tpub struct ScatterResult {\n363\t    pub shard_pages: Vec<ShardHitPage>,\n364\t    pub failed_shards: HashMap<u32, NodeError>,\n365\t    pub partial: bool,\n366\t    pub deadline_exceeded: bool,\n367\t}\n368\t\n369\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(query_seq, rf, shard_count))]\n370\tpub fn plan_search_scatter(\n371\t    topology: &Topology,\n372\t    query_seq: u64,\n373\t    rf: usize,\n374\t    shard_count: u32,\n375\t) -> ScatterPlan {\n376\t    let chosen_group = query_group(query_seq, topology.replica_group_count());\n377\t\n378\t    let group = match topology.group(chosen_group) {\n379\t        Some(g) => g,\n380\t        None => {\n381\t            return ScatterPlan {\n382\t                chosen_group, target_shards: Vec::new(),\n383\t                shard_to_node: HashMap::new(), deadline_ms: 0, hedging_eligible: false,\n384\t            };\n385\t        }\n386\t    };\n387\t\n388\t    let _covering = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, query_seq);\n389\t\n390\t    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n391\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n392\t        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n393\t        let selected = replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone();\n394\t        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected);\n395\t    }\n396\t\n397\t    ScatterPlan {\n398\t        chosen_group,\n399\t        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n400\t        shard_to_node,\n401\t        deadline_ms: 5000,\n402\t        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n403\t    }\n404\t}\n405\t\n406\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(node_count))]\n407\tpub async fn execute_scatter<C: NodeClient>(\n408\t    plan: ScatterPlan,\n409\t    client: &C,\n410\t    req: SearchRequest,\n411\t    topology: &Topology,\n412\t    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n413\t) -> Result<ScatterResult> {\n414\t    let mut node_to_shards: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<u32>> = HashMap::new();\n415\t    for (&shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n416\t        if plan.target_shards.contains(&shard_id) {\n417\t            node_to_shards.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default().push(shard_id);\n418\t        }\n419\t    }\n420\t    tracing::Span::current().record(\"node_count\", node_to_shards.len());\n421\t\n422\t    let mut shard_pages = Vec::new();\n423\t    let mut failed_shards = HashMap::new();\n424\t    let mut deadline_exceeded = false;\n425\t\n426\t    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n427\t    for (node_id, shards) in node_to_shards {\n428\t        let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n429\t            Some(n) => n.clone(),\n430\t            None => {\n431\t                for shard_id in shards {\n432\t                    failed_shards.insert(shard_id, NodeError::NetworkError(\"node not in topology\".to_string()));\n433\t                }\n434\t                continue;\n435\t            }\n436\t        };\n437\t        let client_ref = client;\n438\t        let req_clone = req.clone();\n439\t        let node_id_clone = node_id.clone();\n440\t        let shard_count = shards.len();\n441\t        // Create a span for this node's scatter call\n442\t        let span = info_span!(\n443\t            \"scatter_node\",\n444\t            node_id = %node_id_clone,\n445\t            address = %node.address,\n446\t            shard_count = shard_count,\n447\t        );\n448\t        tasks.push(async move {\n449\t            let result = client_ref.search_node(&node_id_clone, &node.address, &req_clone).await;\n450\t            (node_id_clone, shards, result)\n451\t        }.instrument(span));\n452\t    }\n453\t\n454\t    let results = futures_util::future::join_all(tasks).await;\n455\t\n456\t    for (_node_id, shards, result) in results {\n457\t        match result {\n458\t            Ok(body) => {\n459\t                for _shard_id in shards {\n460\t                    shard_pages.push(ShardHitPage { body: body.clone() });\n461\t                }\n462\t            }\n463\t            Err(NodeError::Timeout) => {\n464\t                deadline_exceeded = true;\n465\t                for shard_id in shards { failed_shards.insert(shard_id, NodeError::Timeout); }\n466\t            }\n467\t            Err(e) => {\n468\t                for shard_id in shards { failed_shards.insert(shard_id, e.clone()); }\n469\t            }\n470\t        }\n471\t    }\n472\t\n473\t    let partial = !failed_shards.is_empty();\n474\t\n475\t    match policy {\n476\t        UnavailableShardPolicy::Error => {\n477\t            if !failed_shards.is_empty() {\n478\t                return Err(crate::error::MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"{} shard(s) unavailable\", failed_shards.len())));\n479\t            }\n480\t        }\n481\t        UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial => {}\n482\t        UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback => {\n483\t            // Group-unavailability fallback: try other replica groups for failed shards\n484\t            if !failed_shards.is_empty() {\n485\t                let mut fallback_pages = Vec::new();\n486\t                let mut remaining_failed = HashMap::new();\n487\t\n488\t                for (&shard_id, error) in &failed_shards {\n489\t                    let mut fallback_succeeded = false;\n490\t\n491\t                    // Try each other replica group\n492\t                    for group_id in 0..topology.replica_group_count() {\n493\t                        if group_id == plan.chosen_group {\n494\t                            continue; // Skip the already-tried group\n495\t                        }\n496\t\n497\t                        if let Some(group) = topology.group(group_id) {\n498\t                            let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf());\n499\t                            if replicas.is_empty() {\n500\t                                continue;\n501\t                            }\n502\t\n503\t                            // Try each replica in the fallback group\n504\t                            for node_id in replicas {\n505\t                                if let Some(node) = topology.node(&node_id) {\n506\t                                    match client.search_node(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n507\t                                        Ok(body) => {\n508\t                                            fallback_pages.push(ShardHitPage { body });\n509\t                                            fallback_succeeded = true;\n510\t                                            break;\n511\t                                        }\n512\t                                        Err(_) => {\n513\t                                            continue; // Try next replica\n514\t                                        }\n515\t                                    }\n516\t                                }\n517\t                            }\n518\t\n519\t                            if fallback_succeeded {\n520\t                                break; // Found a working replica\n521\t                            }\n522\t                        }\n523\t                    }\n524\t\n525\t                    if !fallback_succeeded {\n526\t                        remaining_failed.insert(shard_id, error.clone());\n527\t                    }\n528\t                }\n529\t\n530\t                // Merge fallback results\n531\t                shard_pages.extend(fallback_pages);\n532\t\n533\t                // Update failed_shards with only those that truly failed\n534\t                if remaining_failed.is_empty() {\n535\t                    // All shards succeeded via fallback\n536\t                    return Ok(ScatterResult {\n537\t                        shard_pages,\n538\t                        failed_shards: HashMap::new(),\n539\t                        partial: false,\n540\t                        deadline_exceeded,\n541\t                    });\n542\t                } else {\n543\t                    // Some shards still failed\n544\t                    failed_shards = remaining_failed;\n545\t                }\n546\t            }\n547\t        }\n548\t    }\n549\t\n550\t    Ok(ScatterResult { shard_pages, failed_shards, partial, deadline_exceeded })\n551\t}\n552\t\n553\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %req.index_uid))]\n554\tpub async fn scatter_gather_search<C: NodeClient>(\n555\t    plan: ScatterPlan,\n556\t    client: &C,\n557\t    req: SearchRequest,\n558\t    topology: &Topology,\n559\t    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n560\t    strategy: &dyn MergeStrategy,\n561\t) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n562\t    let scatter_result = execute_scatter(plan, client, req.clone(), topology, policy).await?;\n563\t\n564\t    let mut shard_pages = scatter_result.shard_pages;\n565\t    if scatter_result.partial {\n566\t        for shard_id in scatter_result.failed_shards.keys() {\n567\t            shard_pages.push(ShardHitPage {\n568\t                body: serde_json::json!({\"success\": false, \"message\": format!(\"shard {} unavailable\", shard_id)}),\n569\t            });\n570\t        }\n571\t    }\n572\t\n573\t    // Collect failed shard IDs for the X-Miroir-Degraded header\n574\t    let failed_shards: Vec<u32> = scatter_result.failed_shards.keys().copied().collect();\n575\t\n576\t    let merge_input = MergeInput {\n577\t        shard_hits: shard_pages,\n578\t        offset: req.offset,\n579\t        limit: req.limit,\n580\t        client_requested_score: req.ranking_score,\n581\t        facets: req.facets.clone(),\n582\t        failed_shards,\n583\t    };\n584\t\n585\t    // Span for the merge operation\n586\t    let _span = info_span!(\n587\t        \"merge\",\n588\t        shard_count = merge_input.shard_hits.len(),\n589\t        offset = req.offset,\n590\t        limit = req.limit,\n591\t    ).entered();\n592\t\n593\t    strategy.merge(merge_input)\n594\t}\n595\t\n596\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n597\t// OP#4: Global-IDF preflight execution\n598\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n599\t\n600\t/// Extract unique query terms from a search query string.\n601\tpub fn extract_query_terms(query: &Option<String>) -> Vec<String> {\n602\t    match query {\n603\t        Some(q) if !q.is_empty() => {\n604\t            let mut seen = std::collections::HashSet::new();\n605\t            let mut terms = Vec::new();\n606\t            for term in q.split_whitespace() {\n607\t                let lower = term.to_lowercase();\n608\t                if seen.insert(lower.clone()) { terms.push(lower); }\n609\t            }\n610\t            terms\n611\t        }\n612\t        _ => Vec::new(),\n613\t    }\n614\t}\n615\t\n616\t/// Execute the preflight phase: gather term frequencies from all shards.\n617\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(node_count, term_count = req.terms.len()))]\n618\tpub async fn execute_preflight<C: NodeClient>(\n619\t    plan: &ScatterPlan,\n620\t    client: &C,\n621\t    req: &PreflightRequest,\n622\t    topology: &Topology,\n623\t) -> Result<GlobalIdf> {\n624\t    if req.terms.is_empty() {\n625\t        return Ok(GlobalIdf { total_docs: 0, avg_doc_length: 0.0, terms: HashMap::new() });\n626\t    }\n627\t\n628\t    let mut node_to_shards: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<u32>> = HashMap::new();\n629\t    for (&shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n630\t        if plan.target_shards.contains(&shard_id) {\n631\t            node_to_shards.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default().push(shard_id);\n632\t        }\n633\t    }\n634\t\n635\t    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n636\t    for (node_id, _) in node_to_shards {\n637\t        let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n638\t            Some(n) => n.clone(),\n639\t            None => continue,\n640\t        };\n641\t        let client_ref = client;\n642\t        let req_clone = req.clone();\n643\t        let nid = node_id.clone();\n644\t        tasks.push(async move { client_ref.preflight_node(&nid, &node.address, &req_clone).await });\n645\t    }\n646\t\n647\t    let results = futures_util::future::join_all(tasks).await;\n648\t    let responses: Vec<PreflightResponse> = results.into_iter().filter_map(|r| r.ok()).collect();\n649\t    Ok(GlobalIdf::from_preflight_responses(&responses))\n650\t}\n651\t\n652\t/// Execute a full dfs_query_then_fetch search (OP#4 global-IDF preflight).\n653\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %req.index_uid))]\n654\tpub async fn dfs_query_then_fetch_search<C: NodeClient>(\n655\t    plan: ScatterPlan,\n656\t    client: &C,\n657\t    req: SearchRequest,\n658\t    topology: &Topology,\n659\t    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n660\t    strategy: &dyn MergeStrategy,\n661\t) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n662\t    let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n663\t        index_uid: req.index_uid.clone(),\n664\t        terms: extract_query_terms(&req.query),\n665\t        filter: req.filter.clone(),\n666\t    };\n667\t    let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, client, &preflight_req, topology).await?;\n668\t    let mut search_req = req;\n669\t    search_req.global_idf = Some(global_idf);\n670\t    scatter_gather_search(plan, client, search_req, topology, policy, strategy).await\n671\t}\n672\t\n673\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n674\t// Mock client\n675\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n676\t\n677\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n678\tpub struct MockNodeClient {\n679\t    pub responses: HashMap<NodeId, Value>,\n680\t    pub preflight_responses: HashMap<NodeId, PreflightResponse>,\n681\t    pub errors: HashMap<NodeId, NodeError>,\n682\t    pub delay_ms: u64,\n683\t}\n684\t\n685\timpl NodeClient for MockNodeClient {\n686\t    async fn search_node(\n687\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &SearchRequest,\n688\t    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, NodeError> {\n689\t        let _ = self.delay_ms;\n690\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n691\t        Ok(self.responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n692\t            serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0, \"processingTimeMs\": 0, \"facetDistribution\": {}})\n693\t        }))\n694\t    }\n695\t\n696\t    async fn preflight_node(\n697\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &PreflightRequest,\n698\t    ) -> std::result::Result<PreflightResponse, NodeError> {\n699\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n700\t        Ok(self.preflight_responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n701\t            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 1000, avg_doc_length: 50.0, term_stats: HashMap::new() }\n702\t        }))\n703\t    }\n704\t\n705\t    async fn write_documents(\n706\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &WriteRequest,\n707\t    ) -> std::result::Result<WriteResponse, NodeError> {\n708\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n709\t        Ok(WriteResponse {\n710\t            success: true,\n711\t            task_uid: Some(1),\n712\t            message: None,\n713\t            code: None,\n714\t            error_type: None,\n715\t        })\n716\t    }\n717\t\n718\t    async fn delete_documents(\n719\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &DeleteByIdsRequest,\n720\t    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n721\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n722\t        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n723\t            success: true,\n724\t            task_uid: Some(1),\n725\t            message: None,\n726\t            code: None,\n727\t            error_type: None,\n728\t        })\n729\t    }\n730\t\n731\t    async fn delete_documents_by_filter(\n732\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &DeleteByFilterRequest,\n733\t    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n734\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n735\t        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n736\t            success: true,\n737\t            task_uid: Some(1),\n738\t            message: None,\n739\t            code: None,\n740\t            error_type: None,\n741\t        })\n742\t    }\n743\t\n744\t    async fn fetch_documents(\n745\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, request: &FetchDocumentsRequest,\n746\t    ) -> std::result::Result<FetchDocumentsResponse, NodeError> {\n747\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n748\t        Ok(FetchDocumentsResponse {\n749\t            results: Vec::new(),\n750\t            limit: request.limit,\n751\t            offset: request.offset,\n752\t            total: 0,\n753\t        })\n754\t    }\n755\t\n756\t    fn get_task_status(\n757\t        &self,\n758\t        node: &NodeId,\n759\t        _address: &str,\n760\t        _request: &TaskStatusRequest,\n761\t    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<TaskStatusResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n762\t        let node = node.clone();\n763\t        let task_uid = _request.task_uid;\n764\t        let error = self.errors.get(&node).cloned();\n765\t        async move {\n766\t            if let Some(err) = error {\n767\t                return Err(err);\n768\t            }\n769\t            Ok(TaskStatusResponse {\n770\t                task_uid,\n771\t                status: \"succeeded\".to_string(),\n772\t                error: None,\n773\t                error_type: None,\n774\t            })\n775\t        }\n776\t    }\n777\t}\n778\t\n779\t#[cfg(test)]\n780\tmod tests {\n781\t    use super::*;\n782\t    use crate::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n783\t\n784\t    fn make_test_topology() }\n925\t\n926\t    #[tokio::test]\n927\t    async fn test_execute_preflight() {\n928\t        let topo = make_test_topology();\n929\t        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64);\n930\t        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n931\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n932\t            total_docs: 30000, avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n933\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 3000 })]),\n934\t        });\n935\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), 9500);\n947\t    }\n948\t\n949\t    #[tokio::test]\n950\t    async fn test_dfs_query_then_fetch() {\n951\t        let topo = make_test_topology();\n952\t        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64);\n953\t        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n954\t        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [{\"id\": \"a\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.9}], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1, \"processingTimeMs\": 5}));\n955\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n956\t            total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n957\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 500 })]),\n958\t        });\n959\t        let s = crate::merger::RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n960\t        let r = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(plan, &c, make_req(), &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &s).await.unwrap();\n961\t        assert!(!r.degraded);\n962\t        assert!(!r.hits.is_empty());\n963\t    }\n964\t\n965\t    /// Integration test for dfs_query_then_fetch with severely skewed shard distribution.\n966\t    ///\n967\t    /// This test simulates the scenario described in miroir-yio:\n968\t    /// - Shard 0: Normal (1,000 docs, term df = 100)\n969\t    /// - Shard 1: 100x normal (100,000 docs, term df = 10,000)\n970\t    /// - Shard 2: Near-empty (10 docs, term df = 1)\n971\t    ///\n972\t    /// Without global IDF preflight, each shard would compute different local IDF values:\n973\t    /// - Shard 0: IDF ≈ log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n974\t    /// - Shard 1: IDF ≈ log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n975\t    /// - Shard 2: IDF ≈ log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.8\n976\t    ///\n977\t    /// With global IDF preflight, all shards use the same IDF:\n978\t    /// - Global: N = 101,010, df = 10,101\n979\t    /// - IDF ≈ log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n980\t    ///\n981\t    /// This ensures scores are comparable across shards, enabling correct score-based merge.\n982\t    #[tokio::test]\n983\t    async fn test_dfs_skewed_shards_global_idf_aggregation() {\n984\t        let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1);\n985\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n986\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n987\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n988\t\n989\t        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3);\n990\t\n991\t        // Simulate severely skewed shard distribution\n992\t        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n993\t\n994\t        // Shard 0: Normal distribution\n995\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n996\t            total_docs: 1000,\n997\t            avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n998\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n999\t                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 100 }),\n1000\t                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 80 }),\n1001\t            ]),\n1002\t        });\n1003\t\n1004\t        // Shard 1: 100x normal (severely skewed)\n1005\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1006\t            total_docs: (10101.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1041\t        let actual_idf_machine = global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().idf;\n1042\t        assert!((actual_idf_machine - expected_idf_machine).abs() < + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1046\t        let actual_idf_learning = global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().idf;\n1047\t        assert!((actual_idf_learning - expected_idf_learning).abs() <
