Scatter orchestration: fan-out logic and covering set builder.\n\nuse crate::config::UnavailableShardPolicy;\nuse tracing::{instrument, info_span, Instrument};\nuse crate::merger::{MergeInput, MergedSearchResult, MergeStrategy, ShardHitPage};\nuse crate::router::{covering_set, query_group};\nuse crate::topology::{NodeId, Topology};\nuse crate::Result;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\n\n/// Scatter plan: the exact shard→node mapping for a search query.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ScatterPlan {\n    pub chosen_group: u32,\n    pub target_shards: Vec<u32>,\n    pub shard_to_node: HashMap<u32, NodeId>,\n    pub deadline_ms: u32,\n    pub hedging_eligible: bool,\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// §15 OP#4: Global-IDF preflight (dfs_query_then_fetch pattern)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Per-term document frequency from a single shard.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct TermStats {\n    pub df: u64,\n}\n\n/// Preflight request: gather term-frequency statistics from a shard.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct PreflightRequest {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub terms: Vec<String>,\n    pub filter: Option<Value>,\n}\n\n/// Response from a shard's preflight query.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct PreflightResponse {\n    pub total_docs: u64,\n    pub avg_doc_length: f64,\n    pub term_stats: HashMap<String, TermStats>,\n}\n\n/// Aggregated global term statistics after coordinator aggregation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct GlobalTermStats {\n    pub df: u64,\n    pub idf: f64,\n}\n\n/// Aggregated global IDF data computed at the coordinator.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct GlobalIdf {\n    pub total_docs: u64,\n    pub avg_doc_length: f64,\n    pub terms: HashMap<String, GlobalTermStats>,\n}\n\nimpl GlobalIdf {\n    /// Aggregate per-shard preflight responses into global IDF.\n    pub fn from_preflight_responses(responses: &[PreflightResponse]) -> Self {\n        let mut total_docs = 0u64;\n        let mut total_length = 0.0f64;\n        let mut term_df: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n        for resp in responses {\n            total_docs += resp.total_docs;\n            total_length += resp.avg_doc_length * resp.total_docs as f64;\n            for (term, stats) in &resp.term_stats {\n                *term_df.entry(term.clone()).or_insert(0) += stats.df;\n            }\n        }\n\n        let avg_doc_length = if total_docs > 0 {\n            total_length / total_docs as f64\n        } else {\n            0.0\n        };\n\n        let n = total_docs as f64;\n        let terms = term_df\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|(term, df)| {\n                let idf = if df == 0 {\n                    0.0\n                } else {\n                    ((n - df as f64 + 0.5) / (df as f64 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0\n                };\n                (term, GlobalTermStats { df, idf })\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        Self { total_docs, avg_doc_length, terms }\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// NodeClient trait\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Write path: document operations (P2.2)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Request to add/replace documents on a node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct WriteRequest {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub documents: Vec<Value>,\n    pub primary_key: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Response from a single node's document write operation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct WriteResponse {\n    pub success: bool,\n    pub task_uid: Option<u64>,\n    pub message: Option<String>,\n    pub code: Option<String>,\n    pub error_type: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Request to delete documents by IDs.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct DeleteByIdsRequest {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub ids: Vec<String>,\n}\n\n/// Request to delete all documents matching a filter.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct DeleteByFilterRequest {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub filter: Value,\n}\n\n/// Response from a delete operation.\npub type DeleteResponse = WriteResponse;\n\n/// Request to fetch documents with a filter (used for shard migration).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct FetchDocumentsRequest {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub filter: Value,\n    pub limit: u32,\n    pub offset: u32,\n}\n\n/// Response from a fetch documents operation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct FetchDocumentsResponse {\n    pub results: Vec<Value>,\n    pub limit: u32,\n    pub offset: u32,\n    pub total: u64,\n}\n\n/// Request to get task status from a node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct TaskStatusRequest {\n    /// The task UID to query\n    pub task_uid: u64,\n}\n\n/// Response from a single node's task status query.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct TaskStatusResponse {\n    /// The task UID\n    pub task_uid: u64,\n    /// Current task status\n    pub status: String,\n    /// Error message if failed\n    pub error: Option<String>,\n    /// Error type if failed\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    pub error_type: Option<String>,\n}\n\nimpl TaskStatusResponse {\n    /// Convert Meilisearch status string to NodeTaskStatus.\n    pub fn to_node_status(&self) -> crate::task::NodeTaskStatus {\n        match self.status.as_str() {\n            \"enqueued\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n            \"processing\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Processing,\n            \"succeeded\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded,\n            \"failed\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Failed,\n            _ => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// NodeClient trait\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// HTTP client for communicating with a Meilisearch node.\n#[allow(async_fn_in_trait)]\npub trait NodeClient: Send + Sync {\n    async fn search_node(\n        &self,\n        node: &NodeId,\n        address: &str,\n        request: &SearchRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, NodeError>;\n\n    /// Execute a preflight request (OP#4 global-IDF phase).\n    async fn preflight_node(\n        &self,\n        _node: &NodeId,\n        _address: &str,\n        _request: &PreflightRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<PreflightResponse, NodeError> {\n        Ok(PreflightResponse { total_docs: 0, avg_doc_length: 0.0, term_stats: HashMap::new() })\n    }\n\n    /// Write documents to a node (add/replace).\n    async fn write_documents(\n        &self,\n        _node: &NodeId,\n        _address: &str,\n        _request: &WriteRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<WriteResponse, NodeError> {\n        Ok(WriteResponse {\n            success: false,\n            task_uid: None,\n            message: Some(\"not implemented\".to_string()),\n            code: None,\n            error_type: None,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Get task status from a node.\n    fn get_task_status(\n        &self,\n        _node: &NodeId,\n        _address: &str,\n        _request: &TaskStatusRequest,\n    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<TaskStatusResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n        async move {\n            Ok(TaskStatusResponse {\n                task_uid: _request.task_uid,\n                status: \"succeeded\".to_string(),\n                error: None,\n                error_type: None,\n            })\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Delete documents by IDs from a node.\n    async fn delete_documents(\n        &self,\n        _node: &NodeId,\n        _address: &str,\n        _request: &DeleteByIdsRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n            success: false,\n            task_uid: None,\n            message: Some(\"not implemented\".to_string()),\n            code: None,\n            error_type: None,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Delete all documents matching a filter from a node.\n    async fn delete_documents_by_filter(\n        &self,\n        _node: &NodeId,\n        _address: &str,\n        _request: &DeleteByFilterRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n            success: false,\n            task_uid: None,\n            message: Some(\"not implemented\".to_string()),\n            code: None,\n            error_type: None,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Fetch documents with a filter from a node (used for shard migration).\n    async fn fetch_documents(\n        &self,\n        _node: &NodeId,\n        _address: &str,\n        _request: &FetchDocumentsRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<FetchDocumentsResponse, NodeError> {\n        Ok(FetchDocumentsResponse {\n            results: Vec::new(),\n            limit: _request.limit,\n            offset: _request.offset,\n            total: 0,\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub enum NodeError {\n    Timeout,\n    HttpError { status: u16, body: String },\n    NetworkError(String),\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct SearchRequest {\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub query: Option<String>,\n    pub offset: usize,\n    pub limit: usize,\n    pub filter: Option<Value>,\n    pub facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n    pub ranking_score: bool,\n    pub body: Value,\n    /// Global IDF data from the preflight phase (OP#4).\n    pub global_idf: Option<GlobalIdf>,\n}\n\nimpl SearchRequest {\n    /// Build the request body for sending to a node.\n    ///\n    /// Injects `showRankingScore: true` unconditionally so the merger can global-sort.\n    /// Each node receives `offset + limit` results to ensure the coordinator has enough\n    /// data to apply pagination.\n    pub fn to_node_body(&self) -> Value {\n        let mut body = self.body.clone();\n\n        // Inject showRankingScore: true unconditionally for global sorting\n        body[\"showRankingScore\"] = serde_json::json!(true);\n\n        // Set limit to offset + limit so we get enough results for pagination\n        // (coordinator applies final offset/limit after merging)\n        body[\"limit\"] = serde_json::json!(self.offset + self.limit);\n\n        // Set offset to 0 on individual nodes (coordinator handles offset)\n        body[\"offset\"] = serde_json::json!(0);\n\n        // Ensure query is set\n        if let Some(q) = &self.query {\n            body[\"q\"] = serde_json::json!(q);\n        }\n\n        // Ensure filter is set if provided\n        if let Some(filter) = &self.filter {\n            body[\"filter\"] = filter.clone();\n        }\n\n        // Ensure facets are set if provided\n        if let Some(facets) = &self.facets {\n            body[\"facets\"] = serde_json::json!(facets);\n        }\n\n        body\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub struct ScatterResult {\n    pub shard_pages: Vec<ShardHitPage>,\n    pub failed_shards: HashMap<u32, NodeError>,\n    pub partial: bool,\n    pub deadline_exceeded: bool,\n}\n\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(query_seq, rf, shard_count))]\npub fn plan_search_scatter(\n    topology: &Topology,\n    query_seq: u64,\n    rf: usize,\n    shard_count: u32,\n) -> ScatterPlan {\n    let chosen_group = query_group(query_seq, topology.replica_group_count());\n\n    let group = match topology.group(chosen_group) {\n        Some(g) => g,\n        None => {\n            return ScatterPlan {\n                chosen_group, target_shards: Vec::new(),\n                shard_to_node: HashMap::new(), deadline_ms: 0, hedging_eligible: false,\n            };\n        }\n    };\n\n    let _covering = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, query_seq);\n\n    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n        let selected = replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone();\n        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected);\n    }\n\n    ScatterPlan {\n        chosen_group,\n        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n        shard_to_node,\n        deadline_ms: 5000,\n        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n    }\n}\n\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(node_count))]\npub async fn execute_scatter<C: NodeClient>(\n    plan: ScatterPlan,\n    client: &C,\n    req: SearchRequest,\n    topology: &Topology,\n    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n) -> Result<ScatterResult> {\n    let mut node_to_shards: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<u32>> = HashMap::new();\n    for (&shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n        if plan.target_shards.contains(&shard_id) {\n            node_to_shards.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default().push(shard_id);\n        }\n    }\n    tracing::Span::current().record(\"node_count\", node_to_shards.len());\n\n    let mut shard_pages = Vec::new();\n    let mut failed_shards = HashMap::new();\n    let mut deadline_exceeded = false;\n\n    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n    for (node_id, shards) in node_to_shards {\n        let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n            Some(n) => n.clone(),\n            None => {\n                for shard_id in shards {\n                    failed_shards.insert(shard_id, NodeError::NetworkError(\"node not in topology\".to_string()));\n                }\n                continue;\n            }\n        };\n        let client_ref = client;\n        let req_clone = req.clone();\n        let node_id_clone = node_id.clone();\n        let shard_count = shards.len();\n        // Create a span for this node's scatter call\n        let span = info_span!(\n            \"scatter_node\",\n            node_id = %node_id_clone,\n            address = %node.address,\n            shard_count = shard_count,\n        );\n        tasks.push(async move {\n            let result = client_ref.search_node(&node_id_clone, &node.address, &req_clone).await;\n            (node_id_clone, shards, result)\n        }.instrument(span));\n    }\n\n    let results = futures_util::future::join_all(tasks).await;\n\n    for (_node_id, shards, result) in results {\n        match result {\n            Ok(body) => {\n                for _shard_id in shards {\n                    shard_pages.push(ShardHitPage { body: body.clone() });\n                }\n            }\n            Err(NodeError::Timeout) => {\n                deadline_exceeded = true;\n                for shard_id in shards { failed_shards.insert(shard_id, NodeError::Timeout); }\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                for shard_id in shards { failed_shards.insert(shard_id, e.clone()); }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    let partial = !failed_shards.is_empty();\n\n    match policy {\n        UnavailableShardPolicy::Error => {\n            if !failed_shards.is_empty() {\n                return Err(crate::error::MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"{} shard(s) unavailable\", failed_shards.len())));\n            }\n        }\n        UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial => {}\n        UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback => {\n            // Group-unavailability fallback: try other replica groups for failed shards\n            if !failed_shards.is_empty() {\n                let mut fallback_pages = Vec::new();\n                let mut remaining_failed = HashMap::new();\n\n                for (&shard_id, error) in &failed_shards {\n                    let mut fallback_succeeded = false;\n\n                    // Try each other replica group\n                    for group_id in 0..topology.replica_group_count() {\n                        if group_id == plan.chosen_group {\n                            continue; // Skip the already-tried group\n                        }\n\n                        if let Some(group) = topology.group(group_id) {\n                            let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf());\n                            if replicas.is_empty() {\n                                continue;\n                            }\n\n                            // Try each replica in the fallback group\n                            for node_id in replicas {\n                                if let Some(node) = topology.node(&node_id) {\n                                    match client.search_node(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n                                        Ok(body) => {\n                                            fallback_pages.push(ShardHitPage { body });\n                                            fallback_succeeded = true;\n                                            break;\n                                        }\n                                        Err(_) => {\n                                            continue; // Try next replica\n                                        }\n                                    }\n                                }\n                            }\n\n                            if fallback_succeeded {\n                                break; // Found a working replica\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n\n                    if !fallback_succeeded {\n                        remaining_failed.insert(shard_id, error.clone());\n                    }\n                }\n\n                // Merge fallback results\n                shard_pages.extend(fallback_pages);\n\n                // Update failed_shards with only those that truly failed\n                if remaining_failed.is_empty() {\n                    // All shards succeeded via fallback\n                    return Ok(ScatterResult {\n                        shard_pages,\n                        failed_shards: HashMap::new(),\n                        partial: false,\n                        deadline_exceeded,\n                    });\n                } else {\n                    // Some shards still failed\n                    failed_shards = remaining_failed;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(ScatterResult { shard_pages, failed_shards, partial, deadline_exceeded })\n}\n\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %req.index_uid))]\npub async fn scatter_gather_search<C: NodeClient>(\n    plan: ScatterPlan,\n    client: &C,\n    req: SearchRequest,\n    topology: &Topology,\n    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n    strategy: &dyn MergeStrategy,\n) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n    let scatter_result = execute_scatter(plan, client, req.clone(), topology, policy).await?;\n\n    let mut shard_pages = scatter_result.shard_pages;\n    if scatter_result.partial {\n        for shard_id in scatter_result.failed_shards.keys() {\n            shard_pages.push(ShardHitPage {\n                body: serde_json::json!({\"success\": false, \"message\": format!(\"shard {} unavailable\", shard_id)}),\n            });\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Collect failed shard IDs for the X-Miroir-Degraded header\n    let failed_shards: Vec<u32> = scatter_result.failed_shards.keys().copied().collect();\n\n    let merge_input = MergeInput {\n        shard_hits: shard_pages,\n        offset: req.offset,\n        limit: req.limit,\n        client_requested_score: req.ranking_score,\n        facets: req.facets.clone(),\n        failed_shards,\n    };\n\n    // Span for the merge operation\n    let _span = info_span!(\n        \"merge\",\n        shard_count = merge_input.shard_hits.len(),\n        offset = req.offset,\n        limit = req.limit,\n    ).entered();\n\n    strategy.merge(merge_input)\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// OP#4: Global-IDF preflight execution\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Extract unique query terms from a search query string.\npub fn extract_query_terms(query: &Option<String>) -> Vec<String> {\n    match query {\n        Some(q) if !q.is_empty() => {\n            let mut seen = std::collections::HashSet::new();\n            let mut terms = Vec::new();\n            for term in q.split_whitespace() {\n                let lower = term.to_lowercase();\n                if seen.insert(lower.clone()) { terms.push(lower); }\n            }\n            terms\n        }\n        _ => Vec::new(),\n    }\n}\n\n/// Execute the preflight phase: gather term frequencies from all shards.\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(node_count, term_count = req.terms.len()))]\npub async fn execute_preflight<C: NodeClient>(\n    plan: &ScatterPlan,\n    client: &C,\n    req: &PreflightRequest,\n    topology: &Topology,\n) -> Result<GlobalIdf> {\n    if req.terms.is_empty() {\n        return Ok(GlobalIdf { total_docs: 0, avg_doc_length: 0.0, terms: HashMap::new() });\n    }\n\n    let mut node_to_shards: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<u32>> = HashMap::new();\n    for (&shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n        if plan.target_shards.contains(&shard_id) {\n            node_to_shards.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default().push(shard_id);\n        }\n    }\n\n    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n    for (node_id, _) in node_to_shards {\n        let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n            Some(n) => n.clone(),\n            None => continue,\n        };\n        let client_ref = client;\n        let req_clone = req.clone();\n        let nid = node_id.clone();\n        tasks.push(async move { client_ref.preflight_node(&nid, &node.address, &req_clone).await });\n    }\n\n    let results = futures_util::future::join_all(tasks).await;\n    let responses: Vec<PreflightResponse> = results.into_iter().filter_map(|r| r.ok()).collect();\n    Ok(GlobalIdf::from_preflight_responses(&responses))\n}\n\n/// Execute a full dfs_query_then_fetch search (OP#4 global-IDF preflight).\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %req.index_uid))]\npub async fn dfs_query_then_fetch_search<C: NodeClient>(\n    plan: ScatterPlan,\n    client: &C,\n    req: SearchRequest,\n    topology: &Topology,\n    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n    strategy: &dyn MergeStrategy,\n) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n    let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n        index_uid: req.index_uid.clone(),\n        terms: extract_query_terms(&req.query),\n        filter: req.filter.clone(),\n    };\n    let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, client, &preflight_req, topology).await?;\n    let mut search_req = req;\n    search_req.global_idf = Some(global_idf);\n    scatter_gather_search(plan, client, search_req, topology, policy, strategy).await\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Mock client\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct MockNodeClient {\n    pub responses: HashMap<NodeId, Value>,\n    pub preflight_responses: HashMap<NodeId, PreflightResponse>,\n    pub errors: HashMap<NodeId, NodeError>,\n    pub delay_ms: u64,\n}\n\nimpl NodeClient for MockNodeClient {\n    async fn search_node(\n        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &SearchRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, NodeError> {\n        let _ = self.delay_ms;\n        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n        Ok(self.responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n            serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0, \"processingTimeMs\": 0, \"facetDistribution\": {}})\n        }))\n    }\n\n    async fn preflight_node(\n        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &PreflightRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<PreflightResponse, NodeError> {\n        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n        Ok(self.preflight_responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 1000, avg_doc_length: 50.0, term_stats: HashMap::new() }\n        }))\n    }\n\n    async fn write_documents(\n        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &WriteRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<WriteResponse, NodeError> {\n        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n        Ok(WriteResponse {\n            success: true,\n            task_uid: Some(1),\n            message: None,\n            code: None,\n            error_type: None,\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn delete_documents(\n        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &DeleteByIdsRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n            success: true,\n            task_uid: Some(1),\n            message: None,\n            code: None,\n            error_type: None,\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn delete_documents_by_filter(\n        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &DeleteByFilterRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n            success: true,\n            task_uid: Some(1),\n            message: None,\n            code: None,\n            error_type: None,\n        })\n    }\n\n    async fn fetch_documents(\n        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, request: &FetchDocumentsRequest,\n    ) -> std::result::Result<FetchDocumentsResponse, NodeError> {\n        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n        Ok(FetchDocumentsResponse {\n            results: Vec::new(),\n            limit: request.limit,\n            offset: request.offset,\n            total: 0,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn get_task_status(\n        &self,\n        node: &NodeId,\n        _address: &str,\n        _request: &TaskStatusRequest,\n    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<TaskStatusResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n        let node = node.clone();\n        let task_uid = _request.task_uid;\n        let error = self.errors.get(&node).cloned();\n        async move {\n            if let Some(err) = error {\n                return Err(err);\n            }\n            Ok(TaskStatusResponse {\n                task_uid,\n                status: \"succeeded\".to_string(),\n                error: None,\n                error_type: None,\n            })\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n\n    fn make_test_topology() -> Topology {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(64, 2, 2);\n        for i in 0u32..6 {\n            let rg = if i < 3 { 0 } else { 1 };\n            let mut node = Node::new(NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")), format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"), rg);\n            node.status = crate::topology::NodeStatus::Active;\n            topo.add_node(node);\n        }\n        topo\n    }\n\n    fn make_req() -> SearchRequest {\n        SearchRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(), query: Some(\"test\".into()),\n            offset: 0, limit: 10, filter: None, facets: None,\n            ranking_score: false, body: serde_json::json!({}), global_idf: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_plan_pure_function() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64);\n        assert_eq!(plan.chosen_group, 0);\n        assert_eq!(plan.target_shards.len(), 64);\n        assert!(plan.hedging_eligible);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_plan_group_rotation() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        assert_eq!(plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64).chosen_group, 0);\n        assert_eq!(plan_search_scatter(&topo, 1, 2, 64).chosen_group, 1);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_plan_shard_mapping() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64);\n        for s in 0..64 { assert!(plan.shard_to_node.contains_key(&s)); }\n        let g0 = topo.group(0).unwrap();\n        for (_, nid) in &plan.shard_to_node { assert!(g0.nodes().contains(nid)); }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_plan_hedging() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(64, 1, 1);\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"n0\".into()), \"http://n0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        assert!(!plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 64).hedging_eligible);\n        assert!(plan_search_scatter(&make_test_topology(), 0, 2, 64).hedging_eligible);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_scatter_mock() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64);\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [{\"id\": \"doc1\"}], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1, \"processingTimeMs\": 5}));\n        let r = execute_scatter(plan, &c, make_req(), &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial).await.unwrap();\n        assert!(!r.partial);\n        assert_eq!(r.shard_pages.len(), 64);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_scatter_partial() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64);\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n        let r = execute_scatter(plan, &c, make_req(), &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial).await.unwrap();\n        assert!(r.partial);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_scatter_error_policy() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64);\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n        assert!(execute_scatter(plan, &c, make_req(), &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Error).await.is_err());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_plan_invalid_group() {\n        assert!(plan_search_scatter(&Topology::new(64, 0, 1), 0, 1, 64).shard_to_node.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_scatter_node_not_in_topo() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64);\n        let r = execute_scatter(plan, &MockNodeClient::default(), make_req(), &Topology::new(64, 2, 2), UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial).await.unwrap();\n        assert!(r.partial);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_sg_rrf() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64);\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [{\"id\": \"a\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.9}], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1, \"processingTimeMs\": 5}));\n        let s = crate::merger::RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n        let r = scatter_gather_search(plan, &c, make_req(), &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &s).await.unwrap();\n        assert!(!r.degraded);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_sg_degraded() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64);\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [{\"id\": \"a\"}], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1, \"processingTimeMs\": 5}));\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n        let s = crate::merger::RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n        assert!(scatter_gather_search(plan, &c, make_req(), &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &s).await.unwrap().degraded);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_query_terms() {\n        assert_eq!(extract_query_terms(&Some(\"hello world hello\".into())), vec![\"hello\", \"world\"]);\n        assert!(extract_query_terms(&None).is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_global_idf_aggregation() {\n        let resp = vec![\n            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 50.0, term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"a\".into(), TermStats { df: 5000 })]) },\n            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 60.0, term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"a\".into(), TermStats { df: 4500 })]) },\n        ];\n        let g = GlobalIdf::from_preflight_responses(&resp);\n        assert_eq!(g.total_docs, 100000);\n        assert!((g.avg_doc_length - 55.0).abs() < 0.001);\n        assert_eq!(g.terms.get(\"a\").unwrap().df, 9500);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_global_idf_empty() {\n        let g = GlobalIdf::from_preflight_responses(&[]);\n        assert_eq!(g.total_docs, 0);\n        assert!(g.terms.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_execute_preflight() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64);\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 30000, avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 3000 })]),\n        });\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 30000, avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 2500 })]),\n        });\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 40000, avg_doc_length: 52.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"search\".into(), TermStats { df: 4000 })]),\n        });\n        let req = PreflightRequest { index_uid: \"test\".into(), terms: vec![\"search\".into()], filter: None };\n        let g = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(g.total_docs, 100000);\n        assert_eq!(g.terms.get(\"search\").unwrap().df, 9500);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_query_then_fetch() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64);\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [{\"id\": \"a\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.9}], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1, \"processingTimeMs\": 5}));\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 500 })]),\n        });\n        let s = crate::merger::RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n        let r = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(plan, &c, make_req(), &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &s).await.unwrap();\n        assert!(!r.degraded);\n        assert!(!r.hits.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    /// Integration test for dfs_query_then_fetch with severely skewed shard distribution.\n    ///\n    /// This test simulates the scenario described in miroir-yio:\n    /// - Shard 0: Normal (1,000 docs, term df = 100)\n    /// - Shard 1: 100x normal (100,000 docs, term df = 10,000)\n    /// - Shard 2: Near-empty (10 docs, term df = 1)\n    ///\n    /// Without global IDF preflight, each shard would compute different local IDF values:\n    /// - Shard 0: IDF ≈ log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n    /// - Shard 1: IDF ≈ log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n    /// - Shard 2: IDF ≈ log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.8\n    ///\n    /// With global IDF preflight, all shards use the same IDF:\n    /// - Global: N = 101,010, df = 10,101\n    /// - IDF ≈ log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n    ///\n    /// This ensures scores are comparable across shards, enabling correct score-based merge.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_skewed_shards_global_idf_aggregation() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1);\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3);\n\n        // Simulate severely skewed shard distribution\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // Shard 0: Normal distribution\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 1000,\n            avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 100 }),\n                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 80 }),\n            ]),\n        });\n\n        // Shard 1: 100x normal (severely skewed)\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 100_000,\n            avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 10_000 }),\n                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 8_000 }),\n            ]),\n        });\n\n        // Shard 2: Near-empty\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 10,\n            avg_doc_length: 45.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n            ]),\n        });\n\n        // Execute preflight to get global IDF\n        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            terms: vec![\"machine\".into(), \"learning\".into()],\n            filter: None,\n        };\n\n        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n\n        // Verify global aggregation\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 101_010);\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().df, 10_101);\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().df, 8_081);\n\n        // Verify global IDF is the same for all shards\n        // Expected IDF for \"machine\": log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1\n        let expected_idf_machine: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 10101.0 + 0.5) / (10101.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        let actual_idf_machine = global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().idf;\n        assert!((actual_idf_machine - expected_idf_machine).abs() < 0.001);\n\n        // Expected IDF for \"learning\": log((101010 - 8081 + 0.5) / (8081 + 0.5)) + 1\n        let expected_idf_learning: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 8081.0 + 0.5) / (8081.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        let actual_idf_learning = global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().idf;\n        assert!((actual_idf_learning - expected_idf_learning).abs() < 0.001);\n\n        // Verify that without global IDF, local IDF values would differ significantly\n        // Shard 0 local IDF for \"machine\": log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.19\n        // Shard 1 local IDF for \"machine\": log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.20\n        // Shard 2 local IDF for \"machine\": log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.85\n        let local_idf_shard_0: f64 = ((1000.0_f64 - 100.0 + 0.5) / (100.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        let local_idf_shard_2: f64 = ((10.0_f64 - 1.0 + 0.5) / (1.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n        assert!((local_idf_shard_2 - local_idf_shard_0).abs() > 0.2, \"Local IDF values should differ significantly\");\n        assert!((local_idf_shard_2 - actual_idf_machine).abs() > 0.3, \"Global IDF should be closer to large-shard local IDF\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test that DFS preflight handles empty query terms gracefully.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_empty_query_terms() {\n        let topo = make_test_topology();\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64);\n        let c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            terms: vec![],\n            filter: None,\n        };\n\n        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 0);\n        assert!(global_idf.terms.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    /// Test that DFS preflight handles partial failures gracefully.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_dfs_partial_failure() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1);\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3);\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // Node 0 returns valid data\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 50000,\n            avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 5000 })]),\n        });\n\n        // Node 1 returns valid data\n        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 30000,\n            avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 3000 })]),\n        });\n\n        // Node 2 fails\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n\n        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            terms: vec![\"test\".into()],\n            filter: None,\n        };\n\n        // Should aggregate from successful nodes only\n        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 80000);\n        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"test\").unwrap().df, 8000);\n    }\n\n    /// Test that to_node_body correctly injects showRankingScore: true and sets limit to offset + limit.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_to_node_body_injects_show_ranking_score() {\n        let req = SearchRequest {\n            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n            query: Some(\"rust programming\".into()),\n            offset: 10,\n            limit: 20,\n            filter: Some(serde_json::json!(\"status = published\")),\n            facets: Some(vec![\"category\".into(), \"tags\".into()]),\n            ranking_score: false, // Client didn't request scores\n            body: serde_json::json!({\"custom\": \"field\"}),\n            global_idf: None,\n        };\n\n        let body = req.to_node_body();\n\n        // showRankingScore must be true unconditionally\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"showRankingScore\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(true)));\n\n        // limit must be offset + limit (coordinator pagination)\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"limit\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(30)));\n\n        // offset must be 0 (coordinator handles offset)\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"offset\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(0)));\n\n        // query must be set\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"q\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(\"rust programming\")));\n\n        // filter must be set\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"filter\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(\"status = published\")));\n\n        // facets must be set\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"facets\"), Some(&serde_json::json!([\"category\", \"tags\"])));\n\n        // custom body field must be preserved\n        assert_eq!(body.get(\"custom\"), Some(&serde_json::json!(\"field\")));\n    }\n\n    /// Test that to_node_body works with minimal request.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_to_node_body_minimal_request() {\n        let req = SearchRequest }\n\n    /// Test group fallback when primary group has failed nodes.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_group_fallback_on_partial_failure() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 2, 2);\n        // Group 0: 2 nodes\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), \"http://g0-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-1\".into()), \"http://g0-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n        // Group 1: 2 nodes (healthy fallback targets)\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), \"http://g1-0:7700\".into(), 1));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), \"http://g1-1:7700\".into(), 1));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 16); // query_seq=0 → group 0\n        assert_eq!(plan.chosen_group, 0);\n\n        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n        // Set up responses: all shards on group 1 nodes return valid data\n        let response_1 = serde_json::json!({\n            \"hits\": [{\"id\": \"doc1\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.9}],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n        });\n        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), response_1.clone());\n        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), response_1);\n\n        // All nodes in group 0 fail\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n        c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-0\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n\n        let req = make_req();\n\n        // With fallback policy, shards should succeed via group 1\n        let result = execute_scatter(plan, &c, req, &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback).await.unwrap();\n\n        // Should have succeeded via fallback (group 1)\n        assert!(!result.partial, \"Fallback should have succeeded\");\n        assert!(result.failed_shards.is_empty(), \"No shards should have failed after fallback\");\n        assert!(!result.shard_pages.is_empty(), \"Should have shard pages from fallback\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test group fallback when both groups are down.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_group_fallback_all_groups_down() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 2, 2);\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), \"http://g0-0:7700\".into(), \"http://g1-0:7700\".into(), 1));\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), \"http://g1-1:7700\".into(), 1));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 16);\n c.errors.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), NodeError::Timeout);\n\n        let req = make_req();\n\n        // With fallback policy, should still fail when all groups are down\n        let test_partial_policy_no_fallback() {\n        let mut topo = Topology::new(16, 2, 2);\n        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g0-0\".into()), \"http://g0-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-g1-1\".into()), \"http://g1-1:7700\".into(), 1));\n\n        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 16);\n        let mut c = {\n86\t        // Check cache first\n87\t        {\n88\t            let cache = self.version_cache.read().await;\n89\t            let last_update = self.last_cache_update.read().await;\n90\t Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(LocalAdminRateLimiterInner::default())),\n158\t        }\n159\t    }\n160\t\n161\t    /// Check rate limit and exponential backoff.\n162\t    /// Returns (allowed, wait_seconds).\n163\t    pub fn check(\n164\t        &self,\n165\t        ip: &str,\n166\t        limit: u64,\n167\t        window_ms: u64,\n168\t        failed_threshold: u32,\n169\t        backoff_start_minutes: u64,\n170\t        backoff_max_hours: u64,\n171\t    ) -> (bool, Option<u64>) {\n172\t        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n173\t        let now = now_ms();\n174\t        let state = inner.state.entry(ip.to_string()).or_default();\n175\t\n176\t        // Check if we're in backoff mode\n177\t        if let Some(backoff_until) = state.backoff_until_ms {\n178\t            if backoff_until > now {\n179\t                let wait_seconds = ((backoff_until - now) / 1000) as u64;\n180\t                return (false, Some(wait_seconds));\n181\t            }\n182\t            // Backoff expired, clear it\n183\t            state.backoff_until_ms = None;\n184\t        }\n185\t\n186\t        // Clean old timestamps outside the window\n187\t        state.request_timestamps_ms.retain(|&ts| now - ts < window_ms as i64);\n188\t\n189\t        // Check if limit exceeded\n190\t        if state.request_timestamps_ms.len() >= limit as usize {\n191\t            // Enter backoff mode after threshold consecutive failures\n192\t            let failed = state.failed_count + 1;\n193\t            state.failed_count = failed;\n194\t\n195\t            if failed >= failed_threshold {\n196\t                let backoff_minutes = backoff_start_minutes * (1u64 << ((failed - failed_threshold) as u64).min(7)); // Cap at 2^7 = 128x\n197\t                let backoff_seconds = (backoff_minutes * 60).min(backoff_max_hours * 3600);\n198\t                state.backoff_until_ms = Some(now + (backoff_seconds as i64 * 1000));\n199\t                return (false, Some(backoff_seconds));\n200\t            }\n201\t\n202\t            return (false, None);\n203\t        }\n204\t\n205\t        // Record this request\n206\t        state.request_timestamps_ms.push(now);\n207\t        (true, None)\n208\t    }\n209\t\n210\t    /// Reset rate limit and backoff state on successful login.\n211\t    pub fn reset(&self, ip: &str) {\n212\t        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n213\t        inner.state.remove(ip);\n214\t    }\n215\t\n216\t    /// Record a failed login attempt (for backoff calculation).\n217\t    pub fn record_failure(&self, ip: &str, failed_threshold: u32, backoff_start_minutes: u64, backoff_max_hours: u64) -> Option<u64> {\n218\t        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n219\t        let now = now_ms();\n220\t        let state = inner.state.entry(ip.to_string()).or_default();\n221\t\n222\t        state.failed_count += 1;\n223\t\n224\t        if state.failed_count >= failed_threshold {\n225\t            let backoff_minutes = backoff_start_minutes * (1u64 << ((state.failed_count - failed_threshold) as u64).min(7));\n226\t            let backoff_seconds = (backoff_minutes * 60).min(backoff_max_hours * 3600);\n227\t            state.backoff_until_ms = Some(now + (backoff_seconds as i64 * 1000));\n228\t            return Some(backoff_seconds);\n229\t        }\n230\t\n231\t        None\n232\t    }\n233\t}\n234\t\n235\timpl Default for LocalAdminRateLimiter {\n236\t    fn default() -> Self {\n237\t        Self::new()\n238\t    }\n239\t}\n240\t\n241\t/// In-memory rate limiter for search UI (local backend only).\n242\t/// Thread-safe using Arc<Mutex<...>>.\n243\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n244\tpub struct LocalSearchUiRateLimiter {\n245\t    inner: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<LocalSearchUiRateLimiterInner>>,\n246\t}\n247\t\n248\t#[derive(Debug, Default)]\n249\tstruct LocalSearchUiRateLimiterInner {\n250\t    /// Map of IP -> request_timestamps_ms\n251\t    state: HashMap<String, Vec<i64>>,\n252\t}\n253\t\n254\timpl LocalSearchUiRateLimiter {\n255\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n256\t        Self {\n257\t            inner: Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(LocalSearchUiRateLimiterInner::default())),\n258\t        }\n259\t    }\n260\t\n261\t    /// Check rate limit for search UI.\n262\t    /// Returns (allowed, wait_seconds).\n263\t    pub fn check(\n264\t        &self,\n265\t        ip: &str,\n266\t        limit: u64,\n267\t        window_ms: u64,\n268\t    ) -> (bool, Option<u64>) {\n269\t        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n270\t        let now = now_ms();\n271\t        let timestamps = inner.state.entry(ip.to_string()).or_default();\n272\t\n273\t        // Clean old timestamps outside the window\n274\t        timestamps.retain(|&ts| now - ts < window_ms as i64);\n275\t\n276\t        // Check if limit exceeded\n277\t        if timestamps.len() >= limit as usize {\n278\t            return (false, None);\n279\t        }\n280\t\n281\t        // Record this request\n282\t        timestamps.push(now);\n283\t        (true, None)\n284\t    }\n285\t}\n286\t\n287\timpl Default for LocalSearchUiRateLimiter {\n288\t    fn default() -> Self {\n289\t        Self::new()\n290\t    }\n291\t}\n292\t\n293\t/// Get current time in milliseconds since Unix epoch.\n294\tfn now_ms() -> i64 {\n295\t    std::time::SystemTime::now()\n296\t        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n297\t        .unwrap_or_default()\n298\t        .as_millis() as i64\n299\t}\n300\t\n301\t/// Shared application state for admin endpoints.\n302\t#[derive(Clone)]\n303\tpub struct AppState {\n304\t    pub config: Arc<MiroirConfig>,\n305\t    pub topology:    pub local_search_ui_rate_limiter: LocalSearchUiRateLimiter,\n315\t    pub rebalancer: Option<Arc<Rebalancer>>,\n316\t    pub migration_coordinator:   pub fn with_redis(\n329\t        config: MiroirConfig,\n330\t        metrics: super::super::middleware::Metrics,\n331\t        redis_store: Option<RedisTaskStore>,\n332\t        pod_id: String,\n333\t        seal_key: SealKey,\n334\t    ) -> Self {\n335\t        // Build initial topology from config\n336\t        let mut topology = Topology::new(\n337\t            config.shards,\n338\t            config.replica_groups,\n339\t            config.replication_factor as usize,\n340\t        );\n341\t\n342\t        for node_config in &config.nodes {\n343\t );\n348\t            // Start nodes in Joining state - health checker will promote to Active\n349\t            topology.add_node(node);\n350\t        }\n351\t\n352\t        let version_state = = redis_store.as_ref().unwrap().clone();\n361\t                store.migrate().expect(\"Redis migration failed\");\n362\t                TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(Arc::new(store))\n363\t            }\n364\t            \"sqlite\" if !config.task_store.path.is_empty() => {\n365\t                TaskRegistryImpl::sqlite(&config.task_store.path)\n366\t                    .expect(\"Failed to open SQLite task store\")\n367\t            }\n368\t            _ => TaskRegistryImpl::in_memory(),\n369\t        };\n370\t\n371\t        let topology_arc = Arc::new(RwLock::new(topology));\n372\t\n373\t        // Initialize rebalancer and migration coordinator\n374\t        let rebalancer_config = RebalancerConfig {\n375\t            max_concurrent_migrations: config.rebalancer.max_concurrent_migrations,\n376\t            migration_timeout_s: config.rebalancer.migration_timeout_s,\n377\t            auto_rebalance_on_recovery: config.anti_entropy.enabled,\n386\t        };\n387\t\n388\t        let migration_coordinator = Arc::new(RwLock::new(\n389\t            MigrationCoordinator::new(migration_config.clone())\n390\t        ));\n391\t\n392\t        // Create migration executor for actual HTTP document migration\n393\t        use miroir_core::rebalancer::HttpMigrationExecutor;\n394\t        let migration_executor = ).with_migration_executor(migration_executor));\n404\t\n405\t        Self {\n406\t            config: Arc::new(config),\n407\t            topology: topology_arc,\n408\t            ready: pod_id,\n414\t            seal_key,\n415\t            local_rate_limiter: LocalAdminRateLimiter::new(),\n416\t            local_search_ui_rate_limiter: LocalSearchUiRateLimiter::new(),\n417\t            rebalancer: Some(rebalancer),\n418\t            migration_coordinator: Some(migration_coordinator),\n419\t        }\n420\t    }\n421\t\n422\t    /// Mark the service as ready (all nodes reachable).\n423\t    pub async fn mark_ready(&self) {\n424\t        *self.ready.write().await = true;\n425\t        info!(\"Service marked as ready\");\n426\t    }\n427\t\n428\t    /// Check if a covering quorum is reachable.\n429\t    pub async fn check_covering_quorum(&self) -> bool {\n430\t        let topo = self.topology.read().await;\n431\t        let node_map = topo.node_map();\n432\t\n433\t        // For each replica group, check if we have enough healthy nodes\n434\t        for group in topo.groups() {\n435\t            let healthy = group.healthy_nodes(&node_map);\n436\t            let required = (topo.rf() + 1) / 2; // Simple majority for quorum\n437\t            if healthy.len() < required {\n438\t                return false;\n439\t            }\n440\t        }\n441\t\n442\t        true\n443\t    }\n444\t}\n445\t\n446\t/// Response for GET /_miroir/topology (plan §10 JSON shape).\n447\t#[derive(Debug, Clone,    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n466\t    pub error: Option<String>,\n467\t}\n468\t\n469\t/// Response for GET /_miroir/shards.\n470\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n471\tpub struct ShardsResponse {\n472\t    pub shards: HashMap<String, Vec<String>>, // shard_id -> list of node IDs\n473\t}\n474\t\n475\t/// GET /_miroir/topology — full cluster state per plan §10.\n476\tpub async fn get_topology<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Result<Json<TopologyResponse>, StatusCode>\n477\twhere\n478\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n479\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n480\t{\n481\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n482\t    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n483\t\n484\t    // Count degraded nodes\n485\t    let degraded_count = topo.nodes().filter(|n| !n.is_healthy()).count() as u32;\n486\t\n487\t    // Check rebalance status\n488\t    let rebalance_in_progress = if let Some(ref rebalancer) = state.rebalancer {\n489\t        let status = rebalancer.status().await;\n490\t        status.in_progress\n491\t    } else {\n492\t        false\n493\t    };\n494\t\n495\t    // Build node info list\n496\t    let nodes: Vec<NodeInfo> format!(\"{:?}\", n.status).to_lowercase(),\n502\t            shard_count: 0, // TODO: compute from routing table\n503\t            last_seen_ms: 0, // TODO: track last health check time\n504\t            error: None,     // TODO: populate from last health check error\n505\t        })\n506\t        .collect();\n507\t\n508\t    // Check if fully covered\n509\t    let fully_covered = degraded_count == 0;\n510\t\n511\t    let response = TopologyResponse {\n512\t        shards: topo.shards,\n513\t        replication_factor: topo.rf() as u32,\n514\t        nodes,\n515\t        degraded_node_count: table.\n524\tpub async fn get_shards<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Result<Json<ShardsResponse>, StatusCode>\n525\twhere\n526\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n527\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n528\t{\n529\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n530\t    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n531\t    let mut shards = HashMap::new();\n532\t\n533\t    // Build shard -> node mapping using rendezvous hash\n534\t    for shard_id in 0..topo.shards {\n535\t        let mut node_ids = Vec::new();\n536\t\n537\t        // Collect nodes from all replica groups for this shard\n538\t        for group in topo.groups() {\n539\t            let assigned = router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topo.rf());\n540\t            for node_id in assigned {\n541\t                node_ids.push(node_id.as_str().to_string());\n542\t            }\n543\t        }\n544\t\n545\t        shards.insert(shard_id.to_string(), node_ids);\n546\t    }\n547\t\n548\t    Ok(Json(ShardsResponse { shards }))\n549\t}\n550\t\n551\t/// GET /_miroir/ready — readiness probe (503 during startup, 200 once ready).\n552\tpub async fn get_ready<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Result<&'static str, StatusCode>\n553\twhere\n554\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n555\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n556\t{\n557\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n558\t    let ready = *state.ready.read().await;\n559\t\n560\t    if ready {\n561\t        Ok(\"\")\n562\t    } else {\n563\t        // Not yet marked ready - check if covering quorum exists\n564\t        let has_quorum = state.check_covering_quorum().await;\n565\t        if has_quorum {\n566\t            // Auto-mark ready on first successful quorum check\n567\t            state.mark_ready().await;\n568\t            Ok(\"\")\n569\t        } else {\n570\t            Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)\n571\t        }\n572\t    }\n573\t}\n574\t\n575\t/// GET /_miroir/metrics — admin-key-gated Prometheus metrics.\n576\tpub async fn get_metrics<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Response\n577\twhere\n578\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n579\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n580\t{\n581\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n582\t    match state.metrics.encode_metrics() {\n583\t        Ok(metrics) => metrics.into_response(),\n584\t        Err(e) => {\n585\t            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"failed to encode metrics\");\n586\t            StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response()\n587\t        }\n588\t    }\n589\t}\n590\t\n591\t/// POST /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/rotate-scoped-key — manual rotation trigger.\n592\t///\n593\t/// Admin-gated endpoint that initiates a scoped key rotation for the given index.\n594\t/// Set `force: true` in the request body to bypass the timing gate.\n595\tpub async fn rotate_scoped_key_handler<S>(\n596\t    State(state): State<S>,\n597\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n598\t    Json(body): Json<RotateScopedKeyRequest>,\n599\t) -> Result<Json<RotateScopedKeyResponse>, (StatusCode, String)>\n600\twhere\n601\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n602\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n603\t{\n604\t    let app_state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n605\t\n606\t    let redis = app_state.redis_store.clone().ok_or_else(|| {\n607\t        (\n608\t            StatusCode::PRECONDITION_FAILED,\n609\t            \"scoped key rotation requires Redis task store\".into(),\n610\t        )\n611\t    })?;\n612\t\n613\t    if !app_state.config.search_ui.enabled {\n614\t        return Err((\n615\t            StatusCode::PRECONDITION_FAILED,\n616\t            \"search_ui is not enabled\".into(),\n617\t        ));\n618\t    }\n619\t\n620\t    let rotation_state = ScopedKeyRotationState {\n621\t        config: app_state.config.clone(),\n622\t        redis,\n623\t        pod_id: app_state.pod_id.clone(),\n624\t    };\n625\t\n626\t    info!(\n627\t        index = %index,\n628\t        force = body.force,\n629\t        pod_id = %app_state.pod_id,\n630\t        \"manual scoped key rotation triggered\"\n631\t    );\n632\t\n633\t    match scoped_key_rotation::check_and_rotate(&rotation_state, &index, body.force).await {\n634\t        Ok(response) => Ok(Json(response)),\n635\t        Err(e) => {\n636\t            error!(index = %index, error = %e, \"manual scoped key rotation failed\");\n637\t            Err((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e))\n638\t        }\n639\t    }\n640\t}\n641\t\n642\t/// Parse a rate limit string like \"10/minute\" into (limit, window_seconds).\n643\tpub fn parse_rate_limit(s: &str) -> Result<(u64, u64), String> {\n644\t    let parts: Vec<&str> = s.split('/').collect();\n645\t    if parts.len() != 2 {\n646\t        return Err(format!(\"invalid rate limit format: '{}', expected 'N/UNIT'\", s));\n647\t    }\n648\t    let limit: u64 = parts[0].parse()\n649\t        .map_err(|_| format!(\"invalid limit number: '{}'\", parts[0]))?;\n650\t    let window_seconds = match parts[1] {\n651\t        \"second\" | \"s\" => 1,\n652\t        \"minute\" | \"m\" => 60,\n653\t        \"hour\" | \"h\" => 3600,\n654\t        \"day\" | \"d\" => 86400,\n655\t        unit => return Err(format!(\"invalid time unit: '{}', expected second/minute/hour/day\", unit)),\n656\t    };\n657\t    Ok((limit, window_seconds))\n658\t}\n659\t\n660\t/// Generate a random session ID.\n661\tfn generate_session_id() -> String {\n662\t    let mut bytes = [0u8; 24];\n663\t    rand::rngs::OsRng.fill_bytes(&mut bytes);\n664\t    hex::encode(&bytes)\n665\t}\n666\t\n667\t/// POST /_miroir/admin/login — admin login with rate limiting and exponential backoff.\n668\t///\n669\t/// Request body:\n670\t/// ```json\n671\t/// { \"admin_key\": \"...\" }\n672\t/// ```\n673\t///\n674\t/// On success, sets a `miroir_admin_session` cookie and returns:\n675\t/// ```json\n676\t/// { \"success\": true }\n677\t/// ```\n678\t///\n679\t/// Rate limiting (per source IP):\n680\t/// - 10 requests per minute (configurable via `admin_ui.rate_limit.per_ip`)\n681\t/// - After 5 consecutive failed attempts, exponential backoff applies:\n682\t///   - 10m, 20m, 40m, ... up to 24h cap\n683\t///\n684\t/// Successful login resets both the rate limit counter and backoff state.\n685\tpub async fn admin_login<S>(\n686\t    State(state): State<S>,\n687\t    headers: HeaderMap,\n688\t    Json(body): Json<AdminLoginRequest>,\n689\t) -> Response\n690\twhere\n691\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n692\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n693\t{\n694\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n695\t\n696\t    // Extract source IP from X-Forwarded-For or X-Real-IP (trust proxy)\n697\t    let source_ip = headers\n698\t        .get(\"x-forwarded-for\")\n699\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n700\t        .and_then(|s| s.split(',').next())\n701\t        .or_else(|| headers.get(\"x-real-ip\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()))\n702\t        .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n703\t        .trim()\n704\t        .to_string();\n705\t\n706\t    // Parse rate limit config\n707\t    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n708\t        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n709\t        Err(e) => {\n710\t            error!(error = %e, \"invalid admin_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config\");\n711\t            return (\n712\t                StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n713\t                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n714\t                    success: false,\n715\t                    message: Some(\"Rate limit configuration error\".into()),\n716\t                }),\n717\t            ).into_response();\n718\t        }\n719\t    };\n720\t\n721\t    // Check rate limit and backoff\n722\t    let backend = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n723\t    if backend == \"redis\" {\n724\t        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n725\t            match redis.check_rate_limit_admin_login(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n726\t                Ok((allowed, wait_seconds)) => {\n727\t                    if !allowed {\n728\t                        if let Some(ws) = wait_seconds {\n729\t                            warn!(\n730\t                                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n731\t                                wait_seconds = ws,\n732\t                                \"admin login rate limited (backoff)\"\n733\t                            );\n734\t                            return (\n735\t                                StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,\n736\t                                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n737\t                                    success: false,\n738\t                                    message: Some(format!(\n739\t                                        \"Too many failed login attempts. Try again in {} seconds.\",\n740\t                                        ws\n741\t                                    )),\n742\t                                }),\n743\t                            ).into_response();\n744\t                        } else {\n745\t                            return (\n746\t                                StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,\n747\t                                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n748\t                                    success: false,\n749\t                                    message: Some(\"Too many login attempts. Please try again later.\".into()),\n750\t                                }),\n751\t                            ).into_response();\n752\t                        }\n753\t                    }\n754\t                    // Allowed, proceed\n755\t                }\n756\t                Err(e) => {\n757\t                    error!(error = %e, \"failed to check admin login rate limit\");\n758\t                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n759\t                }\n760\t            }\n761\t        }\n762\t    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n763\t        // Local backend rate limiting\n764\t        let (allowed, wait_seconds) = state.local_rate_limiter.check(\n765\t            &source_ip,\n766\t            limit,\n767\t            window_seconds * 1000,\n768\t            state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.failed_attempt_threshold,\n769\t            state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_start_minutes,\n770\t            state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_max_hours * 60,\n771\t        );\n772\t        if !allowed {\n773\t            warn!(\n774\t                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n775\t                wait_seconds = ?wait_seconds,\n776\t                \"admin login rate limited (local backend)\"\n777\t            );\n778\t            return (\n779\t                StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,\n780\t                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n781\t                    success: false,\n782\t                    message: if let Some(ws) = wait_seconds {\n783\t                        Some(format!(\n784\t                            \"Too many failed login attempts. Try again in {} seconds.\",\n785\t                            ws\n786\t                        ))\n787\t                    } else {\n788\t                        Some(\"Too many login attempts. Please try again later.\".into())\n789\t                    },\n790\t                }),\n791\t            ).into_response();\n792\t        }\n793\t    }\n794\t\n795\t    // Verify admin_key (constant-time comparison to prevent timing side-channels)\n796\t    use subtle::ConstantTimeEq as _;\n797\t    if body.admin_key.as_bytes().ct_eq(state.config.admin.api_key.as_bytes()).into() {\n798\t        // Successful login - reset rate limit counters\n799\t        if backend == \"redis\" {\n800\t            if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n801\t                if let Err(e) = redis.reset_rate_limit_admin_login(&source_ip) {\n802\t                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to reset admin login rate limit\");\n803\t                }\n804\t            }\n805\t        } else if backend == \"local\" {\n806\t            state.local_rate_limiter.reset(&source_ip);\n807\t        }\n808\t\n809\t        // Generate session ID and seal it\n810\t        let session_id = generate_session_id();\n811\t        let sealed = match seal_session(&session_id, &state.seal_key) {\n812\t            Ok(sealed) => sealed,\n813\t            Err(e) => {\n814\t                error!(error = %e, \"failed to seal admin session\");\n815\t                return (\n816\t                    StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n817\t                    Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n818\t                        success: false,\n819\t                        message: Some(\"Failed to create session\".into()),\n820\t                    }),\n821\t                ).into_response();\n822\t            }\n823\t        };\n824\t\n825\t        info!(\n826\t            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n827\t            session_prefix = &session_id[..8],\n828\t            \"admin login successful\"\n829\t        );\n830\t\n831\t        // Set cookie and return success\n832\t        (\n833\t            StatusCode::OK,\n834\t            [\n835\t                (\"Set-Cookie\", format!(\"{}={}; HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Strict; Path=/; Max-Age={}\",\n836\t                    COOKIE_NAME, sealed, state.config.admin_ui.session_ttl_s)),\n837\t            ],\n838\t            Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n839\t                success: true,\n840\t                message: None,\n841\t            }),\n842\t        ).into_response()\n843\t    } else {\n844\t        // Wrong admin_key - record failure for backoff tracking\n845\t        warn!(\n846\t            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n847\t            \"admin login failed: invalid admin_key\"\n848\t        );\n849\t\n850\t        if backend == \"redis\" {\n851\t            if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n852\t                let backoff_start_minutes = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_start_minutes;\n853\t                let backoff_max_hours = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_max_hours;\n854\t                let failed_threshold = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.failed_attempt_threshold;\n855\t\n856\t                if let Err(e) = redis.record_failure_admin_login(\n857\t                    &source_ip,\n858\t                    failed_threshold,\n859\t                    backoff_start_minutes,\n860\t                    backoff_max_hours,\n861\t                ) {\n862\t                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to record admin login failure\");\n863\t                }\n864\t            }\n865\t        } else if backend == \"local\" {\n866\t            let backoff_start_minutes = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_start_minutes;\n867\t            let backoff_max_hours = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_max_hours;\n868\t            let failed_threshold = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.failed_attempt_threshold;\n869\t\n870\t            state.local_rate_limiter.record_failure(\n871\t                &source_ip,\n872\t                failed_threshold,\n873\t                backoff_start_minutes,\n874\t                backoff_max_hours * 60,\n875\t            );\n876\t        }\n877\t\n878\t        (\n879\t            StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED,\n880\t            Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n881\t                success: false,\n882\t                message: Some(\"Invalid admin key\".into()),\n883\t            }),\n884\t        ).into_response()\n885\t    }\n886\t}\n887\t\n888\t//
